source.subject: Approval needed: Tiered expansion discount for Zenwell Health — CFO sign-off
source.thread:
From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, 9 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Approval needed: Tiered expansion discount for Zenwell Health — CFO sign-off

Team,

Quick background: Zenwell (healthcare analytics) is moving from a pilot (hosted embeddings + chat) to a full expansion that includes Redwood Dedicated + Private for their regulated workloads. Procurement has indicated a tight procurement window — they want a fully executed SOW by 4/15 to hit their Q2 deployment target.

Key commercial summary (attached: Zenwell_SOW_v3.pdf, zenwell_pricing_breakdown.xlsx):
- Term: 36 months; committed spend (total EV): $1.8M
- Year 1 ARR-equivalent: ~$700k (first 12 months skewed toward onboarding & Dedicated consumption)
- Requested pricing: a tiered discount schedule to hit an average ~42% off list across line items (hosted tokens, dedicated device hours, premium observability). Zenwell is also requesting we waive the standard onboarding fee and include the observability add-on free for the first 6 months.
- Current AE delegation: max approval 25% off list for this segment. This request exceeds delegation and needs CFO sign-off for the deviation.

Why I recommend approving (high level):
- Strategic reference value: Zenwell is a nationally recognized health analytics provider; successful on-prem + dedicated deployment in regulated healthcare would be a strong reference for other prospects in our healthcare GTM (estimated pipeline motion: ~$3.5M ARR over 24–36 months).
- Revenue lock-in: Bundling Dedicated + Private increases stickiness and reduces near-term churn risk.
- Operational: Zenwell will commit to a usage minimum (baseline device-hours) and provide a short case-study + reference callback as part of the agreement.

Request: Marissa — can you review and sign the attached internal approval memo if you’re comfortable with the proposed exception? We need a decision by EOD 4/12 to keep procurement on schedule. If you prefer, I can amend the proposal to stage the discount across years (Year 1 larger, then step-down), and add a clawback if they miss usage commitments.

Notes: I included Rafael to flag GL/credit implications and Dylan for SRE/ops to ensure the Dedicated capacity planning is acceptable. Marcus — please advise if you want different commercial guardrails.

Thanks,
Ava
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Apr 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Approval needed: Tiered expansion discount for Zenwell Health — CFO sign-off

Ava — thanks for the summary. I’m supportive of giving Zenwell preferential pricing but want tighter guardrails before endorsing a 42% average discount. Proposed adjustments:

- Staged discount schedule we can present to Zenwell: Year 1 = 38% off list, Year 2 = 25% off list, Year 3 = 15% off list. This keeps headline pricing attractive while protecting long-term unit economics.
- Mandatory minimums: require a baseline committed device-hour minimum (50% of estimated Year1 usage) and a minimum monthly hosted token volume with a clawback for sustained shortfall after month 6.
- Remove the permanent onboarding waiver; convert to a one-time 50% credit in month 1 tied to early signature.
- Require a case study + product reference to be delivered within 9 months of go-live; if not delivered, we recoup 25% of the initial onboarding credit.

If those are acceptable, I would ask Marissa to sign off on up to 38% (Yr1) with the conditions above. Rafael — can you confirm this structure keeps FY26 economics intact (I'll need the modeled margin impact).

—Marcus
VP Sales, Redwood Inference

---

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 11 Apr 2026 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Internal memo for CFO approval — Zenwell tiered discount (D-8421-ZEN)

Marissa — forwarding a concise internal memo I prepared that outlines the ask and conditions Marcus suggested. Doc includes: deal economics, staged discount schedule, required legal clauses (clawback + usage SLAs), and finance GL entries.

Internal approval memo (for signature):
- Customer: Zenwell Health (deal D-8421-ZEN)
- Ask: Approve a customer-specific pricing exception to enable expansion into Dedicated + Private; authorization to sign SOW with the following commercial terms: 36-month term, staged discount (Yr1 38% / Yr2 25% / Yr3 15%), onboarding credit (50% one-time), minimum committed device-hours and hosted token volumes, and a product reference requirement.
- Rationale: Strategic healthcare reference, revenue lock-in via Dedicated/Private, high expansion potential (estimated $3.5M ARR pipeline).
- Financial impact: EV $1.8M; modelled FY26 margin hit ~7% (see attached spreadsheet). Rafael, please confirm classification of the onboarding credit (credit memo vs. cost of sale).
- Approvals requested: CFO signoff for pricing deviation beyond AE delegation (>25%); confirmation of payment terms (Net30 suggested) and billing schedule.

Attachments: Zenwell_SOW_v3.pdf; zenwell_pricing_breakdown.xlsx

If you’re comfortable, please sign and return the internal memo as a signed_discount_memo.pdf; I’ll attach to DocuSign SOW and route to Zenwell procurement.

Thanks — Ava

---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2026 15:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Internal memo for CFO approval — Zenwell tiered discount (D-8421-ZEN)

Ava / team —

After reviewing the memo and Rafael’s preliminary economics, I will approve a conditional pricing exception with the following firm conditions:

1) Final Year 1 discount cap: 38% off list (as Marcus suggested). Year 2 = 25%, Year 3 = 15%.
2) Onboarding: convert the onboarding waiver to a 50% one-time credit; credit will be subject to a clawback of 25% if the customer fails to deliver the case study/reference within 9 months of go-live.
3) Minimums & clawback: include the committed device-hour minimum and hosted token minimum in Schedule A; add explicit clawback language for sustained shortfall after month 6.
4) Payment & collections: Net30. Because of the size, require a first-quarter payment holdback of 10% in escrow to be released after go-live acceptance criteria (Finance to set up; Rafael lead).
5) Legal: require DocuSign with the updated Schedule A and clawback clauses; Legal must sign off prior to execution.

Please proceed with updating the SOW and DocuSign packet. Rafael — once the final pricing file is attached, please post the approval memo to the deal record (HubSpot link above) and generate the necessary GL approval code (chargeback/credit memo code).

I’m attaching my signed approval memo (signed_discount_memo.pdf). Ava — move this to DocuSign and loop procurement.

Marissa Cole
CFO, Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.com

---

[Attachments referenced in thread: Zenwell_SOW_v3.pdf (application/pdf), zenwell_pricing_breakdown.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet), signed_discount_memo.pdf (application/pdf)]

title: Approval needed: Tiered expansion discount for Zenwell Health — CFO sign-off

source.subject: Network flow telemetry requirements for on-prem private deployment
messages:
From: Emma Sullivan <emma.sullivan@edgegrid.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: network-ops@edgegrid.com
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Flow telemetry and evidence for on-prem deployment

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for yesterday's call. As we start moving toward the private deployment PoC, Network Ops asked for a concrete list of flow telemetry and packet/connection metadata the Redwood private stack exposes from the tenant VPCs and on-prem fabric.

Specifically, we need to validate that: 
- flow logs include L4 tuple + process/instance id (where available)
- egress/ingress NAT translations are recorded
- latency histograms for per-flow forwarding decision time are available
- retention and deletion controls map to our compliance window (90 days)

I've attached our internal spec (edgegrid-flow-specs-v1.pdf) and copied the ops alias. Can you point to the Terraform/Helm modules we should review and what evidence you can provide from CI to satisfy our auditors?

Best,
Emma Sullivan
Head of Network Engineering
EdgeGrid Systems

---
Attachment: edgegrid-flow-specs-v1.pdf (application/pdf, 312 KB)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Emma Sullivan <emma.sullivan@edgegrid.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Flow telemetry and evidence for on-prem deployment

Thanks Emma — good, concrete asks. Looping in Markus (runtime networking) and Neha (compliance/iactest owner).

Quick summary of what we can provide out of the box for private (VPC/on-prem) deployments:

- Flow logs: per-tenant flow logs contain 5-tuple + flow duration + bytes/packets. For on-prem we rely on the dataplane agent to emit process and container metadata when the agent runs as privileged.
- NAT visibility: the egress proxy records pre/post NAT 5-tuples in an annotated log stream (we can toggle this in helm values).
- Latency histograms: runtime exposes per-hop forwarding time; we aggregate into 1ms buckets and push to Prometheus histograms.
- Retention controls: logs are routed to the customer sink (S3/MinIO) with lifecycle policies; our private helm chart includes lifecycle CRD mapping for retention TTLs.

What I'd like to do next: 
1) Share a short diff of the Terraform modules and the helm values patch we propose for the on-prem agent so your auditors can see exact changes.
2) Run a CI artifact (iactest) that produces a reproducible evidence bundle (flow samples + histogram exports + lifecycle policy snapshot).

I'll attach a patch with the proposed tf-module changes and a simple diagram. Markus/Neha — can you confirm the iactest checklist we should run (sampling rate, synthetic flows, retention validation)?

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Product Manager, Private Deployments
Redwood Inference

---
Attachments: tf-module-diff.patch (text/x-patch), flow-telemetry-diagram.png (image/png, 48 KB)

From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Emma Sullivan <emma.sullivan@edgegrid.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2026 15:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Flow telemetry and evidence for on-prem deployment

Emma/Ava — quick technical notes after reviewing the patch. I'm pasting the important parts inline for visibility.

1) Terraform: the proposed module adds an optional `flow_agent_enabled` flag and an `agent_privileged` boolean. We should avoid enabling `agent_privileged` by default; instead, we provide a documented manual step for customers who require process-level attribution (EdgeGrid falls into that category).

2) Helm values: `telemetry.sample_rate` defaults to 0.01; for audit evidence we should run at 1.0 (full capture) in the iactest job. We'll include an `iactest_values.yaml` that sets `telemetry.sample_rate: 1.0`, `log_format: annotated`, and `retention_policy_ttl_days: 90`.

3) CI evidence: I'll add a pipeline stage `iactest/flow-evidence` which will:
   - deploy the agent helm chart with iactest_values.yaml
   - generate synthetic flows (tcp/udp/http) across NAT and hairpin scenarios
   - collect sample logs and a prometheus snapshot (histograms)
   - create a signed evidence bundle (sha256) and upload to the Drive folder Ava linked

Neha — for the evidence bundle to be acceptable, please confirm if a signed SHA256 and the pipeline run logs are sufficient, or if we need a notarized timestamping step.

Markus
Senior Software Engineer, Runtime Networking
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment referenced: iactest_values.yaml (to be added to the repo)

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Emma Sullivan <emma.sullivan@edgegrid.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 9 Apr 2026 14:07:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Flow telemetry and evidence for on-prem deployment

Thanks — this looks good. From a compliance perspective: 

- Evidence bundle must include: the iactest pipeline run id, signed artifacts (sha256), the prometheus snapshot file, and a config snapshot (helm values + terraform module versions).
- Retention: customer-configured TTL of 90 days is fine; we will include a short runbook snippet in the evidence bundle that shows how to change/delete data and confirm deletion.
- Timestamping: a notarized timestamp would be nice-to-have but not mandatory for the initial audit. A signed pipeline artifact + CI logs that show the run timestamp is acceptable.

Action items (next steps):
- Markus: create the `iactest/flow-evidence` pipeline job and add `iactest_values.yaml` to the repo.
- Ava: we'll send EdgeGrid a one-off evidence bundle after running the job in the staging on-prem lab.
- Emma: confirm any additional artefacts your auditors want (e.g., passenger_process_id mapping).

Neha Kapoor
Security & Compliance, Redwood Inference

---
Quoted message included below:
> On Thu, Apr 8, 2026 at 11:03 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Thanks Emma — good, concrete asks. Looping in Markus (runtime networking) and Neha (compliance/iactest owner).
> > ...

From: Emma Sullivan <emma.sullivan@edgegrid.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 9 Apr 2026 15:01:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Flow telemetry and evidence for on-prem deployment

Perfect — this covers what we need for the SOC-analyst walkthrough next week. Two quick follow-ups: 

1) We'll provide a small set of hostnames and test VMs for the synthetic flow run; expect an invite from network-ops.
2) Can you include the `tf-module-diff.patch` in the PR so our procurement team can see the exact resource changes? They use that to validate policy scoping.

If you want, my team can run an initial smoke pass once you have the iactest job ready and share a short findings summary — that might speed up the auditor review.

Thanks again,
Emma
---
Inline forwarded: edgegrid-flow-specs-v1.pdf

source.subject: Follow-up: Redwood demo — scheduling & next steps
messages:
From: Samir Patel <samir@orchardbi.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Mar 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Interested in a quick product walkthrough

Hi Ava,

Signed up for a trial this morning and landed on the self-serve page — curious to see how Redwood handles our inference patterns (high throughput, short-context reranks). I grabbed an available 30m slot on your calendar for today at 10am PT.

If that slot doesn’t work, happy to move. Goal is a quick look at:
- typical latency/throughput for batch scoring
- deployment options (dedicated vs private)
- pricing guardrails for spikes

Thanks,
Samir
Product Lead, Orchard BI
(408) 555-0199

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@orchardbi.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Mar 2026 09:35:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Interested in a quick product walkthrough

Hi Samir — welcome, and thanks for signing up. I confirmed the 10:00am PT slot and sent a calendar invite (attached).

Agenda (30m):
- 10m: Orchard BI use case & current constraints
- 10m: Demo of Redwood Dedicated and quick perf estimates
- 10m: Next steps (trial runway, PoC guardrails)

Quick logistics:
- Join: https://meet.redwood.ai/ava-demo (passcode on invite)
- If anything changes, reply to this thread or reschedule via the calendar link.

Attaching a one-pager with how we measure latency/cost so you can skim ahead. Jordan (AE) is cc’d and will handle contract/pricing questions if we get into terms.

Looking forward to it — see you shortly.

Best,
Ava Chen
SDR, Redwood Inference
(415) 555-0101
(attachment: demo-invite.ics)
(attachment: redwood-onboarding-one-pager.pdf)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@orchardbi.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 31 Mar 2026 10:06:00 -07:00
Subject: Missed — quick check in

Samir — we missed you on the demo a few minutes ago. No problem if something came up. I can: 
- reschedule for another 30m this week; or
- send a 15m technical sync with Jordan for perf/pricing detail only.

Proposed times (PT):
- Wed 4/1 10:00am
- Thu 4/2 11:30am
- Fri 4/3 9:30am

Or paste times that work and I’ll lock it in. If you prefer self-serve, here’s my public booking link: https://cal.redwood.ai/j/ava-30min

If this is no longer a priority, quick reply with “not now” and I’ll close the loop.

Thanks — Ava

From: Samir Patel <samir@orchardbi.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2026 08:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Missed — quick check in

Hey Ava — sorry, got pulled into an all-hands. Can we do Thu 4/2 at 11:30am PT? Prefer to do the full 30 if Jordan can join. Also — could you share any short perf benchmarks for short-context reranking ahead of the call?

Thanks,
Samir

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@orchardbi.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2026 09:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Missed — quick check in

Hi Samir — Jordan here (AE). 11:30am PT on Thu 4/2 works — I’ll join and bring perf slides. 

Quick answers for the rerank case (high level):
- Typical p50 token latency (128-token KV cache path): ~12–18ms on a single A100 for batch=1; improves with batching.
- For throughput-heavy scoring, we recommend Dedicated + prefix caching; customers often move to quantized weights for cost savings.

I’ll include a 1-slide perf summary and an example config for batching so you have something to share with the infra team. See you Thursday.

— Jordan
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
(415) 555-0123

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@orchardbi.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Bcc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2026 10:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Quick follow — materials and link for today

Samir — thanks for confirming. I attached the invite for 11:30am PT (demo link + passcode) and Jordan’s one-slide perf summary is linked here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cPERF (view-only).

If anything changes last minute, reply to this thread and I’ll send an updated invite. Otherwise, we’ll use the agenda from the original note and keep the demo tightly scoped to your reranking needs.

See you at 11:30 — Ava
(attachment: demo-invite.ics)

From: Samir Patel <samir@orchardbi.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2026 11:55:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick follow — materials and link for today

Post-demo note — great overview, Jordan. A couple of follow-ups from our infra team:
- Could you share an example autoscaling policy for Dedicated capacity?
- Any guidance on retention settings for KV caches (we have bursty windows overnight)?

Also looping in our Eng manager (Anika Rao) — anika@orchardbi.com — to coordinate PoC timelines.

Thanks,
Samir

source.subject: AcmeCloud marketplace: partner-issued free credits activation request
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Cc: partnerships@redwood.ai
Date: Wed, 18 Mar 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Partner credit activation request — AcmeCloud marketplace

Hi team,

AcmeCloud (marketplace partner) has requested we activate partner-issued free credits for their PLG signups. Quick summary:
- Offer: $500 in Redwood credits (pro-rated to tokens) for new accounts sourcing from AcmeCloud marketplace (promo code ACME_PLG_2026)
- Volume estimate: ~200 activations in first 60 days
- Expiry: 6 months from issuance

They've attached the signed MOU (acme_mou_signed.pdf) and want us to enable self-serve issuance so new users redeem via the marketplace flow. Can we please confirm:
1) Compliance / legal sign-off on the MOU copy (Marissa)
2) Operational cap: max credits per month and overall cap to limit spend (Dylan / Ops)
3) Console/action plan for issuance: will this be a partner-issued code on the marketplace or do we need to manually create credit batches?

If approved, I'd like to enable this for the upcoming Acme promotional window starting 03/25. Let me know if you need more details; attaching the partner email and MOU.

Thanks,
Ava
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0142

Attachments: acme_mou_signed.pdf (signed MOU, 2 pages)
From: Samantha Li <samantha@acmecloud.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: partners@acmecloud.io, marketplace-ops@acmecloud.io
Date: Thu, 19 Mar 2026 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partner credit activation request — AcmeCloud marketplace

Thanks Ava — appreciate the quick turnaround.

Confirming AcmeCloud will surface the promo code ACME_PLG_2026 as part of our onboarding flow. We're attaching the marketplace activation CSV that contains the partner IDs we'll send to Redwood at redemption time. Please confirm the following on your end:
- Batch upload format accepted for credits (CSV columns)
- TTL / expiry rules once a user redeems via marketplace
- Any required webhook/endpoint for real-time redemption confirmations

We'd like to go live 03/25 and will pause on our side until you confirm issuance workflow.

Best,
Samantha Li
Partner Ops | AcmeCloud Integrations
samantha@acmecloud.io

--- Attachment: acme_activation_payload_template.csv (sample)---
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Cc: legal@redwood.ai
Date: Fri, 20 Mar 2026 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partner credit activation request — AcmeCloud marketplace

Ava / Dylan — reviewed the MOU and legal is OK with the language for partner-issued credits; no data residency concerns flagged for the requested flow. Approve the activation with these operational constraints:
- Per-account cap: $500 equivalent in credits
- Monthly cap: 2,000 total credits across all redemptions (to start)
- Expiry: 6 months from issuance, non-extendable without renewed approval

Dylan — please set a spend alert at 75% of the monthly cap and create a HubSpot activity on deal D-2026-9812 to track redemptions. Make sure marketplace redemptions include the Acme partner ID and are attributed to AcmeCloud in metrics.

Once Ops confirms the CSV format and we have the issuance runbook, Ava you can confirm go-live with Samantha.

-Marissa
Marissa Cole
Director, Partnerships | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Samantha Li <samantha@acmecloud.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, billing-ops@redwood.ai
Date: Sun, 21 Mar 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Partner credit activation request — AcmeCloud marketplace

Forwarding final confirmation and issuance details.

On Thu, Mar 19, 2026 at 09:45 AM Samantha Li <samantha@acmecloud.io> wrote:
> Thanks Ava — appreciate the quick turnaround.
> 
> Confirming AcmeCloud will surface the promo code ACME_PLG_2026 as part of our onboarding flow. We're attaching the marketplace activation CSV that contains the partner IDs we'll send to Redwood at redemption time. Please confirm the following on your end:
> - Batch upload format accepted for credits (CSV columns)
> - TTL / expiry rules once a user redeems via marketplace
> - Any required webhook/endpoint for real-time redemption confirmations
> 
> We'd like to go live 03/25 and will pause on our side until you confirm issuance workflow.
> 
> Best,
> Samantha Li
> Partner Ops | AcmeCloud Integrations

Hi all — operational update: issuance completed from Redwood Console (credits batch ID CB-4420). Summary:
- Promo code: ACME_PLG_2026
- Credit amount per redemption: $500 equivalent
- Total issued (initial batch): 200 credits tokens (safely below monthly cap)
- Expiry: 2026-09-21 (6 months)
- HubSpot deal updated: D-2026-9812 (Ava has the tracking link)

Attachments: acme_marketplace_credits.csv (CSV of issued voucher IDs), acme_mou_signed.pdf

Links:
Issue console: https://console.redwood.ai/credits/issue/D-2026-9812
Marketplace offer: https://marketplace.acmecloud.io/offer/redwood-credits

If anything looks off (format, attribution, expiry) flag here and I'll roll back the batch. Otherwise, Ava please confirm with Samantha that we're ready for the 03/25 go-live.

Thanks,
Dylan Brooks
Sales Operations | Redwood Inference
Dylan.Brooks@redwood.ai

To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Cc: partnerships@redwood.ai
Date: Wed, 18 Mar 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Partner credit activation request — AcmeCloud marketplace

Hi team,

AcmeCloud (marketplace partner) has requested we activate partner-issued free credits for their PLG signups. Quick summary:
- Offer: $500 in Redwood credits (pro-rated to tokens) for new accounts sourcing from AcmeCloud marketplace (promo code ACME_PLG_2026)
- Volume estimate: ~200 activations in first 60 days
- Expiry: 6 months from issuance

They've attached the signed MOU (acme_mou_signed.pdf) and want us to enable self-serve issuance so new users redeem via the marketplace flow. Can we please confirm:
1) Compliance / legal sign-off on the MOU copy (Marissa)
2) Operational cap: max credits per month and overall cap to limit spend (Dylan / Ops)
3) Console/action plan for issuance: will this be a partner-issued code on the marketplace or do we need to manually create credit batches?

If approved, I'd like to enable this for the upcoming Acme promotional window starting 03/25. Let me know if you need more details; attaching the partner email and MOU.

Thanks,
Ava
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0142

Best,
Samantha Li

source.subject: CareLink - 429 surge after tier migration
messages:
From: Tom Ellis <t.ellis@carelink.health>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: CareLink Ops <ops@carelink.health>
Date: Fri, 20 Mar 2026 07:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production errors — 429 flood since last night

Ava / team,

We're seeing a sharp spike in 429s starting ~01:30 UTC this morning after we moved a large cohort of traffic to the new enterprise tier. Impact: API calls from our ingestion pipeline are being rate-limited, causing downstream processing delays and timeouts for clinical syncs.

Quick details:
- Affected endpoints: /v1/generate and /v1/embeddings
- Sample org/project IDs: org_carelink_7 / proj-ingest-14
- Approx. 20% of requests returned 429, many followed by retries that compounding failures

Can you confirm whether any quota/tiering change rolled out today and whether you can temporarily raise limits while we stabilize? We're prepared to open a maintenance window if needed.

Attaching a small excerpt of request logs for context (full set available on request).

—Tom
Tom Ellis
Head of Platform Engineering | CareLink Health
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Tom Ellis <t.ellis@carelink.health>
Cc: CareLink Ops <ops@carelink.health>
Date: Fri, 20 Mar 2026 08:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Production errors — 429 flood since last night

Tom — thanks for the quick heads up. I'm on it and looping in our SRE and Solutions team. Immediate next steps:

1) Can you paste one full request-id and exact timestamp (UTC) for a failing call?
2) We'll check recent tier mapping and quota assignments for org_carelink_7 / proj-ingest-14 and the rollout schedule.
3) If we confirm a misassignment, we'll provision a temporary quota bump and disable the new mapping until we have a safe rollback.

Sean (SE) and Grace (SRE) — can you prioritize an audit of CareLink's org/project tier assignment and the rate-limiter logs from 00:00-04:00 UTC? I may need an exec-level temporary quota increase if this is a customer-impacting misconfiguration.

I'll stand up a short bridge if helpful; otherwise we'll update here within 45 minutes.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Mar 2026 08:28:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Production errors — 429 flood since last night

Adding logs and quick findings. I pulled the audit for the tier migration that hit CareLink at 00:47 UTC: team autoscale pushed org_carelink_7 into the enterprise tier but the project label proj-ingest-14 retained the legacy per-project cap (5 rps). Looks like a label aliasing bug during the rollout (alias proj-ingest -> proj-ingest-14) so the project kept the lower quota.

Evidence attached: throttle_config_export.csv (current per-project caps) and a short sample of the traffic (carelink-traffic-logs-20260320.zip).

Proposed immediate mitigation:
- Temporary increase proj-ingest-14 cap to 50 rps (manual override)
- Rollback the aliasing change and re-run the tier mapping job in dry-run mode
- Throttle back client-side retries using 60s backoff guidance (ask CareLink to patch ingestion pipeline)

Grace — can you confirm whether applying the manual override will trigger any downstream autoscaling churn? I want to ensure we don't create a follow-on capacity surge.

—Sean
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Ellis <t.ellis@carelink.health>, CareLink Ops <ops@carelink.health>
Date: Fri, 20 Mar 2026 09:01:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Production errors — 429 surge after tier migration

I reviewed the rate-limiter and routing traces. Confirming Sean's hypothesis: the org-to-tier mapping executed, but our project-label fallback logic matched the old label variant and applied the wrong per-project quota. That created an effective cap far below expected.

Immediate steps I executed:
- Applied manual override: proj-ingest-14 -> 50 rps (effective within 90s).
- Disabled the problematic alias mapping for the live rollout job.
- Monitored for retry storm amplification — currently seeing request success rates climb from 78% -> 96% over 20 minutes.

Caveats / follow-ups:
- We'll need a short scheduled maintenance window to safely re-run the tiering job with corrected label canonicalization.
- Recommend a temporary customer-side retry guidance email: set exponential backoff starting at 15s, cap at 5 attempts. Their current 2s immediate retries are amplifying the issue.

Attaching: throttle_config_export.csv (post-override).

—Grace
SRE, Redwood Inference
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Ellis <t.ellis@carelink.health>, CareLink Ops <ops@carelink.health>
Date: Fri, 20 Mar 2026 13:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Production errors — CareLink stabilization and next steps

Aditya — heads up and request for short approval. CareLink experienced a 429 spike due to a tiering label alias bug. Grace applied a manual override (proj-ingest-14 -> 50 rps) and we've seen success rate recover to ~96%.

Asks:
- Approve a one-time customer credit for the incident (recommendation: one week of overage forgiveness capped at $5k) to expedite renewal conversations.
- Approve a limited maintenance window tomorrow 02:00–03:00 UTC to re-run the corrected tiering job and validate label canonicalization in staging.

I'll coordinate customer communications and a short technical postmortem with CareLink once the rollback/retest is complete.

Tom / Ops — appreciate the quick collaboration — we'll send a short bulletin with retry guidance and confirm the maintenance window timing.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 (555) 212-0943
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Combined product demo + MSA redline session — proposed slots
messages:
From: Marta Gomez <marta.gomez@heliosystems.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, procurement@heliosystems.com
Date: Thu, 19 Mar 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: combined product demo + MSA redline session

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the intro earlier this week. Procurement and legal at Helio are trying to compress our remaining intake steps into one touchpoint to meet an internal deadline. Would Redwood be able to run a combined session that covers: a short product walkthrough (value props + integration requirements) and an MSA redline discussion? We have both technical and legal stakeholders who need to be present.

Suggested format: 75 minutes total — 45 min demo (product + integration Qs) then 30 min legal redline walk-through.

Two windows that work for our group (PDT):
- Mon Mar 23, 10:00–11:15
- Tue Mar 24, 08:30–09:45

If neither works, we can do two separate back-to-back invites but prefer one combined slot if possible. I can upload our current markups if that helps.

Attendees (high-level):
- Marta Gomez (procurement)
- Priya Shah (legal)
- Ravi Menon (platform lead)

Thanks — appreciate any confirmation and who from your side should join this call.

Best,
Marta
Marta Gomez
Head of Procurement, Helio Systems

---
From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Marta Gomez <marta.gomez@heliosystems.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Mar 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: combined product demo + MSA redline session

Hi Marta — great to hear. We can absolutely run that combined session and the 75-minute split sounds reasonable.

From Redwood side I propose:
- 45 min product + integration walkthrough: Ava Chen (Enterprise Sales) + Jonas Weber (Solutions Engineering)
- 30 min MSA redline: Kimberly Park (Legal)

We have availability for Mon Mar 23 10:00–11:15 PDT. If that works for you we can send a single invite and attach a short agenda and your redline file.

Quick logistics/questions:
- Please share the current redline (PDF or Word) so Kimberly can review ahead of the call.
- Do you want the demo to include a short screenshare of your planned integration points? (Jonas can tailor.)

I can loop in our technical PM if you expect deeper infra questions.

If Mon 23 is good I will send a calendar invite with the combined agenda and ask Kimberly to join the second half.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123

---
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Mar 2026 15:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: Helio combined session — legal availability

Ava — thanks for flagging this. I can do the Mon Mar 23 10:00–11:15 window (PDT) but will need the redline at least 24 hours prior to the call so I can triage material and note major clauses. If Helio can attach the file when they confirm, that will let me prep and avoid a surprise on the call.

Jonas — can you plan for a 45-minute demo focused on integration patterns and typical onboarding blockers (auth, data retention, telemetry)? If you think we need a PM, we should bring one.

Proposed call agenda (draft):
1) 10:00–10:10 — intros & success criteria (Ava / Marta)
2) 10:10–10:55 — product demo & integration Qs (Jonas)
3) 10:55–11:25 — MSA redline highlights (Kimberly)
4) 11:25–11:15 — next steps / action items (Ava)

(Yes, agenda order has the redline compressed to 30 min — we can follow up with a separate legal deep-dive if needed.)

I will be sure to join the second half; tag me if they send the MSARedlines PDF.

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
kimberly_park@redwood.com

---
From: Marta Gomez <marta.gomez@heliosystems.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@heliosystems.com, Priya Shah <priya.shah@heliosystems.com>, Ravi Menon <ravi.menon@heliosystems.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Mar 2026 11:35:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: combined product demo + MSA redline session

Ava / Kimberly / Jonas — thank you all for the quick turnaround. Mon Mar 23, 10:00–11:15 PDT works for our team. I've attached the latest redline and a short agenda; please see the two attachments below.

source.subject: Exec update: Runtime cost optimization — workload-aware batching defaults (SLO tiers) | status + go/no-go gates
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 12, 2026 at 9:18 AM PDT
Subject: Exec update: Runtime cost optimization — workload-aware batching defaults (SLO tiers) | status + go/no-go gates

Sharing a leadership-level status update on “workload-aware batching defaults” (fast / standard / cost-efficient tiers) ahead of the runtime-1.22 cut.

TL;DR
- On track for staged canary in late March, with a hosted subset rollout target early April if gates stay green.
- Expected unit economics impact: meaningful cost reduction for standard + cost-efficient tiers without breaking published latency SLOs.
- Primary risks we’re actively mitigating: fast-tier tail latency regression on one model family, and telemetry cardinality / cost.

Progress (last 2 weeks)
1) Runtime + config plumbing
- Tiered batching profile schema is in (backwards compatible; existing routes keep behavior unless tier defaults enabled).
- Scheduler now consumes workload signals: streaming vs non-streaming + prompt-length buckets. Concurrency signal is in progress behind a flag.
- Dedicated override precedence implemented (customer override > route policy > tier default).

2) Observability + explainability
- Tier label is present on the key latency/cost metrics, with cardinality controls (after early canary tests showed the obvious foot-gun).
- Reason codes emitted for “why this profile” (tier default vs override vs model constraint vs overload mode).

3) Benchmarking + perf canary
- Mixed-workload scenarios (mixed prompt lengths + mixed streaming) are now in benchmark-lab. Perf canary gates updated to enforce tier-specific p95/p99 thresholds.

Expected impact (current perf summary)
- Standard tier: ~8–12% better throughput (tokens/sec per GPU) on representative hosted workloads; p95 within target in the canary harness. $/1K tokens improvement tracks throughput.
- Cost-efficient tier: ~15–22% throughput improvement in non-streaming + higher concurrency mixes; requires tighter overload guardrails to avoid queue blowups.
- Fast tier: intended to be neutral on cost (minor throughput impact acceptable) with stronger p99 protections. This is where we saw a p99 regression on one model family in one region under mixed workloads; mitigations below.

Key risks + mitigations
1) Fast tier tail latency regressions (p99)
- Root cause hypothesis: admission control + wait-budget parameters interacting poorly with long-prompt prefill under mixed traffic.
- Mitigation: fast-tier anti-starvation tweaks + more conservative max-wait + stricter queue admission when long-prefill dominance detected.
- Status: fix is implemented behind a flag; re-running benchmark-lab + perf canary baselines this week.

2) Overload behavior + queue instability (cost-efficient)
- Risk: aggressive batching can increase queue depth quickly during bursts.
- Mitigation: overload mode with backpressure + max queue depth limits; hard stop to load shed before tail latency explodes.

3) Telemetry cost / cardinality
- Risk surfaced in early internal canary simulation; tier label can amplify series count.
- Mitigation: cardinality controls + tier sampling rules; additional “hotfix” path is ready if we see series explosion again.

Proposed rollout (subject to go/no-go gates)
- Internal canary: 1 region, 1–2 top model families, fast + standard enabled first; cost-efficient only on non-streaming endpoints.
- Hosted API subset: 5% traffic for standard; fast opt-in for streaming routes; cost-efficient opt-in first.
- Dedicated: opt-in only initially; provide clear override precedence docs + support playbook.

Go/No-Go gates (what must be green)
1) Perf canary
- Fast tier: p99 regression <= 3% vs baseline on top models/regions; no starvation under mixed workloads.
- Standard tier: p95 regression <= 2% vs baseline; p99 regression <= 3%.
- Cost-efficient tier: p95 allowed up to tier target, but no queue blowups (queue depth bounded; no sustained overload mode in steady state).

2) Reliability + error budget
- No increase in 5xx / timeouts attributable to batching changes in canary.
- Error budget burn within weekly guardrails during canary; paging thresholds for fast-tier p99 only (standard/cost-efficient are ticketing).

3) Observability
- Tier-labeled dashboards live + validated; reason codes present in traces for sample traffic.
- Telemetry series count within budget (explicit cap by region/model/tier).

4) Rollback readiness
- Kill switch verified end-to-end (runtime flag + control plane) with a documented rollback runbook.

Attachments
- ExecUpdate_WorkloadAwareBatchingDefaults_2026-03-12.pdf (1-pager)
- TieredBatching_GoNoGo_Gates_v3.xlsx (gates + owners)

If helpful, I can do a 15-min walkthrough in next week’s product/eng leadership sync.

—
Ava Chen
Program Lead, Runtime Cost Optimization
Redwood Inference


On Thu, Mar 12, 2026 at 10:02 AM PDT, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 12, 2026 at 10:02 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Runtime cost optimization — workload-aware batching defaults (SLO tiers) | status + go/no-go gates

Thanks Ava.

Two asks from the Optimize side so we don’t over-promise:
1) Can we add an explicit note that “cost-efficient” is primarily targeted at non-streaming workloads (at least for initial launch), and that streaming endpoints should default to standard unless the customer opts into fast?
2) For the exec 1-pager: do we have a single “headline number” for hosted unit cost reduction we’re comfortable with (even as a range) so Product can align messaging?

Also +1 on the 15-min walkthrough. I’ll slot it if you send me 2–3 times.

Aditya


On Fri, Mar 13, 2026 at 8:26 AM PDT, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 13, 2026 at 8:26 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Runtime cost optimization — workload-aware batching defaults (SLO tiers) | status + go/no-go gates

+1, this is good.

Hosted API rollout considerations from my side:
- I’d like the initial hosted subset to be “standard only” by default (5% ramp) and keep fast and cost-efficient as explicit opt-ins until we have at least 1 week of canary data.
- We should define an explicit customer comms trigger for fast-tier p99 regression (status page vs support proactive outreach). Even if we don’t expect it, we should decide now.

Question: are we comfortable tying the go/no-go to “no sustained overload mode in steady state” as a gate? That seems a little subjective—can we define it as e.g. <1% of requests entering overload mode over a 60-min window during canary?

Nikhil


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 13, 2026 at 11:06 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Runtime cost optimization — workload-aware batching defaults (SLO tiers) | status + go/no-go gates

All makes sense.

Aditya
1) Yes — we’ll state clearly: cost-efficient is initially targeted to non-streaming, high concurrency workloads. For streaming, we’ll recommend standard by default; fast is the “stable streaming latency” option.
2) Headline number: for hosted, we should stick to a range and the tier qualifier. Proposed: “Standard: 8–12% $/1K token reduction on representative workloads; Cost-efficient: 15–22% on non-streaming high concurrency workloads; Fast: cost neutral / slight increase acceptable to protect p99.” I’ll update the 1-pager wording to match.

Nikhil
- Agree on standard-only default for the first hosted subset.
- For overload-mode gate: yes, we’ll make it quantitative. I’ll align with SRE on a crisp definition; your suggestion is a good starting point. Tentatively:
  - Overload mode entry rate < 1% over rolling 60 min during canary, and
  - No continuous overload mode > 5 min on a single cluster without triggering automatic shed + alert.
- Customer comms trigger: I’ll propose a simple matrix for status page vs proactive outreach based on fast-tier p99 delta + duration + impacted traffic share; will share for review.

I’ll send 3 times for a 15-min walkthrough; aiming for Tue/Wed next week.

—
Ava

Ava Chen
Program Lead, Runtime Cost Optimization
Redwood Inference

- For overload-mode gate: yes, we’ll make it quantitative. I’ll align with SRE on a crisp definition; your suggestion is a good starting point. Tentatively:
  - Overload mode entry rate < 1% over rolling 60 min during canary, and
  - No continuous overload mode > 5 min on a single cluster without triggering automatic shed + alert.
- Customer comms trigger: I’ll propose a simple matrix for status page vs proactive outreach based on fast-tier p99 delta + duration + impacted traffic share; will share for review.

I’ll send 3 times for a 15-min walkthrough; aiming for Tue/Wed next week.

—
Ava

Ava Chen
Program Lead, Runtime Cost Optimization
Redwood Inference
text:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 12, 2026 at 9:18 AM PDT
Subject: Exec update: Runtime cost optimization — workload-aware batching defaults (SLO tiers) | status + go/no-go gates

Sharing a leadership-level status update on “workload-aware batching defaults” (fast / standard / cost-efficient tiers) ahead of the runtime-1.22 cut.

TL;DR
- On track for staged canary in late March, with a hosted subset rollout target early April if gates stay green.
- Expected unit economics impact: meaningful cost reduction for standard + cost-efficient tiers without breaking published latency SLOs.
- Primary risks we’re actively mitigating: fast-tier tail latency regression on one model family, and telemetry cardinality / cost.

Progress (last 2 weeks)
1) Runtime + config plumbing
- Tiered batching profile schema is in (backwards compatible; existing routes keep behavior unless tier defaults enabled).
- Scheduler now consumes workload signals: streaming vs non-streaming + prompt-length buckets. Concurrency signal is in progress behind a flag.
- Dedicated override precedence implemented (customer override > route policy > tier default).

2) Observability + explainability
- Tier label is present on the key latency/cost metrics, with cardinality controls (after early canary tests showed the obvious foot-gun).
- Reason codes emitted for “why this profile” (tier default vs override vs model constraint vs overload mode).

3) Benchmarking + perf canary
- Mixed-workload scenarios (mixed prompt lengths + mixed streaming) are now in benchmark-lab. Perf canary gates updated to enforce tier-specific p95/p99 thresholds.

Expected impact (current perf summary)
- Standard tier: ~8–12% better throughput (tokens/sec per GPU) on representative hosted workloads; p95 within target in the canary harness. $/1K tokens improvement tracks throughput.
- Cost-efficient tier: ~15–22% throughput improvement in non-streaming + higher concurrency mixes; requires tighter overload guardrails to avoid queue blowups.
- Fast tier: intended to be neutral on cost (minor throughput impact acceptable) with stronger p99 protections. This is where we saw a p99 regression on one model family in one region under mixed workloads; mitigations below.

Key risks + mitigations
1) Fast tier tail latency regressions (p99)
- Root cause hypothesis: admission control + wait-budget parameters interacting poorly with long-prompt prefill under mixed traffic.
- Mitigation: fast-tier anti-starvation tweaks + more conservative max-wait + stricter queue admission when long-prefill dominance detected.
- Status: fix is implemented behind a flag; re-running benchmark-lab + perf canary baselines this week.

2) Overload behavior + queue instability (cost-efficient)
- Risk: aggressive batching can increase queue depth quickly during bursts.
- Mitigation: overload mode with backpressure + max queue depth limits; hard stop to load shed before tail latency explodes.

3) Telemetry cost / cardinality
- Risk surfaced in early internal canary simulation; tier label can amplify series count.
- Mitigation: cardinality controls + tier sampling rules; additional “hotfix” path is ready if we see series explosion again.

Proposed rollout (subject to go/no-go gates)
- Internal canary: 1 region, 1–2 top model families, fast + standard enabled first; cost-efficient only on non-streaming endpoints.
- Hosted API subset: 5% traffic for standard; fast opt-in for streaming routes; cost-efficient opt-in first.
- Dedicated: opt-in only initially; provide clear override precedence docs + support playbook.

Go/No-Go gates (what must be green)
1) Perf canary
- Fast tier: p99 regression <= 3% vs baseline on top models/regions; no starvation under mixed workloads.
- Standard tier: p95 regression <= 2% vs baseline; p99 regression <= 3%.
- Cost-efficient tier: p95 allowed up to tier target, but no queue blowups (queue depth bounded; no sustained overload mode in steady state).

2) Reliability + error budget
- No increase in 5xx / timeouts attributable to batching changes in canary.
- Error budget burn within weekly guardrails during canary; paging thresholds for fast-tier p99 only (standard/cost-efficient are ticketing).

3) Observability
- Tier-labeled dashboards live + validated; reason codes present in traces for sample traffic.
- Telemetry series count within budget (explicit cap by region/model/tier).

4) Rollback readiness
- Kill switch verified end-to-end (runtime flag + control plane) with a documented rollback runbook.

Attachments
- ExecUpdate_WorkloadAwareBatchingDefaults_2026-03-12.pdf (1-pager)
- TieredBatching_GoNoGo_Gates_v3.xlsx (gates + owners)

If helpful, I can do a 15-min walkthrough in next week’s product/eng leadership sync.

—
Ava Chen
Program Lead, Runtime Cost Optimization
Redwood Inference


On Thu, Mar 12, 2026 at 10:02 AM PDT, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 12, 2026 at 10:02 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Runtime cost optimization — workload-aware batching defaults (SLO tiers) | status + go/no-go gates

Thanks Ava.

Two asks from the Optimize side so we don’t over-promise:
1) Can we add an explicit note that “cost-efficient” is primarily targeted at non-streaming workloads (at least for initial launch), and that streaming endpoints should default to standard unless the customer opts into fast?
2) For the exec 1-pager: do we have a single “headline number” for hosted unit cost reduction we’re comfortable with (even as a range) so Product can align messaging?

Also +1 on the 15-min walkthrough. I’ll slot it if you send me 2–3 times.

Aditya


On Fri, Mar 13, 2026 at 8:26 AM PDT, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 13, 2026 at 8:26 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Runtime cost optimization — workload-aware batching defaults (SLO tiers) | status + go/no-go gates

+1, this is good.

Hosted API rollout considerations from my side:
- I’d like the initial hosted subset to be “standard only” by default (5% ramp) and keep fast and cost-efficient as explicit opt-ins until we have at least 1 week of canary data.
- We should define an explicit customer comms trigger for fast-tier p99 regression (status page vs support proactive outreach). Even if we don’t expect it, we should decide now.

Question: are we comfortable tying the go/no-go to “no sustained overload mode in steady state” as a gate? That seems a little subjective—can we define it as e.g. <1% of requests entering overload mode over a 60-min window during canary?

Nikhil


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 13, 2026 at 11:06 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Runtime cost optimization — workload-aware batching defaults (SLO tiers) | status + go/no-go gates

All makes sense.

Aditya
1) Yes — we’ll state clearly: cost-efficient is initially targeted to non-streaming, high concurrency workloads. For streaming, we’ll recommend standard by default; fast is the “stable streaming latency” option.
2) Headline number: for hosted, we should stick to a range and the tier qualifier. Proposed: “Standard: 8–12% $/1K token reduction on representative workloads; Cost-efficient: 15–22% on non-streaming high concurrency workloads; Fast: cost neutral / slight increase acceptable to protect p99.” I’ll update the 1-pager wording to match.

Nikhil
- Agree on standard-only default for the first hosted subset.
- For overload-mode gate: yes, we’ll make it quantitative. I’ll align with SRE on a crisp definition; your suggestion is a good starting point. Tentatively:
  - Overload mode entry rate < 1% over rolling 60 min during canary, and
  - No continuous overload mode > 5 min on a single cluster without triggering automatic shed + alert.
- Customer comms trigger: I’ll propose a simple matrix for status page vs proactive outreach based on fast-tier p99 delta + duration + impacted traffic share; will share for review.

I’ll send 3 times for a 15-min walkthrough; aiming for Tue/Wed next week.

—
Ava

Ava Chen
Program Lead, Runtime Cost Optimization
Redwood Inference

messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Megan Li <megan.li@aurorafin.com>, Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@aurorafin.com>
Cc: procurement@aurorafin.com
Date: Mon, 09 Mar 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: RFI response: Assimilation roadmap and interoperability acceptance checklist

Hi Megan / Oliver,

Thanks for the detailed RFI — attached is our first-pass assimilation roadmap and a lightweight interoperability acceptance checklist to help Aurora align internal teams. Key highlights:

- Proposed 6-week onboarding cadence (pre-prod shadowing -> staged canary -> full cutover). See tab "Milestones" in the attached roadmap.
- Canary plan: 3-week ramp with percent-based traffic shifts (1%, 5%, 20%, 60%, 100%) and telemetry gates at each step.
- Integration touchpoints: VPC peering (or PrivateLink), SAML SSO + SCIM provisioning, and mutual TLS for service-to-service calls.
- Data controls: model version pinning, per-route token redaction, and optional prefix caching to limit PII exposure.

Items we need from Aurora to finalize timeline:
1) Preferred canary windows (UTC range) and maintenance blackout days
2) VPC CIDR for peering/testing and NAT/Egress constraints
3) Decision on SSO vs API key for service accounts (we support both)
4) Any runbook owners for Aurora (name/email) so we can include them in the kick-off

Attachments included: redwood_assimilation_roadmap_v1.xlsx (timeline + gating), integration_acceptance_criteria.pdf (acceptance checklist), network_requirements_summary.pdf (ports & endpoints).

If helpful, we can run a 30-minute walkthrough of the worksheet tomorrow (Tue). Proposed times: 10:00–10:30am PT or 2:00–2:30pm PT.

Cheers,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

---
Attachments:
- redwood_assimilation_roadmap_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet, 5 sheets)
- integration_acceptance_criteria.pdf (PDF, 4 pages)
- network_requirements_summary.pdf (PDF, 2 pages)

From: Megan Li <megan.li@aurorafin.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@aurorafin.com>
Cc: procurement@aurorafin.com
Date: Mon, 09 Mar 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: RFI response: Assimilation roadmap and interoperability acceptance checklist

Thanks Ava — this is very helpful. A few clarifications from the Aurora integration team so we can finalize dependencies: 

- Canary windows: we prefer low-traffic windows for the heavier shifts. Our default maintenance window is 01:00–04:00 UTC on Sundays. Are your telemetry gates timezone-agnostic (they're just thresholds we can evaluate in UTC)?
- Data residency: Aurora requires customer data to remain in us-west-2 for this initial rollout. Can you confirm the hosted model instances can be pinned to that region and that logs/audit trails are stored in our dedicated S3 bucket?
- Auth: procurement prefers SAML for human SSO and short-lived API keys for machine/service accounts. Need confirmation on SCIM support for user provisioning.
- Acceptance criteria: add a bullet to the checklist for latency SLOs (p95 <= 350ms for standard generation route) and error-rate threshold (<0.5% 5xx across 24h).

Also attaching a short set of Aurora security questions we still need answered (I will send the doc separately to procurement). For the timeline: can you split out the "pre-prod shadowing" phase into two weeks (1 week shadowing, 1 week parallel validate)? That gives us time to exercise our egress guards.

We can do the walkthrough tomorrow at 2:00pm PT. I'll invite Oliver and our integration lead (David Park) if available.

Thanks,
Megan Li
Integration Lead, Aurora Financial
megan.li@aurorafin.com

---
On Mon, Mar 9, 2026 at 09:12 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> [original message omitted]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 09 Mar 2026 13:10:00 -07:00
Subject: FW: RFI response: Assimilation roadmap and interoperability acceptance checklist  (internal)

Team — forwarding Aurora's clarifications and a quick ask so we can turn around an updated plan during the 2pm walkthrough. Internal only.

Summary of asks from Aurora:
- Pin hosted instances to us-west-2 and ensure audit logs can be exported to Aurora's S3 (they will provide bucket ARN).
- Split pre-prod shadowing into 1wk shadow + 1wk parallel validate.
- Add latency and error-rate acceptance criteria to the checklist.
- Confirm SCIM support and SAML for human SSO.

Action items:
- Markus: confirm region pinning feasibility and any additional infra lead time for reserved capacity in us-west-2. Also confirm whether we can offer HSM/KMS integration for customer-managed keys during Private mode onboarding.
- Ben: draft a canary gating runbook with telemetry thresholds and rollback triggers (include probe queries and a small eval suite). Put a preview in the shared drive link.
- Laura: update the acceptance criteria doc to include p95 latency and 5xx error thresholds; add an "Operational Sign-off" checklist for Aurora to complete post-canary.

I'll consolidate changes and send the updated package + DocuSign SOW link to Aurora after the walkthrough.

Thanks —
Ava

On Mon, Mar 9, 2026 at 11:03 AM Megan Li <megan.li@aurorafin.com> wrote:
> [external message omitted]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Megan Li <megan.li@aurorafin.com>, Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@aurorafin.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, procurement@aurorafin.com
Date: Wed, 11 Mar 2026 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Updated assimilation roadmap, acceptance criteria, and runbook preview

Hi Megan / Oliver,

Thanks for the walkthrough earlier today — great alignment. Attached are the updated artifacts we discussed:

- redwood_assimilation_roadmap_v1.xlsx (updated):
  * Week 0: Kickoff & network provisioning (VPC peering / PrivateLink + S3 bucket access)
  * Weeks 1–2: Pre-prod (Week 1 shadowing, Week 2 parallel validate)
  * Weeks 3–5: Canary ramp (1% -> 5% -> 20% -> 60% -> 100%, gating windows listed in sheet)
  * Week 6: Final acceptance and handover

- integration_acceptance_criteria.pdf (updated):
  * Added operational sign-off checklist and explicit SLOs (p95 <= 350ms, 5xx < 0.5% 24h).
  * Added security gating: SOC2 evidence pack checklist and data residency confirmation.

- runbook_preview.md: Excerpt of canary gating runbook with rollback triggers:
  * Automatic rollback on sustained >0.5% 5xx for 30m OR p95 degradation >150ms vs baseline for 15m.
  * Manual rollback if functional regressions discovered by Aurora validation team with documented repro steps.

Region pinning: Markus confirmed we can reserve capacity in us-west-2 and route Aurora's tenant to instances in that region. We can also export audit logs to the S3 bucket (we will need a dedicated IAM role ARN from Aurora). HSM integration for customer-managed keys is available as part of Private deployments; we can include that in the SOW addendum.

SCIM and SAML: Laura confirms SCIM provisioning is supported for user lifecycle; SAML 2.0 works for UI login and we emit short-lived JWTs for SDK/service flows. We'll include a sample SAML metadata XML in the shared drive.

Next steps:
- I've uploaded the updated artifacts to our shared folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3-redwood-aurora-runbook
- DocuSign SOW link (for signature once procurement confirms commercial terms): https://www.docusign.com/Envelope/Pages/DocuSignID-AF-1834

Please review the updated docs. If Aurora is comfortable, proposed start for Pre-prod shadowing: Mon, Mar 23, 2026 (shadowing week1), with parallel validate starting Mon, Mar 30, 2026.

Regards,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com

---
Attachments:
- redwood_assimilation_roadmap_v1.xlsx (updated)
- integration_acceptance_criteria.pdf (updated)
- runbook_preview.md (Markdown excerpt)

From: Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@aurorafin.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Megan Li <megan.li@aurorafin.com>
Cc: procurement@aurorafin.com, David Park <david.park@aurorafin.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Mar 2026 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Updated assimilation roadmap, acceptance criteria, and runbook preview

Ava / team —

Appreciate the fast turnaround and the level of detail. Aurora is comfortable with the proposed split (shadowing + parallel validate) and the canary ramp percentages and gates. A few operational confirmations: 

- We will provide the S3 bucket ARN and a dedicated IAM role by Fri, Mar 13.
- Procurement will review the DocuSign SOW tomorrow; target to sign the commercial SOW by Fri, Mar 20.
- Proposed start: Pre-prod shadowing week of Mon, Mar 23 is confirmed on our side. Our integration lead (David Park) will run the validation exercises during Week 2.

One ask: can Redwood provide a short smoke-test harness (curl + example prompt) we can run during Week 1 shadowing to validate latency and tokenization behavior? If Ben can drop that into the shared drive preview, that will speed validation.

If all is green on your end, we'll treat this as green-light and start internal scheduling.

Thanks,
Oliver Grant
VP, Platform Integrations
Aurora Financial
oliver.grant@aurorafin.com

---

> [updated artifacts omitted]

messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Sanjay Patel <sanjay.patel@finsigma.com>, Kate Liu <kate.liu@finsigma.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 02 Mar 2026 15:05:00 +0000
Subject: Recap + next steps — SDK auth, routing, and rate-limit items

Hi Sanjay / Kate —

Great to meet today. Quick recap of what we covered (tech-focused items) and proposed next steps so we can get a short POC running next week:

What we covered
- SDK setup and auth: token rotation, short-lived API keys per environment, KMS integration for Private installs.
- Routing and model variants: edge routing for latency-sensitive endpoints; fallback to smaller/quantized models for high throughput.
- Structured output & tool-calling: function schema validation and deterministic output for payments/workflow calls.
- Embeddings vs reranker: embeddings for retrieval; reranker to re-rank top-N results when precision matters.
- Rate limiting / quotas / retries: per-API-key quotas, 429 handling, Retry-After, idempotency keys for mutating calls.

Immediate asks / next steps
1) Can you share a test service account + sample query patterns (tokens per request, qps, burst behavior)?
2) We'll send a short integration guide and SDK snippets (attached). Please try the token-auth example and confirm you can get a 200.
3) Engineering sync: proposed Wed 2026-03-04 at 15:00 PT — confirm availability.

Attachments: redwood_integration_guide.pdf (overview), rate_limit_matrix.csv (estimate table), sdk-snippets.zip (auth + backoff examples)

I'll loop in Ava on the engineering thread so she can answer detailed questions.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

From: Sanjay Patel <sanjay.patel@finsigma.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
Cc: Kate Liu <kate.liu@finsigma.com>
Date: Mon, 02 Mar 2026 17:18:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Recap + next steps — SDK auth, routing, and rate-limit items

Aditya —

Thanks for the quick recap and for the attachments. Quick questions from our infra side before the POC:

1) Rate limits: do you expose both per-second and per-minute limits? We need to map to our gateway which applies burst limits per-connection. Also wondering about concurrent connection limits for streaming endpoints.
2) 429 handling: what headers do you return (Retry-After, X-Rate-Limit-Remaining, X-Rate-Limit-Reset)? We prefer graceful client-side retries with jitter.
3) Idempotency: for calls that create resources (e.g., tool-triggered payments), do you recommend an Idempotency-Key header? Any server-side dedupe windows/TTL?
4) Embeddings: for batch-upsert, what's the recommended batch size and how do you advise avoiding hitting transient rate limits during bulk imports?

I'll attach the traffic profile doc after I extract it. For the meeting Wed, 15:00 PT works for Kate and me.

—Sanjay
Sanjay Patel
Head of Platform Engineering
Finsigma

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Sanjay Patel <sanjay.patel@finsigma.com>, Kate Liu <kate.liu@finsigma.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 02 Mar 2026 20:35:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Recap + next steps — SDK auth, routing, and rate-limit items

Sanjay / Kate —

Thanks for the questions. I'm Ada (engineer on Integrations) — below are pragmatic recommendations you can implement in the POC and link back to for production. I'll paste a short pseudocode snippet for throttling/retries and call out headers we return.

1) Rate limits & quotas- We publish a rate-limit matrix (attached). We enforce both per-second (r/s) and per-minute (r/m) limits on the hosted SKU and per-customer quotas on Dedicated/Private. Typical hosted example: 50 r/s and 3000 r/m for medium tier; streaming endpoints have a concurrent-stream cap (default 10 concurrent streams per API key).
- Bursting: gateway allows short bursts; we track steady-state ROQ and enforce sliding-window counts.

2) Headers we return on throttled requests- Retry-After: seconds until next allowed request (if known)
- X-RateLimit-Limit: configured limit window size (e.g., 50)
- X-RateLimit-Remaining: tokens left in current window
- X-RateLimit-Reset: epoch seconds when window resets
- On 429 we also include a machine-readable body with reason and "suggested_backoff_ms" when applicable.

3) 429 handling recommendations- Always examine Retry-After when present. If absent, use exponential backoff with full jitter. Prefer capped backoff (max 60s) and an overall retry budget per operation (e.g., 6 attempts).

Suggested pseudocode (client-side)
- base = 200 ms
- max_backoff = 60000 ms
- for attempt in 1..max_attempts:
    sleep = min(max_backoff, base * 2^(attempt - 1))
    jitter = random(0, sleep)
    wait(jitter)
    resp = call_api()
    if resp.status == 200: return resp
    if resp.status == 429:
        if resp.headers['Retry-After']:
            wait_seconds = parse_retry_after(resp.headers['Retry-After'])
            wait(wait_seconds)
        else:
            continue // will backoff on next loop iteration
    if resp.is_transient_error(): continue else: raise

4) Idempotency and request IDs- For any non-idempotent/side-effecting calls (payments, job creation, emails, tool calls) set an "Idempotency-Key" header (UUID v4). Server-side dedupe window is 24 hours by default for hosted endpoints; we can configure shorter windows for high-volume endpoints on Dedicated. We also log the client-supplied idempotency key in audit trails so you can correlate retries.

5) Embeddings bulk import guidance- Use batching sized to your RPC and CPU profile. Typical safe batch: 64-256 vectors per request depending on vector size; larger batches are more efficient but can hit mem/latency thresholds. When doing large imports, use concurrency throttling (e.g., 8 concurrent workers with per-worker backoff) to avoid bursting the account quota. We provide a bulk-import helper in our SDK (see sdk-snippets.zip) that coordinates worker pools and exponential backoff with jitter.

6) Routing & structured output- If latency is critical, route to regionally-located endpoints and model variants with lower compute (quantized) for routine responses. For structured output and tool-calling, use our function-schema enforcement; SDK offers a verify_schema() helper that rejects ill-formed responses locally to avoid repeated calls.

Attachments: sdk-snippets.zip (examples: idempotency header usage, backoff helper, bulk-embed helper), rate_limit_matrix.csv

If helpful I can paste a short Node/Python snippet during the call.

—Ava Chen
Integrations Engineer, Redwood Inference

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Sanjay Patel <sanjay.patel@finsigma.com>, Kate Liu <kate.liu@finsigma.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 03 Mar 2026 00:12:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Recap + next steps — SDK auth, routing, and rate-limit items

Thanks Sanjay — great questions and glad Wed at 15:00 PT works. Action items for us before the call:

- Finsigma: share test service account credentials (minimal privileges) and an example traffic pattern (CSV or simple script).
- Redwood: we will load a small POC with Embedding bulk-upsert using the SDK helper and exercise streaming + structured outputs. We'll run through: token-auth example, idempotency-key demonstration, and 429 + backoff behavior.

I'll book the invite and send a short agenda. If you can drop the test account in the Drive folder linked above before EOD Tue, Ava will run the POC and share logs ahead of the meeting.

Appreciate it — looking forward to the sync.

-Aditya
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
