Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 02 Mar 2026 20:35:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Recap + next steps — SDK auth, routing, and rate-limit items

Sanjay / Kate —

Thanks for the questions. I'm Ada (engineer on Integrations) — below are pragmatic recommendations you can implement in the POC and link back to for production. I'll paste a short pseudocode snippet for throttling/retries and call out headers we return.

1) Rate limits & quotas- We publish a rate-limit matrix (attached). We enforce both per-second (r/s) and per-minute (r/m) limits on the hosted SKU and per-customer quotas on Dedicated/Private. Typical hosted example: 50 r/s and 3000 r/m for medium tier; streaming endpoints have a concurrent-stream cap (default 10 concurrent streams per API key).
- Bursting: gateway allows short bursts; we track steady-state ROQ and enforce sliding-window counts.

2) Headers we return on throttled requests- Retry-After: seconds until next allowed request (if known)
- X-RateLimit-Limit: configured limit window size (e.g., 50)
- X-RateLimit-Remaining: tokens left in current window
- X-RateLimit-Reset: epoch seconds when window resets
- On 429 we also include a machine-readable body with reason and \"suggested_backoff_ms\" when applicable.

3) 429 handling recommendations- Always examine Retry-After when present. If absent, use exponential backoff with full jitter. Prefer capped backoff (max 60s) and an overall retry budget per operation (e.g., 6 attempts).

Suggested pseudocode (client-side)
- base = 200 ms
- max_backoff = 60000 ms
- for attempt in 1..max_attempts:
    sleep = min(max_backoff, base * 2^(attempt - 1))
    jitter = random(0, sleep)
    wait(jitter)
    resp = call_api()
    if resp.status == 200: return resp
    if resp.status == 429:
        if resp.headers['Retry-After']:
            wait_seconds = parse_retry_after(resp.headers['Retry-After'])
            wait(wait_seconds)
        else:
            continue // will backoff on next loop iteration
    if resp.is_transient_error(): continue else: raise

4) Idempotency and request IDs- For any non-idempotent/side-effecting calls (payments, job creation, emails, tool calls) set an \"Idempotency-Key\" header (UUID v4). Server-side dedupe window is 24 hours by default for hosted endpoints; we can configure shorter windows for high-volume endpoints on Dedicated. We also log the client-supplied idempotency key in audit trails so you can correlate retries.

5) Embeddings bulk import guidance- Use batching sized to your RPC and CPU profile. Typical safe batch: 64-256 vectors per request depending on vector size; larger batches are more efficient but can hit mem/latency thresholds. When doing large imports, use concurrency throttling (e.g., 8 concurrent workers with per-worker backoff) to avoid bursting the account quota. We provide a bulk-import helper in our SDK (see sdk-snippets.zip) that coordinates worker pools and exponential backoff with jitter.

6) Routing & structured output- If latency is critical, route to regionally-located endpoints and model variants with lower compute (quantized) for routine responses. For structured output and tool-calling, use our function-schema enforcement; SDK offers a verify_schema() helper that rejects ill-formed responses locally to avoid repeated calls.

Attachments: sdk-snippets.zip (examples: idempotency header usage, backoff helper, bulk-embed helper), rate_limit_matrix.csv

If helpful I can paste a short Node/Python snippet during the call.

—Ava Chen
Integrations Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Sanjay Patel <sanjay.patel@finsigma.com>, Kate Liu <kate.liu@finsigma.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 03 Mar 2026 00:12:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Recap + next steps — SDK auth, routing, and rate-limit items

Thanks Sanjay — great questions and glad Wed at 15:00 PT works. Action items for us before the call:

- Finsigma: share test service account credentials (minimal privileges) and an example traffic pattern (CSV or simple script).
- Redwood: we will load a small POC with Embedding bulk-upsert using the SDK helper and exercise streaming + structured outputs. We'll run through: token-auth example, idempotency-key demonstration, and 429 + backoff behavior.

I'll book the invite and send a short agenda. If you can drop the test account in the Drive folder linked above before EOD Tue, Ava will run the POC and share logs ahead of the meeting.

Appreciate it — looking forward to the sync.

-Aditya
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Offer band guidance + HC approval request — Ishaan Patel (Backend)
messages:
From: Elena Rossi <elena@zenrecruit.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 25, 2026 at 09:12 AM -0800
Subject: Candidate: Ishaan Patel — suggested band for Backend role

Hi Ava / Lauren,

Great chat yesterday — I wanted to follow up with Ishaan's background and the band we've been recommending based on market comps. Attached: Ishaan_Patel_Resume.pdf.

Quick summary:
- Senior backend engineer, ~6.5 years exp, strong infra + scale systems background.
- Current comp: $185k base + RSU tranche, open to manager-track conversations but prefers IC ladder.
- Recruiter market comp suggests L5 (Mid-Senior) at Redwood, with a narrow L4/L5 ambiguity depending on scope.

Can you confirm the target band and whether HC approval is required for a manager-track title at offer? We can move fast — candidate is talking to two other finalists.

Best,
Elena
ZenRecruit

Attachment: Ishaan_Patel_Resume.pdf

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 25, 2026 at 10:05 AM -0800
Subject: Fwd: Candidate: Ishaan Patel — suggested band for Backend role

Forwarding Elena's note — candidate looks strong. Quick context and asks:
- Hiring manager (Marcus) and I aligned that the role's scope currently maps to L5 expectations (ownership across subsystems, cross-team projects, mentoring).
- Candidate has manager-track interest; we need to decide whether to offer "Senior Engineer, Manager track" title (still individual contributor compensation band) or push for a people-manager hire.
- Headcount: this is a new hire on the Platform team (Requisition 9874). We currently have 2 open IC HC and 1 manager HC on the org plan; this req is approved for 1 IC.

Requests:
1) Marcus — confirm level (L5 vs L6) and any suggested title that helps future mobility.
2) Monica/People Ops — do we need a formal HC exception to target manager-track title or is the IC HC sufficient?
3) Tessa/Comp — if we want to make a manager-track offer without manager HC, what band/comp exceptions are allowable?

I've attached the comp band reference and our offer matrix for 9874 (offer-matrix-9874.xlsx, comp-band-reference-Q1-2026.pdf). Candidate timeline: hiring loop + final with Marcus this week; Elena can convert to verbal offer as soon as we align.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Director, Recruiting Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachments: comp-band-reference-Q1-2026.pdf, offer-matrix-9874.xlsx

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 26, 2026 at 08:22 AM -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Candidate: Ishaan Patel — suggested band for Backend role

Ava / team —

I interviewed Ishaan yesterday — he demonstrates L5 scope today. Key points: owns cross-service performance initiatives, designed an infra migration, and has some experience running small feature teams but hasn't been a direct manager. Given that, I recommend offering at L5 with a title that signals manager-track growth: "Senior Software Engineer (Manager track)".

Rationale:
- Keeps hiring within approved IC HC (no immediate manager HC needed).
- Signals career path for candidate; we can convert to an EM role later with a promotion/HC change.

Comp ask: if we offer L5, target base $195k (midpoint). If candidate pushes for manager-level comp, we should consider a small sign-on or elevated equity grant rather than shift to L6 band.

I don't see a reason to go to L6 at hire based on interviews.

Marcus
Staff Engineer, Platform
Redwood Inference

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 27, 2026 at 11:03 AM -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Candidate: Ishaan Patel — suggested band for Backend role

Agree with Marcus on level = L5. From a comp perspective:
- L5 base midpoint (Q1 2026 band): $190k–$200k.
- For manager-track signaling without manager HC, standard practice is: keep L5 base, add 5–10% sign-on OR 10–25% more in RSU grant depending on internal parity.
- We should avoid creating a special L5+ band; keep offer within banding guidance to preserve internal equity.

HC note: People Ops (Monica) — please confirm we don't need an HC exception to use a manager-tracking title (we've done this twice in the past year). If it requires a formal headcount change, candidate timing might slip.

Lauren
VP, Talent Acquisition
Redwood Inference

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 2, 2026 at 02:45 PM -0800
Subject: RE: Fwd: Candidate: Ishaan Patel — suggested band for Backend role

Thanks team — quick operational confirmation:
- No formal HC exception required to use a manager-track title so long as the hire remains on IC headcount. Title is an internal designation; HC type (IC vs Manager) is what matters for budget.
- Approve offering L5 base up to $200,000 + discretionary sign-on up to 7% (one-time cash) OR up to 20% additional RSUs depending on equity budget for the role.
- Please route the final offer package through People Ops (offers@redwoodinference.com) and include the signed comp approval worksheet.

Ava — once you get candidate acceptance, loop me and we'll schedule the onboarding + initial 90-day success plan touchpoints.

Monica Patel
Head of People Operations (Interim)
Redwood Inference

--

> ...

source.subject: Re: URGENT: Streaming responses hanging (partial outputs)
source.body:
From: Mira Desai <mira@latticeflow.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: ops@latticeflow.ai
Date: Fri, Feb 14, 2026 at 5:58 PM UTC
Subject: URGENT: Streaming responses hanging (partial outputs)

Hi Ava,
We’re seeing streamed chat completions hang part way through. The connection stays open but no more events arrive. Users get partial answers.

This started today. We’re on stream=true (SSE).
Example request_id: req_9e2c... around 18:12 UTC.

Can you escalate?

— Mira


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mira Desai <mira@latticeflow.ai>
Date: Fri, Feb 14, 2026 at 6:07 PM UTC
Subject: Re: URGENT: Streaming responses hanging (partial outputs)

Thanks Mira — escalating now. Can you confirm region + whether you’re going through any proxy (Cloudflare, ALB, etc.)?

I’m opening a support ticket so we can track it: SUP-1842.

— Ava
Customer Success
Redwood Inference


From: Mira Desai <mira@latticeflow.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 14, 2026 at 6:15 PM UTC
Subject: Re: URGENT: Streaming responses hanging (partial outputs)

Region is us-east. We’re using our own ALB in front of the client service but no special proxy for the Redwood call.

Sometimes it looks like it “freezes” ~20s in. No error.


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mira Desai <mira@latticeflow.ai>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 15, 2026 at 2:32 AM UTC
Subject: Re: URGENT: Streaming responses hanging (partial outputs)

Jumping in from Eng. We’re investigating gateway flushing for SSE.

Two quick asks:
1) Are you using HTTP/2 from the client?
2) Can you share if you see the TCP connection close at the client watchdog timeout, or does it remain open until you cancel?


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mira Desai <mira@latticeflow.ai>
Date: Sun, Feb 16, 2026 at 9:10 AM UTC
Subject: Re: URGENT: Streaming responses hanging (partial outputs)

Update: we rolled a mitigation in us-east that sends periodic keepalive comments on the stream to avoid intermediary buffering.

Can you retest and let us know if the hangs stop?


From: Mira Desai <mira@latticeflow.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Feb 16, 2026 at 9:21 AM UTC
Subject: Re: URGENT: Streaming responses hanging (partial outputs)

Retested across ~200 requests, looks significantly better. No more stuck streams so far. We’ll keep an eye on it. Thanks.

Subject: Re: URGENT: Streaming responses hanging (partial outputs)

Region is us-east. We’re using our own ALB in front of the client service but no special proxy for the Redwood call.

Sometimes it looks like it “freezes” ~20s in. No error.


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mira Desai <mira@latticeflow.ai>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 15, 2026 at 2:32 AM UTC
Subject: Re: URGENT: Streaming responses hanging (partial outputs)

Jumping in from Eng. We’re investigating gateway flushing for SSE.

Two quick asks:
1) Are you using HTTP/2 from the client?
2) Can you share if you see the TCP connection close at the client watchdog timeout, or does it remain open until you cancel?


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mira Desai <mira@latticeflow.ai>
Date: Sun, Feb 16, 2026 at 9:10 AM UTC
Subject: Re: URGENT: Streaming responses hanging (partial outputs)

Update: we rolled a mitigation in us-east that sends periodic keepalive comments on the stream to avoid intermediary buffering.

Can you retest and let us know if the hangs stop?


From: Mira Desai <mira@latticeflow.ai>

Subject: Re: URGENT: Streaming responses hanging (partial outputs)

source.subject: Dedicated capacity: commit sizing, throughput SLAs, and single-tenant compliance premium
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@coretech.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2026 09:15:00 -0800
Subject: Dedicated capacity follow-up — sizing and single-tenant pricing

Hi Jordan,

Great to run through the demo yesterday — appreciate the detailed product questions. Attached is a draft sizing worksheet (RTS_dedicated_sizing_v1.pdf) that maps estimated token volumes to GPU pool sizes and expected throughput. Highlights: 

- Baseline commit: 2 x A100-80GB equivalents (continuous), handles ~120 qps at 8k context with batching profile A.
- Burst pool: +1 GPU to absorb 2x short spikes (auto-burst).
- Throughput guarantee: 99th pct latency SLO target of 200 ms on the primary model + autoscaling policy.

A few open items for pricing / commercial packaging I want to confirm with you:
1) Preferred commit length (12 vs 36 months). Long commits unlock steeper discounts.
2) Whether you require single-tenant (single-tenant VPC + dedicated control plane) for compliance. We price a single-tenant isolation premium separately and it includes: dedicated audit logging, customer KMS integration, and isolation of metadata.
3) Any SLA add-ons (higher uptime / financial credits) you want baked in.

If you can share a preferred commit term and an expected peak qps window, I can run this back with the precise pricing by EOD Friday.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
+1 415-555-0103

Attachment: RTS_dedicated_sizing_v1.pdf (pdf, 420KB)
From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@coretech.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@coretech.ai>
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity follow-up — sizing and single-tenant pricing

Hi Ava,

Thanks — this is helpful. A couple clarifying Qs from procurement and infra:

- Commit length: we prefer 12 months initially but open to a 24-month term if the economics make sense.
- Throughput guarantees: can you break down what the 99th pct SLO means in terms of sustained qps capacity we should reserve? Our peak pattern is 300–450 qps for 15–30 minute windows, 3x daily.
- Single-tenant: yes, compliance requires VPC isolation + KMS integration. Also want to see proof of audit log retention and format (SOC2 + our auditors will want a snapshot).
- Pricing: can you show the breakdown: base commit vs single-tenant premium vs security/compliance add-ons? Procurement also asked about credits for onboarding (waived invoices) and expedited support (SLA credits).

Timeline: procurement needs an approved commercial term sheet by 2/22 to hit their Q1 budget cycle.

Jordan
VP Product, CoreTech.ai
jordan.lee@coretech.ai

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 08:42:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Dedicated capacity follow-up — sizing and single-tenant pricing

FYI — Jordan asked for a detailed commercial breakout and audit log evidence. Procurement deadline 2/22. Can you both review and propose a consumer-facing price sheet?

Forwarded message:
> From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@coretech.ai>
> Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2026 10:03:00 -0800
> To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
> Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity follow-up — sizing and single-tenant pricing
> 
> (see above)

Notes / ask from me:
- Neha: please enumerate 12m vs 24m commit pricing and show effective per-token/unit costs.
- Rishi: need a compliance bundle price — single-tenant premium + KMS integration + audit log retention (12 months) + SOC2 evidence snapshot. Also suggest any onboarding credits we can offer while protecting margin.

Target: return a single slide price table + a short bullet summary (3 options: conservative 12m, balanced 24m, aggressive 36m) by EOD Monday so I can loop Jordan before 2/22.

Thanks,
Ava
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 15 Feb 2026 11:47:00 -0800
Subject: Pricing proposal: CoreTech — commit options + compliance premium (draft)

Team — draft commercial structure attached (CoreTech_SOW_v3.pdf, KMS_Requirements.xlsx). High level: 

Option A — 12-month (conservative)
- Base reserved commit: 2 x A100-80GB equiv (continuous) + 1 burst GPU
- Monthly commit: $145,000
- Single-tenant isolation premium: +20% of base commit (one-time transition fee amortized over term)
- Security/compliance bundle (KMS + audit logging + SOC2 snapshot): $18,000 one-time
- Onboarding credit: $10,000 (applied first month)
- Throughput guarantee: target sustained 300 qps with 200 ms 99th pct under expected batching profile; higher SLAs available as add-on

Option B — 24-month (balanced)
- Monthly commit: $125,000 (effective discount for term)
- Single-tenant premium: +18%
- Security bundle: $15,000 one-time
- Onboarding credit: $15,000

Option C — 36-month (aggressive)
- Monthly commit: $105,000
- Single-tenant premium: +15%
- Security bundle: $12,000 one-time
- Onboarding credit: $25,000

Notes and rationale:
- Single-tenant premium covers dedicated control plane, isolated metadata store, per-customer audit logging retained 12 months, and integration effort for KMS/HSM (we assume standard KMS — HSM adds an uplift).
- We priced throughput to cover their 300–450 qps bursts via burst pool sizing; sustained baseline is sized for 120 qps to optimize cost.
- The premium percentages are negotiable; we can concede up to 3–5% for a 24m or 36m commit in exchange for accelerated payment or higher onboarding credit.

Requested internal approval:
- I recommend we offer Option B (24m) as our standard with a one-time discount of 5% on the single-tenant premium if they sign by 2/20.
- Approve $15k onboarding credit and allow me to provide a DocuSign term sheet.

Attachments: CoreTech_SOW_v3.pdf (pdf, 230KB); KMS_Requirements.xlsx (xlsx, 45KB)

-Neha
Director, Sales Strategy | Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwood.ai
From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@coretech.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Procurement Team <procurement@coretech.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Sun, 15 Feb 2026 17:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pricing proposal: CoreTech — commit options + compliance premium (draft)

Ava / team — thanks for the quick turnaround and clear options. We like Option B (24m) with the 5% concession on the single-tenant premium if you can confirm by 2/20. Procurement will need the SOW and a sample SOC2/audit-log extract for their auditors; can you attach the SOC2 snapshot and confirm KMS integration steps?

If you can send the finalized term sheet + DocuSign link with those attachments, we’ll aim to get procurement sign-off the week of 2/22.

Jordan
VP Product, CoreTech.ai
jordan.lee@coretech.ai
Attachment references: request SOC2 snapshot (image/pdf), DocuSign expected

source.subject: Re: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back
source.body:
From: Nina Shah <nina.shah@acme-example.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 9:43 AM
Subject: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back

Hi Ava,

We attempted the 2.8.0 -> 2.9.0 upgrade last night. Its been "rolling back" for hours and never completes. The UI now says "Rollback in progress" indefinitely.

Can you advise? We can share logs.

Nina

---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nina Shah <nina.shah@acme-example.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 10:01 AM
Subject: Re: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back

Thanks Nina  were looking. Can you attach the installer controller logs around the rollback start?

In the meantime: are you seeing any mention of a "rollback lock" or repeated rollback retries?

Ava

---

From: Nina Shah <nina.shah@acme-example.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 10:22 AM
Subject: Re: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back

Attached installer-controller-logs.txt. There are repeated lines like: "scheduling rollback retry" and it never clears.

---

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 10:48 AM
Subject: Re: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back

This matches the stuck rollback / rollback loop we saw (likely RRB-17). Workaround: delete CM `installer-rollback-lock` in the redwood-private namespace, then restart installer-controller. Id recommend we share steps with guardrails + ask them to confirm name/namespace before deleting.

---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nina Shah <nina.shah@acme-example.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 11:08 AM
Subject: Re: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back

Nina  we have a workaround that has unblocked this in our lab. If youre comfortable running a couple k8s commands, please do the following (and confirm youre in the *redwood-private* namespace):

1) `kubectl -n redwood-private delete cm installer-rollback-lock`
2) `kubectl -n redwood-private rollout restart deploy/installer-controller`

This should allow the rollback to complete. Please reply with the outcome + any remaining errors.

We are also tracking a patch to prevent the rollback loop from happening again.

Ava Chen
Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Team,

After yesterday’s near-miss (Optimize change nearly promoted without meeting gates), we need to tighten the override policy immediately.

Two goals:
1) Overrides remain possible for true emergencies (e.g., rollback unblockers), but
2) They cannot be a “convenience button” that bypasses quality and compliance.

Please reply today with:
- Who should be allowed to approve overrides (roles + named groups)
- Time bounds (how long an override can live)
- What must be recorded for audit (who/what/why + artifacts)
- Where enforcement lives (Optimize apply boundary vs rollout controller vs both)

We should also assume this will come up in enterprise security reviews. If our answer is “it’s logged somewhere,” that’s not sufficient.

Ava

—
Ava Chen
VP, Product & Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:46 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Proposed policy (MVP → tighten further at GA if needed):

Approvers:
- Only “Optimize Override Approver” role (not general admin), initially: Optimize oncall (rotation) + Optimize PM lead (me) as backup.
- For enterprise accounts, require a second approver (SRE oncall or Security) depending on reason code.

Time bounds:
- Default 24h expiration.
- Max 72h (requires escalation beyond oncall).

Required evidence (must be attached/linked in the override request):
- Gate run id + summary (pass/fail deltas + flake score)
- The exact config diff hash
- Reason code (prod incident, capacity, false positive/flaky suite, customer block)
- Ticket reference (INC/SUP/INT)

Audit:
- Emit audit events on: override requested, override approved/denied, override expired, override revoked.
- Include approver identity + RBAC role + timestamps.

Enforcement:
- Block at Optimize “apply config” boundary AND rollout controller should refuse canary promotion without gate=passed OR override=active.

If we align on this today, we can update PM-482 and coordinate RBAC via INT-3391.

Aditya

—
Aditya Rao
Product, Optimize


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 10:18 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

+1 on defense-in-depth.

Security requirements I’d like to see explicitly:

1) Two-person rule when the override would allow production traffic to run on a config that failed quality gates.
   - One approver can be Product (Optimize), second should be SRE or Security (depending on reason). This avoids “PM approves their own ship.”

2) No permanent overrides.
   - 24h default is good. I’d push max to 48h unless a VP signs off.

3) Audit trail must be exportable (not only UI).
   - Audit log event must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, baseline reference, gate_run_id(s), reason code, approver(s), expiry.
   - Kevin: please confirm we can route these events into the enterprise audit export / SIEM pipeline.

4) “Break-glass” access should be rare.
   - If we need an emergency knob (e.g., to restore service during an incident), it must be separate from the normal override path and auto-notify #incidents.

Marcus

—
Marcus Lin
Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 11:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

From SRE POV:

- Agree on two-person approval if the gate is failing.
- We should distinguish “override because gates are flaky/infra broken” vs “override because we accept regression.” Those have different blast radius.

Operational asks:
1) Any override must require a ticket id (INC- / SUP- / INT-). No ticket, no override.
2) Auto-expire and auto-revoke. No “forgotten override” risk.
3) Post to #incidents (or at least #eng-sre) when an override is granted for production routes.
4) Add a dashboard panel: active overrides by route/account + time to expiry.

Also, I’d rather we keep max duration short (48h) and require Ava/Priya for anything longer.

Sean

—
Sean Gallagher
SRE Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 12:24 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Couple implementation notes so we don’t end up with a policy that’s unenforceable:

- Enforcement should be in *both* places:
  (a) Optimize service blocks apply unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  (b) Rollout controller blocks promotion unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  This covers UI/API clients and any drift in rollout workflows.

- RBAC needs to be role-based, not “admins.” Let’s implement a dedicated role:
  QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER
  and keep membership narrow.

- Override object needs to include: scope (account/route/model), diff hash, expiry, reason code, ticket ref, and approver set.

- Please avoid “VP in the loop for every override” unless we can make it asynchronous.
  Suggestion: two-person rule (PM + SRE/Sec) for <=48h, VP escalation only for >48h.

Priya

—
Priya Natarajan
VP Engineering, Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 1:37 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Decision (effective immediately for Hosted API routes; extend to Dedicated as we roll out):

1) Two-person approval REQUIRED for any override that permits applying/promoting a config change when the quality gate is failing.
   - Approver A: Optimize PM oncall OR Optimize product lead (Aditya as backup)
   - Approver B: SRE oncall (primary) OR Security (Marcus delegate) if reason code is security/compliance-critical

2) Time bounds:
   - Default expiration: 24 hours
   - Maximum: 48 hours
   - >48 hours requires VP escalation (Priya or me) AND a written plan to resolve (e.g., fix flaky suite, adjust prompt coverage) + follow-up owner.

3) Required fields/evidence (no exceptions):
   - Ticket id (INC/SUP/INT). No ticket, no override.
   - Gate run id(s) + artifact links
   - Config diff hash + scope (account_id / route_id / model)
   - Reason code (choose from a fixed list)
   - Free-text justification (1–3 paragraphs) including risk assessment

4) Audit & notification:
   - Emit audit log events for request/approve/deny/expire/revoke.
   - Events must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, gate_run_id(s), reason code, both approvers, expiry timestamp.
   - Overrides granted for production must post to #incidents and be visible in Console audit view.
   - Kevin: ensure these events are included in audit export (enterprise ask).

5) Enforcement must be defense-in-depth:
   - Optimize apply boundary AND rollout controller promotion checks must enforce gate==passed OR override==active.

Talia: please capture this as an update in PM-482 / the Confluence policy doc and track the RBAC change in INT-3391.

Ava


From: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 2:41 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Captured. Action items I’m tracking:

- Update override policy doc + link decision section (PM-482)
- RBAC: create QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER role + limit membership (INT-3391)
- Console: add “Active overrides” view + expiry + ticket ids
- Observability: add metric + alert for active overrides > N hours remaining / unexpected spikes
- Incident comms: confirm #incidents posting format

I’ll send a short status update EOD with owners/dates.

Talia

—
Talia Benson
Program Manager
Redwood Inference

   - Events must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, gate_run_id(s), reason code, both approvers, expiry timestamp.
   - Overrides granted for production must post to #incidents and be visible in Console audit view.
   - Kevin: ensure these events are included in audit export (enterprise ask).

5) Enforcement must be defense-in-depth:
   - Optimize apply boundary AND rollout controller promotion checks must enforce gate==passed OR override==active.

Talia: please capture this as an update in PM-482 / the Confluence policy doc and track the RBAC change in INT-3391.

Ava


From: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 2:41 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Captured. Action items I’m tracking:

- Update override policy doc + link decision section (PM-482)
- RBAC: create QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER role + limit membership (INT-3391)
- Console: add “Active overrides” view + expiry + ticket ids
- Observability: add metric + alert for active overrides > N hours remaining / unexpected spikes
- Incident comms: confirm #incidents posting format

I’ll send a short status update EOD with owners/dates.

Talia

—
Talia Benson
Program Manager
Redwood Inference
text:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Team,

After yesterday’s near-miss (Optimize change nearly promoted without meeting gates), we need to tighten the override policy immediately.

Two goals:
1) Overrides remain possible for true emergencies (e.g., rollback unblockers), but
2) They cannot be a “convenience button” that bypasses quality and compliance.

Please reply today with:
- Who should be allowed to approve overrides (roles + named groups)
- Time bounds (how long an override can live)
- What must be recorded for audit (who/what/why + artifacts)
- Where enforcement lives (Optimize apply boundary vs rollout controller vs both)

We should also assume this will come up in enterprise security reviews. If our answer is “it’s logged somewhere,” that’s not sufficient.

Ava

—
Ava Chen
VP, Product & Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:46 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Proposed policy (MVP → tighten further at GA if needed):

Approvers:
- Only “Optimize Override Approver” role (not general admin), initially: Optimize oncall (rotation) + Optimize PM lead (me) as backup.
- For enterprise accounts, require a second approver (SRE oncall or Security) depending on reason code.

Time bounds:
- Default 24h expiration.
- Max 72h (requires escalation beyond oncall).

Required evidence (must be attached/linked in the override request):
- Gate run id + summary (pass/fail deltas + flake score)
- The exact config diff hash
- Reason code (prod incident, capacity, false positive/flaky suite, customer block)
- Ticket reference (INC/SUP/INT)

Audit:
- Emit audit events on: override requested, override approved/denied, override expired, override revoked.
- Include approver identity + RBAC role + timestamps.

Enforcement:
- Block at Optimize “apply config” boundary AND rollout controller should refuse canary promotion without gate=passed OR override=active.

If we align on this today, we can update PM-482 and coordinate RBAC via INT-3391.

Aditya

—
Aditya Rao
Product, Optimize


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 10:18 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

+1 on defense-in-depth.

Security requirements I’d like to see explicitly:

1) Two-person rule when the override would allow production traffic to run on a config that failed quality gates.
   - One approver can be Product (Optimize), second should be SRE or Security (depending on reason). This avoids “PM approves their own ship.”

2) No permanent overrides.
   - 24h default is good. I’d push max to 48h unless a VP signs off.

3) Audit trail must be exportable (not only UI).
   - Audit log event must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, baseline reference, gate_run_id(s), reason code, approver(s), expiry.
   - Kevin: please confirm we can route these events into the enterprise audit export / SIEM pipeline.

4) “Break-glass” access should be rare.
   - If we need an emergency knob (e.g., to restore service during an incident), it must be separate from the normal override path and auto-notify #incidents.

Marcus

—
Marcus Lin
Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 11:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

From SRE POV:

- Agree on two-person approval if the gate is failing.
- We should distinguish “override because gates are flaky/infra broken” vs “override because we accept regression.” Those have different blast radius.

Operational asks:
1) Any override must require a ticket id (INC- / SUP- / INT-). No ticket, no override.
2) Auto-expire and auto-revoke. No “forgotten override” risk.
3) Post to #incidents (or at least #eng-sre) when an override is granted for production routes.
4) Add a dashboard panel: active overrides by route/account + time to expiry.

Also, I’d rather we keep max duration short (48h) and require Ava/Priya for anything longer.

Sean

—
Sean Gallagher
SRE Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 12:24 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss)

Couple implementation notes so we don’t end up with a policy that’s unenforceable:

- Enforcement should be in *both* places:
  (a) Optimize service blocks apply unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  (b) Rollout controller blocks promotion unless (gate==passed) OR (override==active)
  This covers UI/API clients and any drift in rollout workflows.

- RBAC needs to be role-based, not “admins.” Let’s implement a dedicated role:
  QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER
  and keep membership narrow.

- Override object needs to include: scope (account/route/model), diff hash, expiry, reason code, ticket ref, and approver set.

- Please avoid “VP in the loop for every override” unless we can make it asynchronous.
  Suggestion: two-person rule (PM + SRE/Sec) for <=48h, VP escalation only for >48h.

Priya

—
Priya Natarajan
VP Engineering, Platform
Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 1:37 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Decision (effective immediately for Hosted API routes; extend to Dedicated as we roll out):

1) Two-person approval REQUIRED for any override that permits applying/promoting a config change when the quality gate is failing.
   - Approver A: Optimize PM oncall OR Optimize product lead (Aditya as backup)
   - Approver B: SRE oncall (primary) OR Security (Marcus delegate) if reason code is security/compliance-critical

2) Time bounds:
   - Default expiration: 24 hours
   - Maximum: 48 hours
   - >48 hours requires VP escalation (Priya or me) AND a written plan to resolve (e.g., fix flaky suite, adjust prompt coverage) + follow-up owner.

3) Required fields/evidence (no exceptions):
   - Ticket id (INC/SUP/INT). No ticket, no override.
   - Gate run id(s) + artifact links
   - Config diff hash + scope (account_id / route_id / model)
   - Reason code (choose from a fixed list)
   - Free-text justification (1–3 paragraphs) including risk assessment

4) Audit & notification:
   - Emit audit log events for request/approve/deny/expire/revoke.
   - Events must include: account_id, route_id, model_id, config_diff_hash, gate_run_id(s), reason code, both approvers, expiry timestamp.
   - Overrides granted for production must post to #incidents and be visible in Console audit view.
   - Kevin: ensure these events are included in audit export (enterprise ask).

5) Enforcement must be defense-in-depth:
   - Optimize apply boundary AND rollout controller promotion checks must enforce gate==passed OR override==active.

Talia: please capture this as an update in PM-482 / the Confluence policy doc and track the RBAC change in INT-3391.

Ava


From: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 2:41 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quality gates: override policy escalation (post near-miss) — decision

Captured. Action items I’m tracking:

- Update override policy doc + link decision section (PM-482)
- RBAC: create QUALITY_GATE_OVERRIDE_APPROVER role + limit membership (INT-3391)
- Console: add “Active overrides” view + expiry + ticket ids
- Observability: add metric + alert for active overrides > N hours remaining / unexpected spikes
- Incident comms: confirm #incidents posting format

I’ll send a short status update EOD with owners/dates.

Talia

—
Talia Benson
Program Manager
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Re: Urgent: streaming responses freeze mid-generation
source.body:
From: Nina Patel <nina.patel@arcadiahealth.example>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 5:08 PM
Subject: Urgent: streaming responses freeze mid-generation

Hi Ava,
Were seeing streaming responses freeze mid-generation. The connection stays open but no more tokens come in, and we never get a [DONE]. This is happening in production and our agents are getting stuck.

Attached is a curl -N sample where it stalls around ~400 tokens.

-Nina

---
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nina Patel <nina.patel@arcadiahealth.example>
Cc: Samir Desai <samir.desai@arcadiahealth.example>
Date: Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 5:22 PM
Subject: Re: Urgent: streaming responses freeze mid-generation

Thanks Nina  were pulling this into an escalation now (SUP-1842). Can you confirm if youre using the OpenAI-compatible streaming endpoint and whether any proxy/load balancer sits between you and us?

In the meantime: please set a client-side read timeout and retry with an idempotency key if possible.

Ava
Customer Success
Redwood Inference

---
From: Samir Desai <samir.desai@arcadiahealth.example>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 6:01 PM
Subject: Re: Urgent: streaming responses freeze mid-generation

Yes using the OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions with stream=true. No proxy on our side (direct from our VPC egress). Seems worse during peak hours.

---
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 23, 2026 at 9:44 AM
Subject: Re: Urgent: streaming responses freeze mid-generation

I can reproduce under load when gateway gzip is enabled for SSE. Were working on disabling gzip for text/event-stream and forcing flush/heartbeat. Will update ETA shortly.

---
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nina Patel <nina.patel@arcadiahealth.example>
Date: Mon, Jan 26, 2026 at 4:18 PM
Subject: Re: Urgent: streaming responses freeze mid-generation

We deployed a gateway change in us-east dedicated: gzip disabled for SSE + periodic heartbeat flush. Can you rerun your load test and let us know if you still see freezes?

---
From: Nina Patel <nina.patel@arcadiahealth.example>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 27, 2026 at 10:41 AM
Subject: Re: Urgent: streaming responses freeze mid-generation

We ran ~500 streaming requests this morning and didnt see any stalls. Looks much better. Well keep an eye on it but this unblocks us. Thanks!

source.subject: GA go/no-go: Safe apply + one-click rollback (optimization config) — approvals + checklist
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: GA go/no-go: Safe apply + one-click rollback (optimization config) — approvals + checklist

Team — aiming to make a go/no-go call tomorrow (Fri) for LIMITED GA enablement of the optimization config safe apply + one-click rollback feature flag (Hosted + Dedicated only; Private docs/installer shipped but flag remains customer-controlled).

Requesting explicit approvals from: Product (Marissa), Release Eng (Connor), Security (Aisha) to enable the feature flag for the initial allowlist.

Proposed rollout scope (LIMITED GA):
- Enable in prod for allowlisted tenants only (initial: internal dogfood + 3 design partners)
- Canary required by default for any apply that touches >1 region or changes quantization profile
- Manual rollback only (one-click rollback in Console/API; no auto-rollback for GA)

Go/No-Go checklist (current status as of 09:00 PT):

1) Core functionality
- [DONE] Versioned Optimization Config objects (immutable versions + metadata) (PR-19834, PR-19888)
- [DONE] Diff view + semantic validation (PR-19857)
- [DONE] Rollback API endpoint + authz behavior (PR-19902)
- [DONE] Canary rollout controller + pause/resume hooks (PR-19991)

2) Safety + correctness
- [DONE] Runtime apply transaction checkpoints (PR-19915)
- [DONE] Idempotent applier + retry safety (PR-19918)
- [DONE] Fix apply/rollback race leading to mixed worker state (PR-20111) — merged, needs final production verification
- [DONE] Gate hooks (5xx + p95/p99) (PR-19933) — default thresholds set; overrides RBAC’d

3) Observability + on-call readiness
- [DONE] Rollout Health dashboard panels + annotations (PR-20012)
- [DONE] Runbook drafted + gameday completed (Drive: rollback-gameday-plan; results summarized in checklist)
- [OPEN] SRE sign-off not required for this thread, but I have confirmation from on-call lead that runbook is “usable as-is” for limited GA

4) Security / compliance
- [DONE] Audit log events for create/validate/apply/promote/rollback (PR-19941)
- [DONE] RBAC policy bundle update for “optimization change operator” (PR-20070)
- [OPEN] Confirm audit event payload includes request_id + actor + scope + config_version + prior_version for rollback (Aisha: can you confirm this meets launch bar?)
- [NOTE] We had SUP-18544 (“missing rollback event”) previously — fixed in PR-19941; should be covered by e2e + manual validation

5) Console / UX
- [DONE] Console backend endpoints (PR-19952, PR-19960)
- [DONE] Confirmation modal includes blast radius summary + type-to-confirm for multi-region (per UX notes)
- [OPEN] Copy nit: modal uses “Revert” vs “Rollback” in one spot — low risk; can patch next week

6) Release readiness
- [OPEN] Release train sequencing: runtime + control plane + console already in prod; feature flag remains off. Need Connor to confirm no remaining backports needed.

Attachments:
- SafeApplyRollback_GA_GoNoGo_Checklist_v6.pdf
- RolloutHealthDashboard_Screenshots_2026-01-22.png

If approved, plan is:
- Fri 1/23 15:00 PT: enable for internal dogfood only (1 tenant)
- Mon 1/26: expand allowlist to 3 design partners (Hosted + Dedicated)
- Two-week observation window; then re-evaluate for broader GA

Please reply with: APPROVE / HOLD and any conditions.

— Ava


On Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 10:01 AM Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 10:01 AM PST
Subject: Re: GA go/no-go: Safe apply + one-click rollback (optimization config) — approvals + checklist

Release Eng view:
- Runtime/control-plane/console bits you listed are already on the Jan train and deployed to prod earlier this week.
- The only “must-have” for limited GA was the apply/rollback race fix (PR-20111) and its regression test — I see it merged + green.
- No pending backports for the flag enablement path.

Condition from my side:
- We gate Friday’s internal enablement behind a 30–60 min watch window with dashboard + synthetic load (canary job) running.
- If rollback is used, we capture timestamps + config_version in the release note for Monday.

I’m comfortable with LIMITED GA as proposed.

APPROVE (Release Eng) pending Security sign-off on audit payload.

— Connor
Release Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


On Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 11:26 AM Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 11:26 AM PST
Subject: Re: GA go/no-go: Safe apply + one-click rollback (optimization config) — approvals + checklist

Reviewed the audit event examples in the checklist + compared to our required fields.

Minimum required for launch (for enterprise auditability):
- actor (user_id + email) and actor_type
- action (apply/promote/rollback)
- request_id / correlation_id
- scope (tenant_id, model, region) + blast_radius classification if present
- from_version and to_version (rollback must include both)
- result (success/failure) + failure_reason
- timestamp in UTC

PR-19941 appears to include all of these. Please ensure:
1) rollback events include prior_active_version (not just “target_version”)
2) we’re not dropping events on retries (idempotency should still emit exactly-once externally; at minimum at-least-once with stable event_id)

If those two are true in prod, Security is OK with limited GA.

APPROVE with condition: Ava to confirm rollback audit payload in prod (one manual test on internal tenant) before enabling for external design partners.

source.subject: Exec update: Serving runtime tail latency investment (KV cache compaction) — timeline + risk management
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 9:08 AM PST
Subject: Exec update: Serving runtime tail latency investment (KV cache compaction) — timeline + risk management

Team — sharing a quick exec update on the serving-runtime tail latency workstream (KV cache compaction + tier-aware eviction) since it’s now gating both SLO confidence and a couple of late-stage conversations.

Why this matters (business + technical):
- SLO / reliability: We’re seeing burst-driven p95/p99 spikes in sustained decode workloads with mixed sequence lengths, especially when request churn leaves the KV arena fragmented. In the worst case it creates opportunistic evictions at bad times and can tip us into OOM / request shedding.
- Cost: Today we’re compensating by leaving more headroom than we want (lower effective utilization). If we can keep KV memory contiguous/predictable under load, we should need less “safety padding” in dedicated pools and can be more aggressive on hosted concurrency without tail regressions.
- Customer pressure: This pattern shows up repeatedly in support and in enterprise eval questions (see SUP-28411, SUP-28433). PM has asked for a credible plan and crisp risk story.

What we’re doing:
- Allocator + fragmentation measurement: improve page/block layout + free-list management; add a fragmentation ratio metric that correlates with p99 spikes.
- Budgeted compaction: incremental compaction with an explicit time/bytes budget tied to scheduler boundaries (prefill/decode transitions) to avoid decode stalls.
- Tier-aware eviction: last-resort pressure relief that is safe + observable; interacts cleanly with pinned sessions.
- Guardrails: feature flag, kill-switch, backpressure integration, and rollout policy that auto-rolls back on tail regression.

Timeline (targeting runtime-1.22 train):
- Jan 20–31: discovery/measurement + final design/ADRs (in review now)
- Feb 3–21: implementation (allocator changes + scheduler hook + metrics + initial eviction policy)
- Feb 24–Mar 7: validation (benchmark lab + perf canary + stress/conformance)
- Mar 10+: staged rollout (internal canary → hosted subset → dedicated pools)

Risk management (what I want exec eyes on):
- Primary risk is introducing copy overhead / stalls during compaction. Mitigation is strict budgeting and scheduler-phase enforcement.
- Secondary risk is correctness edge cases (cancelled requests mid-relocation, pinned sessions). Mitigation is stress tests + conservative stopping conditions + kill-switch.
- We will hold rollout if: p99 up > agreed threshold, eviction rate spikes, or compaction time/bytes moved exceeds budget in steady state.

Asks:
1) Mateo/Sean: please confirm you’re aligned on “stop the line” thresholds + who has authority to flip the kill-switch during a canary.
2) Connor/Talia: I’d like this treated as a first-class item on the runtime-1.22 release readiness checklist.
3) Starting next week: weekly status note to this thread (wins, risks, current metrics, next gates). Keep it short.

Attachments:
- KV_Compaction_Review_Slides_v3.pdf
- Runtime_1.22_Rollout_Guardrails_Summary.docx

Ava Chen
VP, Product & Platform
Redwood Inference


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 10:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec update: Serving runtime tail latency investment (KV cache compaction) — timeline + risk management

Aligned.

A couple notes to help frame risk:
- We are not doing “stop-the-world” defragmentation. Compaction is incremental with a hard budget (bytes moved + wall time) and can no-op if we’re in a latency-sensitive phase.
- Eviction is a pressure valve, not the primary mechanism. We expect it to be near-zero in steady state; if eviction is non-trivial, that’s a signal we should treat as capacity/traffic policy rather than “normal.”
- Omar + Sofia are owning correctness + scheduler integration; Noah is doing architecture/correctness review on the invariants.

Re: timeline, I think Feb implementation window is realistic provided we keep scope to allocator + minimal copy/pack kernels.

Mateo Alvarez
VP Runtime
Redwood Inference


---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 11:11 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec update: Serving runtime tail latency investment (KV cache compaction) — timeline + risk management

+1.

On the guardrails / authority question:

title: Exec update: Serving runtime tail latency investment (KV cache compaction) — timeline + risk management
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 9:08 AM PST
Subject: Exec update: Serving runtime tail latency investment (KV cache compaction) — timeline + risk management

Team — sharing a quick exec update on the serving-runtime tail latency workstream (KV cache compaction + tier-aware eviction) since it’s now gating both SLO confidence and a couple of late-stage conversations.

Why this matters (business + technical):
- SLO / reliability: We’re seeing burst-driven p95/p99 spikes in sustained decode workloads with mixed sequence lengths, especially when request churn leaves the KV arena fragmented. In the worst case it creates opportunistic evictions at bad times and can tip us into OOM / request shedding.
- Cost: Today we’re compensating by leaving more headroom than we want (lower effective utilization). If we can keep KV memory contiguous/predictable under load, we should need less “safety padding” in dedicated pools and can be more aggressive on hosted concurrency without tail regressions.
- Customer pressure: This pattern shows up repeatedly in support and in enterprise eval questions (see SUP-28411, SUP-28433). PM has asked for a credible plan and crisp risk story.

What we’re doing:
- Allocator + fragmentation measurement: improve page/block layout + free-list management; add a fragmentation ratio metric that correlates with p99 spikes.
- Budgeted compaction: incremental compaction with an explicit time/bytes budget tied to scheduler boundaries (prefill/decode transitions) to avoid decode stalls.
- Tier-aware eviction: last-resort pressure relief that is safe + observable; interacts cleanly with pinned sessions.
- Guardrails: feature flag, kill-switch, backpressure integration, and rollout policy that auto-rolls back on tail regression.

Timeline (targeting runtime-1.22 train):
- Jan 20–31: discovery/measurement + final design/ADRs (in review now)
- Feb 3–21: implementation (allocator changes + scheduler hook + metrics + initial eviction policy)
- Feb 24–Mar 7: validation (benchmark lab + perf canary + stress/conformance)
- Mar 10+: staged rollout (internal canary → hosted subset → dedicated pools)

Risk management (what I want exec eyes on):
- Primary risk is introducing copy overhead / stalls during compaction. Mitigation is strict budgeting and scheduler-phase enforcement.
- Secondary risk is correctness edge cases (cancelled requests mid-relocation, pinned sessions). Mitigation is stress tests + conservative stopping conditions + kill-switch.
- We will hold rollout if: p99 up > agreed threshold, eviction rate spikes, or compaction time/bytes moved exceeds budget in steady state.

Asks:
1) Mateo/Sean: please confirm you’re aligned on “stop the line” thresholds + who has authority to flip the kill-switch during a canary.
2) Connor/Talia: I’d like this treated as a first-class item on the runtime-1.22 release readiness checklist.
3) Starting next week: weekly status note to this thread (wins, risks, current metrics, next gates). Keep it short.

Attachments:
- KV_Compaction_Review_Slides_v3.pdf
- Runtime_1.22_Rollout_Guardrails_Summary.docx

Ava Chen
VP, Product & Platform
Redwood Inference


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 10:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec update: Serving runtime tail latency investment (KV cache compaction) — timeline + risk management

Aligned.

A couple notes to help frame risk:
- We are not doing “stop-the-world” defragmentation. Compaction is incremental with a hard budget (bytes moved + wall time) and can no-op if we’re in a latency-sensitive phase.
- Eviction is a pressure valve, not the primary mechanism. We expect it to be near-zero in steady state; if eviction is non-trivial, that’s a signal we should treat as capacity/traffic policy rather than “normal.”
- Omar + Sofia are owning correctness + scheduler integration; Noah is doing architecture/correctness review on the invariants.

Re: timeline, I think Feb implementation window is realistic provided we keep scope to allocator + minimal copy/pack kernels.

Mateo Alvarez
VP Runtime
Redwood Inference


---

source.subject: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)
Attachments: 2026-01-15-controlled-failover-metrics-snapshot.pdf, gameday-action-items-2026-01-16.csv

Team — sharing a concise leadership readout from Thursday’s controlled regional failover (us-east → eu-west) and what we’re doing next.

TL;DR
- The core automation path worked end-to-end (signal → controller decision → routing policy flip → traffic shift → stabilization).
- Customer impact was limited but not zero: brief elevated 5xx/timeouts for a small slice of Hosted traffic + streaming disconnects for some clients that don’t handle redirects cleanly.
- We found several “must-fix before wider rollout” items (anti-flap tuning, dashboard annotations, streaming reconnect guidance, and capacity headroom guardrails).

What we tested
- Scenario: planned impairment simulation in us-east and controlled failover to eu-west for a scoped set of routes/models and a limited tenant allowlist.
- Objective: validate (1) detection + decisioning speed, (2) safe automation/rollback behavior, (3) observability + on-call usability, and (4) Dedicated protection (no unexpected pool drain).

Results against success criteria (high level)
- Detection + failover time:
  - First corroborated impairment signal observed: ~T+75s from impairment start.
  - Policy flip executed: ~T+2m40s.
  - Traffic settled in eu-west: ~T+4–5m (client retry/backoff dominated tail).
- Error/latency impact:
  - Hosted API: short spike in 5xx/timeouts during the shift window; elevated tail latency in eu-west for ~12–18 minutes as retries + warm-up converged.
  - Dedicated: no unplanned customer pool drain; no sustained SLO-relevant impact observed.
- Safety/guardrails:
  - No uncontrolled oscillation, but we did see an automated rollback trigger earlier than intended due to hold-down/threshold interaction (details below).

Customer impact / support signals
- One customer reported transient 5xx/timeouts during the planned window (SUP-25310).
- Streaming disconnects were reported by a couple customers whose clients did not gracefully reconnect on redirect (SUP-25322).
- Follow-on ticket on elevated tail latency in eu-west immediately post-shift (SUP-25341) — correlates with headroom + retry storm.

What went well
- On-call coordination and go/no-go discipline were strong (checklist completion and rollback readiness were clear).
- Region health aggregation + policy engine evaluation produced explainable decisions (we could point to which signals tripped).
- Dedicated isolation worked as expected (Hosted shift did not inadvertently trigger Dedicated drain behaviors).

Issues found (key)
1) Observability gap: dashboards lacked consistent automated annotations for: impairment start, policy flip, rollback evaluation points. This made timeline reconstruction slower than it should be.
2) Anti-flap / rollback tuning: hold-down timer and corroboration windows were too aggressive in one segment, resulting in an early rollback evaluation while the region was still stabilizing.
3) Tail latency in eu-west after shift: headroom assumptions were optimistic for the specific routes/models in scope; combined with client retry behavior this caused a brief tail spike.
4) Streaming client behavior: redirect/reconnect handling still causes user-visible disconnects for some SDK versions / client patterns.

Decisions / next steps
- We are NOT expanding automation scope until the above items are closed and we pass a smaller-blast-radius rehearsal.
- We will schedule Game Day #2 with tighter allowlist + synthetic load shaping, and explicitly measure: failover time, retry amplification, and “time to steady-state p99”.

Action items (short list; full tracker linked)
- Add automation-driven dashboard annotations + incident timeline hooks (Owner: Sean / Observability; target: end of Jan)
- Retune rollback/hold-down thresholds and add “stabilization grace” logic (Owner: Rafael + Routing; target: end of Jan)
- Capacity guardrail: require minimum eu-west headroom for routes in allowlist before executing flip (Owner: Irene + Fleet; target: early Feb)
- Streaming: finalize redirect-safe reconnect behavior + publish SDK guidance (Owner: Priya + Runtime/Gateway; target: early Feb)
- Comms: align Support macros + status-page language for planned tests (Owner: Sean; target: before next test)

Links
- Results page (metrics + timeline): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ONCALL/Controlled+Failover+Test+Results+-+2026-01-15
- Draft postmortem (focus on regressions + prevention): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ONCALL/Postmortem%3A+Controlled+Failover+Test+Regression
- Action item tracker (ENG-2426): https://linear.app/redwood/issue/ENG-2426/post-gameday-action-items

Asks
1) Approval to hold Game Day #2 in early Feb (exact date TBD) once the above gating items are closed.
2) Confirmation that we should proactively notify the same set of enterprise accounts as last time (Support has the list), or expand/contract based on risk appetite.

Thanks to everyone who helped make the test controlled and safe — we learned the right things without a large blast radius.

— Ava
Ava Chen
Infrastructure Reliability
Redwood Inference


On Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:03 AM Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)

This is the right framing.

Two adds:
- Let’s treat “dashboard annotations” as a hard gate for the next test. We lost time during the live window correlating graphs to events.
- Support comms: I’d prefer we keep proactive notifications limited to the same cohort (largest Hosted + Dedicated accounts + anyone with data residency questions) until streaming reconnect guidance is fully out.

I’ll sync with Rafael on gating criteria and get a proposed Feb window on the calendar once ENG-2426 is green.

— Sean
Sean Gallagher
Director, Site Reliability Engineering
Redwood Inference


On Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:26 AM Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:26 AM PT
Subject: Re: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)

Thanks, Ava.

A couple questions before I approve another production test window:
1) Were there any indications we violated (or came close to violating) the multi-region availability SLO budget during the test window?
2) What is our plan to ensure the next test doesn’t create a retry amplification event in the target region? (I want this explicitly called out in go/no-go.)

If we can answer those clearly and keep blast radius limited, I’m supportive of early Feb.

— Neha
Neha Kapoor
VP, Infrastructure & Reliability
Redwood Inference


On Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 12:05 PM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 12:05 PM PT
Subject: Re: Post-game day summary + follow-ups (Controlled regional failover us-east → eu-west, 1/15)

Answers inline.

1) SLO/error budget proximity
- We did not materially burn the multi-region availability SLO budget for the month.
- The spike was brief and scoped; the rolling burn-rate alerts triggered as expected, but did not cross paging thresholds for sustained windows.
- We’ll include the exact numbers in the postmortem once the annotation gap is fixed (so we can line up the windows precisely), but this was within the planned “acceptable test impact” bounds.

2) Preventing retry amplification in the target region (explicit go/no-go)
We’re adding three concrete gates before we run Game Day #2:
- Capacity/headroom gate: require minimum eu-west headroom for the exact route/model allowlist (not just aggregate regional headroom). Irene’s team is helping define thresholds.
- Gateway/backoff gate: validate retry budgets and Retry-After behavior in staging under redirect (Priya’s team + Peter’s circuit breaker changes), with a target “max retry amplification” metric.
- Synthetic load rehearsal: replay a shaped load pattern with perf canaries enabled and confirm p99 stabilization time is within target before touching prod.

If those three are green, we’ll propose an early Feb date with an even smaller allowlist than 1/15.

I’ll also make sure the go/no-go doc includes an explicit rollback trigger tied to retry amplification (not just error burn).

— Ava
Ava Chen
Infrastructure Reliability
Redwood Inference
