source.subject: Pricing Exhibit: guardband mechanics and packaging handoff - redline proposal
messages:
From: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@stratumio.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Rao <procurement@stratumio.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Sep 2025 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Pricing exhibit redline & packaging Qs

Hi Ava —

Thanks again for walking us through the demo. Procurement has run a first pass on the pricing exhibit and returned a few redlines that block internal approval. Their two core asks are: 1) clearer definition of the "guardband" / usage floor mechanics so it doesn't trigger unexpectedly during spikes, and 2) clearer language on how partner/marketplace discounts flow through to committed usage calculations.

I've attached the PDF with their markups (StratumIO_PricingExhibit_v3_redline.pdf) and a short table with example math. Can your team confirm:

- whether we can add a 90-day smoothing period for usage spikes before a floor true-up is assessed, and
- whether marketplace partner discounts are applied pre- or post-commit accounting.

Quick call would help — propose Tue 9/16 10am PT?

Thanks,
Jordan
StratumIO — Sr. Procurement Manager
[Attachment: StratumIO_PricingExhibit_v3_redline.pdf (PDF, 243KB)]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@stratumio.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 11 Sep 2025 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pricing exhibit redline & packaging Qs

Jordan —

Appreciate the detailed notes and the example table. We can do a 90-day smoothing window for true-ups, but legal wants that expressed as a "smoothing period" with an explicit maximum adjustment per quarter to avoid retroactive large reconciliations. Suggested language from our side:

- "Smoothing period: Utilization variance will be assessed over a rolling 90-day window. True-up adjustments will not exceed 25% of the average quarterly committed volume in any single reconciliation."

On marketplace discounts: we typically apply partner discounts at the invoice line (post-rate) but count committed-equivalent usage on a pre-discount basis for the purposes of caps/floors so both parties have predictable commitments. That addresses revenue recognition complexity for buyers and avoids double-counting discounts.

Cc'ing Ben/Marissa/Karthik to review potential commercial concessions (credits/buffer) if Stratum needs a lower up-front guard. Also propose we capture the final guardband formula as an explicit table in the Order Schedule to avoid interpretation drift.

Happy to take the Tue 10am slot if that still works.

Best,
Ava Chen
Director, Sales — Redwood Inference
[Signature]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 12 Sep 2025 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: internal: proposed commercial concession for Stratum (guardband + packaging)

Team —

Read Jordan's notes and Ava's response. Commercially I can offer a one-quarter waiver of the floor for the first billing quarter (i.e., no floor true-up applied for Q1 post-commit), plus a temporary 10% onboarding credit to offset migration variance. This keeps revenue expectation but reduces the customer's risk during ramp.

Key tradeoffs:
- Waiver delays realization of committed revenue (internal impact on ARR smoothing).
- Credits should be capped and expire after 90 days.

If legal agrees to Ava's "25% cap on adjustments per reconciliation" language, this combo should be acceptable. I've attached a short spreadsheet with the concession math and an illustration of the invoice lines.

-Ben
Sr. Revenue Manager, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: OrderSchedule_Summary_Table.xlsx (XLSX, 18KB)]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 11:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: internal: proposed commercial concession for Stratum (guardband + packaging)

All —

Legal edit suggestions inline to avoid ambiguity. Major items:

1) Define guardband trigger event explicitly: "Guardband triggers when sustained utilization falls below 60% of committed monthly utilization measured over a 90-day rolling window (i.e., three consecutive 30-day periods)."
2) Add an annual true-up cap: "Annual reconciliation adjustments related to guardband true-ups shall not reduce billed amounts by more than 40% of the annual committed amount in any 12-month period."
3) Packaging/discount flow: "Marketplace partner discounts shall be applied at invoice issuance; however, for the purpose of measuring compliance with committed usage, usage shall be measured on a gross basis (pre-discount)."

Marissa suggests replacing the term "smoothing period" with "rolling window" to match standard contract language. Laura — please review the annual cap number; I set 40% as a negotiation anchor but open to lowering.

I'll convert these points to tracked changes in the attached PDF and push back if procurement asks for a full removal of guardband.

-Marissa
Head of Legal Operations, Redwood Inference

From: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@stratumio.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Rao <procurement@stratumio.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Sep 2025 16:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pricing exhibit redline & packaging Qs — call notes and next steps

Ava/Marissa/Ben —

Thanks for the call earlier. Quick recap and next steps from Stratum:

- The 90-day rolling window + 25% per-reconciliation cap (as described by Ava) is a workable starting point for our CFO, but they asked to replace the 25% cap with a graduated matrix: 0–30 days = 0% adjustment, 31–90 days = up to 25%, >90 days = up to 40% (they want stronger protection for multi-quarter underutilization).
- On discounts, confirmed Stratum prefers counting committed usage on a net basis (post-discount) because partner rebates flow through the billing provider and affect cash flow. They asked if Redwood can run a middle-ground: count pre-discount for the cap/floor math but offer a quarterly reconciliation credit equal to the delta between pre- and post-discount accounting up to a capped amount.

Action items we agreed on:

2) Stratum to circulate the revised redline to finance and return final position by 9/25.
3) Redwood to prepare a short one-page example worksheet that shows how pre/post discount counting and the quarterly delta-credit would work in practice.

Jordan — please confirm receipt and I will roll these edits into the DocuSign test envelope once we lock the language.

Best,

title: Pricing Exhibit: guardband mechanics and packaging handoff - redline proposal
preview:
From: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@stratumio.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Rao <procurement@stratumio.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Sep 2025 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Pricing exhibit redline & packaging Qs

Hi Ava —

Thanks again for walking us through the demo. Procurement has run a first pass on the pricing exhibit and returned a few redlines that block internal approval. Their two core asks are: 1) clearer definition of the \"guardband\" / usage floor mechanics so it doesn't trigger unexpectedly during spikes, and 2) clearer language on how partner/marketplace discounts flow through to committed usage calculations.

I've attached the PDF with their markups (StratumIO_PricingExhibit_v3_redline.pdf) and a short table with example math. Can your team confirm:

- whether we can add a 90-day smoothing period for usage spikes before a floor true-up is assessed, and
- whether marketplace partner discounts are applied pre- or post-commit accounting.

Quick call would help — propose Tue 9/16 10am PT?

Thanks,
Jordan
StratumIO — Sr. Procurement Manager
[Attachment: StratumIO_PricingExhibit_v3_redline.pdf (PDF, 243KB)]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@stratumio.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 11 Sep 2025 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pricing exhibit redline & packaging Qs

Jordan —

Appreciate the detailed notes and the example table. We can do a 90-day smoothing window for true-ups, but legal wants that expressed as a \"smoothing period\" with an explicit maximum adjustment per quarter to avoid retroactive large reconciliations. Suggested language from our side:

- \"Smoothing period: Utilization variance will be assessed over a rolling 90-day window. True-up adjustments will not exceed 25% of the average quarterly committed volume in any single reconciliation.\"

On marketplace discounts: we typically apply partner discounts at the invoice line (post-rate) but count committed-equivalent usage on a pre-discount basis for the purposes of caps/floors so both parties have predictable commitments. That addresses revenue recognition complexity for buyers and avoids double-counting discounts.

Cc'ing Ben/Marissa/Karthik to review potential commercial concessions (credits/buffer) if Stratum needs a lower up-front guard. Also propose we capture the final guardband formula as an explicit table in the Order Schedule to avoid interpretation drift.

Happy to take the Tue 10am slot if that still works.

Best,
Ava Chen
Director, Sales — Redwood Inference
[Signature]


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 12 Sep 2025 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: internal: proposed commercial concession for Stratum (guardband + packaging)

Team —

Read Jordan's notes and Ava's response. Commercially I can offer a one-quarter waiver of the floor for the first billing quarter (i.e., no floor true-up applied for Q1 post-commit), plus a temporary 10% onboarding credit to offset migration variance. This keeps revenue expectation but reduces the customer's risk during ramp.

Key tradeoffs:
- Waiver delays realization of committed revenue (internal impact on ARR smoothing).
- Credits should be capped and expire after 90 days.

If legal agrees to Ava's \"25% cap on adjustments per reconciliation\" language, this combo should be acceptable. I've attached a short spreadsheet with the concession math and an illustration of the invoice lines.

-Ben
Sr. Revenue Manager, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: OrderSchedule_Summary_Table.xlsx (XLSX, 18KB)]

source.subject: SLA uplift request & monthly credit reconciliation
messages:
From: Emily Russo <erusso@peregrineanalytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Sep 2025 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Initial ask: uptime target and monthly credit calc

Hi Ava / Marissa,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — Peregrine is excited about Redwood's latency profile for our inference-heavy pipelines. Before we move into drafting an amendment, procurement asked for two clarifications that will affect the commercial ask:

1) Uptime target: we need a firm commitment for the hosted tier at 99.95% (monthly availability) given our SLA with downstream customers. Is that achievable for your hosted plan or would that require Dedicated capacity?

2) Monthly credit logic: we want the credit calculation to scale proportionally across partial-month degradations rather than a binary trigger. I attached a brief spreadsheet with our expected call volumes and a reference formula. Can Redwood propose a simple table that maps availability bands to credit percentages (monthly credit as % of invoice)?

If possible, could you share a draft amendment and a worked example showing how credits would be calculated for a 3% degradation in p95 latency vs a 0.2% uptime shortfall? Procurement will need the exact wording for their internal approval.

Thanks — Emily
Emily Russo
Head of Procurement, Peregrine Analytics
Attachments: Credit_Calc_Sheet.xlsx
From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Emily Russo <erusso@peregrineanalytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Sep 2025 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: uptime target and monthly credit calc

Hi Emily — thanks for the pragmatic notes and for the spreadsheet, that helped. Quick answers up front:

- Hosted tier: our standard hosted SLA is 99.9% availability. 99.95% is above our standard hosted commitment; we can either (a) offer a dedicated capacity commitment at 99.95% under a reserved contract, or (b) for hosted, we can propose a 99.92% uplift with a small operational exception window and a higher credit band if you prefer to remain on hosted.

- Monthly credit logic: we typically use banded crediting (e.g., >=99.9% -> 0; 99.5–99.9% -> X%; <99.5% -> Y%). However, I can translate that into a proportional formula for your procurement team — the attached draft shows both banded and proportional examples and two worked scenarios (p95 latency regression vs uptime shortfall).

I attached an initial amendment (SLA_Amendment_Peregrine_v0.1.docx) and the credit calc we discussed. Can Marissa/Laura confirm whether we should start with the Dedicated 99.95% path or propose the hosted uplift with higher credit exposure?

Action items:
- Emily: confirm preference (Dedicated vs hosted uplift) and whether the credit should be expressed as % of monthly invoice or as days-of-service equivalent.
- Marissa: I'll need a short note on commercial delta to provision a dedicated pool if they choose it.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference
Attachments: SLA_Amendment_Peregrine_v0.1.docx; Credit_Calc_Sheet.xlsx
From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Emily Russo <erusso@peregrineanalytics.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 4 Sep 2025 10:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: uptime target and monthly credit calc

Emily — looping in Laura (legal) and putting in a concrete proposal to keep the procurement review moving.

Proposal summary (redwood):
- Option A: Dedicated commitment (reserved pool) — 99.95% monthly uptime. Remedy: banded credits: 99.90–99.95% -> 10% of monthly invoice; 99.00–99.90% -> 30%; <99.00% -> 60%. This requires an annual reserved commitment and a short lead time to provision.

- Option B: Hosted uplift pilot — 99.92% monthly uptime (90-day pilot). Remedy: proportional credits expressed as a percentage of the monthly invoice using the attached formula (see sheet) with a cap of 50% per month. Because this remains on shared capacity, it includes an operational exception for scheduled maintenance windows and limited burst events.

We propose expressing credits as % of invoice (simpler for procurement). The attached SLA draft uses clear band definitions and an illustrative worked example for a 3% p95 latency regression (translated into equivalent credit using the latency-to-availability mapping in the doc).

Legal note (Laura): please see section 4.2 in the draft for the exact credit computation; if that looks good, I can send this version to your procurement team and request a short turn on redlines.

Marissa Cole
Head of Customer Success, Redwood Inference
Attachments: SLA_Amendment_Peregrine_v0.1.docx; Credit_Calc_Sheet.xlsx

On Wed, Sep 3, 2025 at 11:02 AM Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Emily — thanks for the pragmatic notes and for the spreadsheet, that helped. Quick answers up front:
>
> - Hosted tier: our standard hosted SLA is 99.9% availability. 99.95% is above our standard hosted commitment; we can either (a) offer a dedicated capacity commitment at 99.95% under a reserved contract, or (b) for hosted, we can propose a 99.92% uplift with a small operational exception window and a higher credit band if you prefer to remain on hosted.
>
> - Monthly credit logic: we typically use banded crediting (e.g., >=99.9% -> 0; 99.5–99.9% -> X%; <99.5% -> Y%). However, I can translate that into a proportional formula for your procurement team — the attached draft shows both banded and proportional examples and two worked scenarios (p95 latency regression vs uptime shortfall).
>
> Ava
From: Emily Russo <erusso@peregrineanalytics.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Sep 2025 16:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: uptime target and monthly credit calc

Thanks — appreciate the clear options. Procurement has sign-off authority for pilot pilots but the board will only approve an annual reserved commitment if we get 99.95% on Dedicated. For now we want to: (1) proceed with Option B hosted uplift pilot at 99.92% expressed as % of invoice with the 50% cap, and (2) add a condition that, after a successful 90-day pilot (defined as <0.1% availability incidents), we convert to a Dedicated 99.95% commitment with a 6-month provisioning lead and a 12-month reserved term.

I reviewed the amendment and suggested two minor redline points in the shared doc (doc comments): clarifying scheduled maintenance notification windows and adding a Customer contact escalation matrix. Once you accept those minor edits we'll loop in Finance for the commercial delta.

Emily
On Thu, Sep 4, 2025 at 10:45 AM Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com> wrote:
> Emily — looping in Laura (legal) and putting in a concrete proposal to keep the procurement review moving.
>
> Proposal summary (redwood):
> - Option A: Dedicated commitment (reserved pool) — 99.95% monthly uptime. Remedy: banded credits: 99.90–99.95% -> 10% of monthly invoice; 99.00–99.90% -> 30%; <99.00% -> 60%. This requires an annual reserved commitment and a short lead time to provision.
>
> - Option B: Hosted uplift pilot — 99.92% monthly uptime (90-day pilot). Remedy: proportional credits expressed as a percentage of the monthly invoice using the attached formula (see sheet) with a cap of 50% per month. Because this remains on shared capacity, it includes an operational exception for scheduled maintenance windows and limited burst events.
>
> Thanks,
> Marissa

To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Sep 2025 16:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: uptime target and monthly credit calc

Thanks — appreciate the clear options. Procurement has sign-off authority for pilot pilots but the board will only approve an annual reserved commitment if we get 99.95% on Dedicated. For now we want to: (1) proceed with Option B hosted uplift pilot at 99.92% expressed as % of invoice with the 50% cap, and (2) add a condition that, after a successful 90-day pilot (defined as <0.1% availability incidents), we convert to a Dedicated 99.95% commitment with a 6-month provisioning lead and a 12-month reserved term.

I reviewed the amendment and suggested two minor redline points in the shared doc (doc comments): clarifying scheduled maintenance notification windows and adding a Customer contact escalation matrix. Once you accept those minor edits we'll loop in Finance for the commercial delta.

Emily

text:
From: Emily Russo <erusso@peregrineanalytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Sep 2025 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Initial ask: uptime target and monthly credit calc

Hi Ava / Marissa,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — Peregrine is excited about Redwood's latency profile for our inference-heavy pipelines. Before we move into drafting an amendment, procurement asked for two clarifications that will affect the commercial ask:

1) Uptime target: we need a firm commitment for the hosted tier at 99.95% (monthly availability) given our SLA with downstream customers. Is that achievable for your hosted plan or would that require Dedicated capacity?

2) Monthly credit logic: we want the credit calculation to scale proportionally across partial-month degradations rather than a binary trigger. I attached a brief spreadsheet with our expected call volumes and a reference formula. Can Redwood propose a simple table that maps availability bands to credit percentages (monthly credit as % of invoice)?

If possible, could you share a draft amendment and a worked example showing how credits would be calculated for a 3% degradation in p95 latency vs a 0.2% uptime shortfall? Procurement will need the exact wording for their internal approval.

Thanks — Emily
Emily Russo
Head of Procurement, Peregrine Analytics
Attachments: Credit_Calc_Sheet.xlsx


From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Emily Russo <erusso@peregrineanalytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Sep 2025 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: uptime target and monthly credit calc

Hi Emily — thanks for the pragmatic notes and for the spreadsheet, that helped. Quick answers up front:

- Hosted tier: our standard hosted SLA is 99.9% availability. 99.95% is above our standard hosted commitment; we can either (a) offer a dedicated capacity commitment at 99.95% under a reserved contract, or (b) for hosted, we can propose a 99.92% uplift with a small operational exception window and a higher credit band if you prefer to remain on hosted.

- Monthly credit logic: we typically use banded crediting (e.g., >=99.9% -> 0; 99.5–99.9% -> X%; <99.5% -> Y%). However, I can translate that into a proportional formula for your procurement team — the attached draft shows both banded and proportional examples and two worked scenarios (p95 latency regression vs uptime shortfall).

I attached an initial amendment (SLA_Amendment_Peregrine_v0.1.docx) and the credit calc we discussed. Can Marissa/Laura confirm whether we should start with the Dedicated 99.95% path or propose the hosted uplift with higher credit exposure?

Action items:
- Emily: confirm preference (Dedicated vs hosted uplift) and whether the credit should be expressed as % of monthly invoice or as days-of-service equivalent.
- Marissa: I'll need a short note on commercial delta to provision a dedicated pool if they choose it.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference
Attachments: SLA_Amendment_Peregrine_v0.1.docx; Credit_Calc_Sheet.xlsx


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Emily Russo <erusso@peregrineanalytics.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 4 Sep 2025 10:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: uptime target and monthly credit calc

Emily — looping in Laura (legal) and putting in a concrete proposal to keep the procurement review moving.

Proposal summary (redwood):
- Option A: Dedicated commitment (reserved pool) — 99.95% monthly uptime. Remedy: banded credits: 99.90–99.95% -> 10% of monthly invoice; 99.00–99.90% -> 30%; <99.00% -> 60%. This requires an annual reserved commitment and a short lead time to provision.

- Option B: Hosted uplift pilot — 99.92% monthly uptime (90-day pilot). Remedy: proportional credits expressed as a percentage of the monthly invoice using the attached formula (see sheet) with a cap of 50% per month. Because this remains on shared capacity, it includes an operational exception for scheduled maintenance windows and limited burst events.

We propose expressing credits as % of invoice (simpler for procurement). The attached SLA draft uses clear band definitions and an illustrative worked example for a 3% p95 latency regression (translated into equivalent credit using the latency-to-availability mapping in the doc).

Legal note (Laura): please see section 4.2 in the draft for the exact credit computation; if that looks good, I can send this version to your procurement team and request a short turn on redlines.

Marissa Cole
Head of Customer Success, Redwood Inference
Attachments: SLA_Amendment_Peregrine_v0.1.docx; Credit_Calc_Sheet.xlsx

> - Monthly credit logic: we typically use banded crediting (e.g., >=99.9% -> 0; 99.5–99.9% -> X%; <99.5% -> Y%). However, I can translate that into a proportional formula for your procurement team — the attached draft shows both banded and proportional examples and two worked scenarios (p95 latency regression vs uptime shortfall).
>
> Ava

source.subject: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Diana Park <diana@medicore.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

Hi Diana —

Thanks again for the time last week. As we talked through during the demo, the fastest path for Medicore to get a short pilot live (1–2 weeks of ingest + 2 weeks evaluation) is if we align on a light MAP that sequences technical acceptance, security evidence, and signature gates.

I pulled together a first-pass MAP (attached) that maps out the:
- Evaluation milestones (data ingest, baseline eval, accuracy check, UAT)
- Contract gating (MSA signature, DPA completion, order form sign-off)
- Procurement items (SOW/PO, billing contact, payment terms)

Can you confirm these constraints from your side:
1) Legal needs a full DPA review by Medicore legal before MSA countersign — true/false?
2) Procurement requires an internal PO number before vendor activation?
3) Any absolute blackout dates for June/July that would block your security team?

If that lines up, Karthik (cc) and I will propose a minimal sequencing that keeps the evaluation independent of final order-form language until you hit the UAT pass. Attached: medicore_map_v1.xlsx (MAP draft).

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0126
Redwood Inference

Attachments: medicore_map_v1.xlsx (MAP draft)

From: Diana Park <diana@medicore.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Sanjay Rao <sanjay.rao@medicore.com>, Elaine Wu <elaine.wu@medicore.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Jun 2025 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the MAP. Quick answers from procurement/legal side:
- DPA: Legal prefers to agree DPA terms before any provisioning for data containing PHI. For a limited pilot we can consider a scoped data subset and a short-form DPA addendum, but Legal (Sanjay) needs to review first.
- PO: Procurement typically issues PO before we process payment, but IT sometimes allows a temporary vendor onboarding form that lets us stand up test accounts for pilots. I'll check if we can do a temporary onboarding to start ingest without full PO.
- Blackouts: Our security reviewers are away Jul 3–10 (conference travel). Any gating work in that window will slow us down.

Sanjay, Elaine — looping you in to confirm DPA path.

Best,
Diana Park
Director, Product Operations | Medicore Health
D: 212-555-0444

---
On Mon, Jun 16, 2025 at 09:12 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Diana —
> 
> Thanks again for the time last week...

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Diana Park <diana@medicore.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Sanjay Rao <sanjay@medicore.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Jun 2025 08:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

Diana / Ava —

Thanks for the clarity. Based on your constraints I recommend a two-track sequencing that minimizes legal blocking for the pilot provisioning:

Track A (Technical onboarding — low legal friction) — timeline: Day 0–10
- Day 0: Temporary vendor onboarding form signed by Medicore IT (no PO) -> Redwood provisions sandbox tenant (no PHI)
- Day 1–4: Data subset ingest (de-identified) + baseline eval run
- Day 5–8: Joint review of metrics; iterate prompts/configs
- Day 9–10: UAT pass for feature set we agreed as pilot success

Track B (Contracting & procurement) — runs in parallel, aim to finish by Day 14
- Day 0–7: Medicore legal reviews short-form DPA addendum + Redwood sends MSA summary (redwood_msa_summary.pdf attached)
- Day 8–12: Procurement issues PO / order form (or provisional PO)
- Day 12–14: Signatures complete, move pilot to production tenant if desired

Key assumptions / asks from Medicore:
- If Legal insists on full DPA prior to any provisioning we will adjust and keep initial runs strictly on synthetic/de-identified data.
- Provide a single procurement contact and billing email to speed PO issuance.

I'll attach a checklist CSV for our side (procurement_checklist.csv) to track items. Marcus — can you review the acceptance gating bullets (below) and confirm they look OK to ship as the MAP success criteria?

Acceptance gating (proposal):
- Data ingest complete (ingested rows match sample schema)
- Top-line latency and throughput within target (95thp < 350ms)
- Baseline quality: precision/recall thresholds met on test set
- No P0 security flags during vetting

Thanks,
Karthik
Director, Solutions Engineering | Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_msa_summary.pdf, procurement_checklist.csv

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Diana Park <diana@medicore.com>
Cc: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@medicore.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Jun 2025 14:06:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

All —

I reviewed the acceptance gating and have two small edits to keep the pilot focused and unblock signature sequencing:
1) Split the "quality" gate into two: baseline metric pass for the instrumented test set (used for internal validation) and a lightweight business-signoff milestone (Medicore product owner confirms results are "fit for purpose"). This lets Legal/Procurement sign the order-form while business-side confirms fit.
2) Add a contingency: if Medicore legal requests changes to the DPA that materially affect data processing, we will cap the pilot to de-identified data and pause PHI-related evaluations until DPA is agreed.

If you (Diana/Sanjay) can confirm the temporary onboarding form option, I propose we target provisioning on Mon Jun 30 and run the technical Track A in parallel with contracting Track B through Jul 10 (noting your blackout Jul 3–10). That timing keeps the evaluation window clear of major holidays and aligns with our engineering squad availability.

Marcus Lin
Principal Product Manager, Redwood Inference
marcus@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0899

On Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 08:27 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com> wrote:
> Diana / Ava —
> 
> Thanks for the clarity. Based on your constraints I recommend a two-track sequencing...

From: Diana Park <diana@medicore.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Sanjay Rao <sanjay@medicore.com>, Elaine Wu <elaine.wu@medicore.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 16:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

Marcus / Ava / Karthik —

Thanks — confirmation from our side:
- Procurement can provide a temporary onboarding form this week to enable sandbox provisioning (no PO required up-front). I will route it to IT and copy you when complete.
- Legal (Sanjay) will start review of the short-form DPA addendum; if we need a full DPA we’ll flag it but will try to keep the pilot scoped to de-identified data until that is concluded.
- We agree to target provisioning on Mon Jun 30 for Track A. Given our review calendar, full contracting (Track B) will likely finish by Jul 14 if all gates pass.

I'll attach the onboarding checklist Medicore uses (internal) in a follow-up — for now, please go ahead and provision the sandbox tenant on your side on Jun 30.

Best,
Diana
Director, Product Operations | Medicore Health

Attachments: (will share Medicore onboarding checklist in follow-up)

- If Legal insists on full DPA prior to any provisioning we will adjust and keep initial runs strictly on synthetic/de-identified data.
- Provide a single procurement contact and billing email to speed PO issuance.

I'll attach a checklist CSV for our side (procurement_checklist.csv) to track items. Marcus — can you review the acceptance gating bullets (below) and confirm they look OK to ship as the MAP success criteria?

Acceptance gating (proposal):
- Data ingest complete (ingested rows match sample schema)
- Top-line latency and throughput within target (95thp < 350ms)
- Baseline quality: precision/recall thresholds met on test set
- No P0 security flags during vetting

Thanks,
Karthik
Director, Solutions Engineering | Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_msa_summary.pdf, procurement_checklist.csv

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Diana Park <diana@medicore.com>
Cc: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@medicore.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Jun 2025 14:06:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

All —

I reviewed the acceptance gating and have two small edits to keep the pilot focused and unblock signature sequencing:
1) Split the "quality" gate into two: baseline metric pass for the instrumented test set (used for internal validation) and a lightweight business-signoff milestone (Medicore product owner confirms results are "fit for purpose"). This lets Legal/Procurement sign the order-form while business-side confirms fit.
2) Add a contingency: if Medicore legal requests changes to the DPA that materially affect data processing, we will cap the pilot to de-identified data and pause PHI-related evaluations until DPA is agreed.

If you (Diana/Sanjay) can confirm the temporary onboarding form option, I propose we target provisioning on Mon Jun 30 and run the technical Track A in parallel with contracting Track B through Jul 10 (noting your blackout Jul 3–10). That timing keeps the evaluation window clear of major holidays and aligns with our engineering squad availability.

Marcus Lin
Principal Product Manager, Redwood Inference
marcus@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0899

On Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 08:27 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com> wrote:
> Diana / Ava —
> 
> Thanks for the clarity. Based on your constraints I recommend a two-track sequencing...


From: Diana Park <diana@medicore.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Sanjay Rao <sanjay@medicore.com>, Elaine Wu <elaine.wu@medicore.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 16:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Contract choreography & pilot eval milestones for Medicore pilot

Marcus / Ava / Karthik —

Thanks — confirmation from our side:
- Procurement can provide a temporary onboarding form this week to enable sandbox provisioning (no PO required up-front). I will route it to IT and copy you when complete.
- Legal (Sanjay) will start review of the short-form DPA addendum; if we need a full DPA we’ll flag it but will try to keep the pilot scoped to de-identified data until that is concluded.
- We agree to target provisioning on Mon Jun 30 for Track A. Given our review calendar, full contracting (Track B) will likely finish by Jul 14 if all gates pass.

I'll attach the onboarding checklist Medicore uses (internal) in a follow-up — for now, please go ahead and provision the sandbox tenant on your side on Jun 30.

Best,
Diana
Director, Product Operations | Medicore Health

Attachments: (will share Medicore onboarding checklist in follow-up)

source.subject: BrightArc onboarding: multi-PO invoices, portal ack SLAs, and OCR fallback
messages:
From: Lucy Park <lucy@brightarc.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Accounting (BrightArc) <payables@brightarc.io>
Date: Sun, 15 Jun 2025 09:07:12 -0700
Subject: Vendor onboarding: questions on multi-PO invoices, naming and portal vs email fallback

Hi Ava / Marissa,

Thanks for starting the BrightArc vendor onboarding. A couple of practical questions from AP as we prepare our first invoice: 

1) Our system generates one PDF per invoice but we often need to charge against multiple Redwood POs on the same invoice (separate PO numbers for different teams). Is it acceptable to send a single PDF that contains multiple PO numbers and line items, or do you require one invoice per PO?

2) File naming — would you prefer the PDF subject line and filename to include the primary PO only, or all PO numbers? Example filename we currently use: BrightArc_Invoice_20250614.pdf.

3) Portal vs email: we prefer to submit via SFTP/portal but need a fallback to email while our connector is approved. If we email the invoice, what is your expected acknowledgement window (how quickly will you confirm receipt and expected payment date)?

4) OCR/searchability: our billing engine outputs scanned PDFs that are not searchable. Do you accept a scanned PDF plus a pasted plain-text invoice body in the email as an OCR fallback, or must the PDF itself be text-searchable (PDF/A)?

Attaching a sample invoice so you can see our layout (BrightArc_Inv_20250614.pdf). Happy to adapt — we just want to align before we submit several invoices next week.

Thanks,

Lucy Park
Finance Operations Lead, BrightArc
lucy@brightarc.io
+1 415-555-0182

Attachment: BrightArc_Inv_20250614.pdf (application/pdf, 1.2MB)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Lucy Park <lucy@brightarc.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 15 Jun 2025 11:22:03 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding: questions on multi-PO invoices, naming and portal vs email fallback

Hi Lucy — great to meet you, and welcome. Short answers first, then some specifics and an attached template that should make multi-PO mapping straightforward.

Summary:
- Portal (preferred): please upload invoices to our Vendor Portal when possible. Portal uploads get an automated acknowledgement within 48 hours and are routed to AP for PO matching.
- Email (fallback): we will accept invoices sent to payables@redwood.ai while you finish connector work, but expect manual processing and a standard acknowledgement timeline of up to 3 business days.
- Multi-PO: single PDF containing multiple PO numbers is acceptable, but you must include a simple CSV or XLSX that maps each invoice line (or subtotal) to the PO number. I attached multi-po-template.csv as an example.
- File naming: include all PO numbers in the email Subject and in the filename where feasible (e.g., BrightArc_INV_20250614_PO1234_PO1250.pdf). If the filename would be too long, include primary PO in the filename and list all POs in the email subject and csv mapping.
- OCR fallback: preferred is a searchable PDF (PDF/A). If you cannot produce that, please paste the line-item table in the email body and attach a CSV/XLSX mapping. That combination will let our OCR/ingest team avoid manual re-keying.

Attached: multi-po-template.csv (CSV showing line->PO mapping) and BrightArc_sample_lines.xlsx (example of how we'd like line items exported).

Couple of operational notes:
- When emailing, put the SUBJECT as: Invoice BrightArc — PO: 1234,1250 — INV: 20250614 -> this helps automated routing.
- For portal uploads, the portal will return an acknowledgement ID and ETA; keep that acknowledgement ID for follow-up.

I'll loop in Marissa (AP) for any special payment-term exceptions; Neha handles vendor setup if you hit any validation errors. Connor — can you confirm whether we need any extra onboarding fields for BrightArc in the portal?

Best,
Ava
Ava Chen
Manager, Procurement Ops | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai

Attachments: multi-po-template.csv (text/csv), BrightArc_sample_lines.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)

From: Lucy Park <lucy@brightarc.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Accounting (BrightArc) <payables@brightarc.io>
Date: Mon, 16 Jun 2025 08:14:29 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding: questions on multi-PO invoices, naming and portal vs email fallback

Thanks Ava — that's clear and helpful. A couple of follow-ups: 

> On Sun, Jun 15, 2025 at 11:22 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> 
> > - Multi-PO: single PDF containing multiple PO numbers is acceptable, but you must include a simple CSV or XLSX that maps each invoice line (or subtotal) to the PO number. I attached multi-po-template.csv as an example.

1) For the CSV mapping, can you confirm if you need a per-line mapping (one line per invoice row) or is a per-subtotal mapping OK? Our exports can do either but default to subtotal groupings.

2) If we paste the OCR'd body in the email — will that be treated as an authoritative copy for routing, or does your AP team still require the CSV/XLSX for automated matching?

3) Our accounting system cannot produce PDF/A without a patch. We have an OCR service we can run before sending that produces an extracted CSV and a searchable PDF; would you accept both the searchable PDF + the extracted CSV PRIMARILY and keep the original scanned PDF as backup?

We'll start with two invoices next week; want to be sure we follow the path that minimizes manual rework.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

—Lucy

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Lucy Park <lucy@brightarc.io>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 17 Jun 2025 14:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding: questions on multi-PO invoices, naming and portal vs email fallback

Lucy — quick operational answers from AP below so you can run with your connector work: 

- Mapping granularity: per-line mapping is ideal because it avoids PO split ambiguity. If you must provide subtotals, include a clearly labelled column in the CSV called "Subtotal_Group" and list the PO for each group. Either option is fine as long as there's an explicit mapping.

- Email body OCR: searchable PDF + extracted CSV is best. If you do paste plain-text invoice lines into the email body, we will accept that as a fallback, but AP will still ask for the CSV for automated matching. Treat the pasted body as a convenience rather than a substitute.

- Acceptable file packaging: one PDF (searchable) + one CSV/XLSX mapping file. If you cannot produce searchable PDF, send: scanned PDF + extracted CSV + paste lines in email body. We'll mark the upload as "manual review required."

- SUBJECT line: Please include all PO numbers as Ava suggested. Example subject we prefer: "Invoice BrightArc — INV: 20250614 — PO: 1234,1250"

If you want, send the two invoices to payables@redwood.ai and mark them "portal fallback" in the subject; I'll prioritize those for manual review to reduce processing lag while your portal connector is pending.

Marissa Cole
Accounts Payable Manager | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai
Attachment: processing_checklist.pdf (PDF, 120KB)

source.subject: First-mile renewal playbook & QBR availability
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sam Lee <sam.lee@brightloop.io>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@brightloop.io
Date: Thu, 12 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: First-mile renewal playbook & QBR availability

Hi Sam —

Thanks again for the time last week. As we discussed, BrightLoop's annual renewal window is coming up and we'd like to kick off a focused runbook to surface: term options, capacity uplift opportunities, pricing levers, and a QBR to align execs and the engineering stakeholders.

To keep this lightweight, proposed next steps:
- Redwood to share a 1-page mutual action plan (attached) with milestones for procurement/legal/ops.
- BrightLoop to share current monthly token/embedding volume and any projected peak events for next 12 months.
- Schedule a 60-min QBR (exec + engineering) in the next 2 weeks to review options.

Can you confirm who from your side should be on the QBR (we've CC'd procurement but happy to add product/infra leads)? And please send 2–3 windows that work for you next week.

I've attached a draft agenda and the mutual action plan for the runbook. Ben will own the QBR facilitation and Karthik will bring capacity/throughput recommendations.

Thanks — look forward to your availability.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: BrightLoop_MutualActionPlan_v1.xlsx, QBR-Agenda-Draft.pdf
From: Sam Lee <sam.lee@brightloop.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@brightloop.io, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Jun 2025 11:07:00 -0700
Subject: Re: First-mile renewal playbook & QBR availability

Thanks Ava — this looks good.

Attendees: Myself (Head of ML), Priya Desai (VP Product), Mark Turner (SRE), and RFP/Contracts (procurement@brightloop.io). If we need an exec, I can invite our CTO (Elena Park) for the QBR readout.

Availability next week:
- Tue 17 Jun, 10:00–11:00 PT
- Wed 18 Jun, 13:00–14:00 PT
- Thu 19 Jun, 09:00–10:00 PT (tight for Mark but possible)

I'll pull and attach a short usage snapshot for May (token and embedding counts) and send it by end of day. Can you also indicate if you plan to present a recommended capacity/price uplift in that QBR or keep it as options?

—Sam
Sam Lee
Head of ML | BrightLoop
sam.lee@brightloop.io

On Thu, Jun 12, 2025 at 09:12 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Hi Sam —
> 
> Thanks again for the time last week...
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sam Lee <sam.lee@brightloop.io>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>

source.subject: Brightline pilot: owners, go/no-go cadence, and weekend oncall
source.messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Cc: Alice Moreno <alice.moreno@brightline.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Brightline pilot: owners, go/no-go cadence, and weekend oncall

Team — quick handoff summary for Brightline (post-sign, SOW attached):

What closed: Brightline agreed to a 90-day pilot (up to 500k tokens/month) with optional extension to a production seat; legal completed terms, awaiting signed DocuSign (attached DS_Signature_Brightline.pdf). HubSpot deal: BL-2025-062.

Proposed kickoff: Wed, Jun 11 — 30/60/90 milestones and acceptance criteria meeting.
Primary asks for CS/OPS:
- Confirm primary Success owner and week-by-week oncall / escalation ladder.
- Confirm initial capacity needs: expected peak RPS and tokens/day (Brightline provided an estimate but we need to validate).
- Identify any immediate compliance/residency constraints (Brightline is US-based; they asked about log retention).

Attachments: SOW_v1.pdf (draft), contract/terms link via DocuSign. Also shared the mutual action plan in Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1BrightlinePilot

Suggested owners (from sales):
- Success lead: Monica Patel (recommended)
- Oncall/infra: Vivek Kulkarni (to confirm weekend coverage)
- Sales PM / escalation: Ben Carter (me)

Alice — feel free to add or correct anything I missed. Monica / Vivek / Ava — can you confirm ownership and whether the proposed Jun 11 kickoff works? If CS needs any pre-kickoff artifacts, I can push them today.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0131

-----

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Cc: Alice Moreno <alice.moreno@brightline.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Jun 2025 12:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Brightline pilot: owners, go/no-go cadence, and weekend oncall

Thanks Ben — I can own Success for this one. Quick clarifying Qs so we scope SOW and runbook properly before the Jun 11 kickoff:

1) Start / go-live expectation: is Jun 11 purely kickoff (yes) or do they expect a pilot traffic start the same week?
2) Peak / average token estimates: the HubSpot note says ~400k tokens/month, but can Alice confirm tokens/day and expected concurrency?
3) Data residency & retention: Alice, any mandated retention periods or encryption keys we should know about?
4) Integrations: will Brightline be using our hosted API (preferred) or do they require private deployment right away? Any cloud connectors or SSO (SAML) requirements?

Proposed internal plan (pre-kickoff deliverables):
- Finalize SOW + acceptance criteria (I will iterate with Ben today).
- Run a lightweight pre-kickoff tech intake with Vivek to validate capacity.
- Build a prompt/regression test set we can run after onboarding (I can draft).

If no blockers, I’ll take the kickoff lead on Jun 11 and run the 30/60/90 cadence. For the first two weeks I’d like to pair with Vivek for oncall coverage — we should lock weekend rotations now.

Attachments I’ll add to the kickoff packet: pilot-risks.pdf (high level), onboarding-checklist.md (draft).

Monica Patel
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.com | +1 617-555-0202

-----

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Jun 2025 13:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Brightline pilot: owners, go/no-go cadence, and weekend oncall

Vivek — forwarding Ben’s note. Monica volunteered to be Success lead and asked me to confirm weekend rotation and capacity. Can you answer the tech items below before EOD?

Tech questions (short):
- Based on ~400k tokens/month (bursty), do you recommend starting on Hosted with pin vs provisioning a Dedicated pool?
- If Dedicated, rough lead time to stand up minimal capacity (GPUs, routing, and canary policy)?
- Any obvious security/compliance gating items for US-only datasets (KMS / audit logs we should schedule with Brightline legal)?

Ask: if you confirm weekend oncall for the first two weeks we can add that to the kickoff invite. Also confirm the infra contact alias we should include in the kickoff packet so Brightline can reach ops during the pilot.

Thanks — Ava
Ava Chen
Customer Onboarding Lead | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 510-555-0144

----- Forwarded message -----
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Brightline pilot: owners, go/no-go cadence, and weekend oncall
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Cc: Alice Moreno <alice.moreno@brightline.com>

(Quoted above)

-----

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Alice Moreno <alice.moreno@brightline.com>
Date: Wed, 4 Jun 2025 08:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Brightline pilot: owners, go/no-go cadence, and weekend oncall

All — quick tech response and recommended run: feel free to copy the infra notes into the kickoff packet.

Short answers:
- Recommendation: start on Hosted API with a pinned model variant and KV-prefix caching. For ~400k tokens/month with bursty traffic, Hosted keeps the operational risk low and reduces lead time. We can migrate to Dedicated if traffic predictably exceeds 1.2M tokens/month or sustained concurrency > 40rps.
- Dedicated lead time: ~10–14 business days to provision a minimal reserved pool (depends on hardware availability and region).
- Compliance: for US-only datasets we need KMS integration (we can use customer-managed keys), and we should enable audit logging from day 1. Brightline legal will need the SOC2 evidence pack; attach capacity-estimate.xlsx for rough sizing.

Oncall / rotation proposal:
- Weekdays: Monica (primary), Vivek (secondary)
- Weekends (first two weeks after pilot start): Vivek (primary), Monica (backup)
- Infra contact alias: infra-team@redwood.com — this reaches SRE/ops rotation.

Operational notes:
- Suggest we include an early ramp smoke test (small traffic window) before the pilot acceptance test.
- Recommend we enable prefix caching and 512-token chunking in the first 30 days to reduce cost and stabilize latency.

I’ll upload capacity-estimate.xlsx to the Drive folder and work with Monica on the intake checklist. If legal needs the SOC2 extract, ping security@redwood.com and I’ll route it.

– Vivek Kulkarni
Director, Inference Operations | Redwood Inference
vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0199

-----

From: Alice Moreno <alice.moreno@brightline.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Cc: Brightline Legal <legal@brightline.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Jun 2025 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Brightline pilot: owners, go/no-go cadence, and weekend oncall

Thanks everyone — excited to get started. A few quick notes from our side: 

- DocuSign sent and returned; I attached the signed copy (DS_Signature_Brightline.pdf) for your records.
- Jun 11 kickoff works for the team — we expect to start limited traffic the following Monday (Jun 16) after the smoke window.
- Tokens/day: we’re estimating a peak of ~35k tokens/day during tests, up to ~70k on heavy days; concurrency should be modest (~12–18 rps).
- Data residency: US-only, retention policy 90 days; we are OK with customer-managed KMS.

Looking forward to the Jun 11 call — please send a calendar invite and the pre-kickoff checklist so our engineers can prep.

Best,

Subject: Re: Brightline pilot: owners, go/no-go cadence, and weekend oncall

All — quick tech response and recommended run: feel free to copy the infra notes into the kickoff packet.

Short answers:
- Recommendation: start on Hosted API with a pinned model variant and KV-prefix caching. For ~400k tokens/month with bursty traffic, Hosted keeps the operational risk low and reduces lead time. We can migrate to Dedicated if traffic predictably exceeds 1.2M tokens/month or sustained concurrency > 40rps.
- Dedicated lead time: ~10–14 business days to provision a minimal reserved pool (depends on hardware availability and region).
- Compliance: for US-only datasets we need KMS integration (we can use customer-managed keys), and we should enable audit logging from day 1. Brightline legal will need the SOC2 evidence pack; attach capacity-estimate.xlsx for rough sizing.

Oncall / rotation proposal:
- Weekdays: Monica (primary), Vivek (secondary)
- Weekends (first two weeks after pilot start): Vivek (primary), Monica (backup)
- Infra contact alias: infra-team@redwood.com — this reaches SRE/ops rotation.

Operational notes:
- Suggest we include an early ramp smoke test (small traffic window) before the pilot acceptance test.
- Recommend we enable prefix caching and 512-token chunking in the first 30 days to reduce cost and stabilize latency.

I’ll upload capacity-estimate.xlsx to the Drive folder and work with Monica on the intake checklist. If legal needs the SOC2 extract, ping security@redwood.com and I’ll route it.

– Vivek Kulkarni
Director, Inference Operations | Redwood Inference
vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0199

- Jun 11 kickoff works for the team — we expect to start limited traffic the following Monday (Jun 16) after the smoke window.
- Tokens/day: we’re estimating a peak of ~35k tokens/day during tests, up to ~70k on heavy days; concurrency should be modest (~12–18 rps).
- Data residency: US-only, retention policy 90 days; we are OK with customer-managed KMS.

Looking forward to the Jun 11 call — please send a calendar invite and the pre-kickoff checklist so our engineers can prep.

source.subject: Orchid AI - SLA uptime anchoring and monthly remedy proposal
messages:
From: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Orchid AI Procurement <procurement@orchid.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the demo last week — the platform looks great. Procurement reviewed the standard SLA you shared and we have two asks before we can move to PO: 

1) Uptime anchoring. For the first 90 days of the pilot we need a provisional uptime floor at 98.0% (rather than the standard 99.5%) due to the expected burst patterns during onboarding. We understand this is temporary and would expect the baseline to re-anchor at 99.5% after the pilot.

2) Latency carveouts. Our inference workload will use scheduled large-batch jobs overnight. We need explicit carveouts for batched inference windows (daily 0100–0400 PT) so batch-induced tail latency doesn’t trigger credits.

Procurement also asked for: monthly remedy cap (we’d like a hard cap of 2% of monthly invoice) and a weekly operational report for the pilot period. I attached our redlines (Orchid_SLA_Redlines.pdf).

Can Redwood accept these pilot terms, or propose an alternative that achieves the same operational protection for Orchid?

Best,
Ethan
Ethan Ross
Head of Platform Ops, Orchid AI
ethan.ross@orchid.ai
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 10:09:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ethan — thanks for the clear note and the redlines. Short answers up front: we can accommodate a temporary pilot uptime anchor and define explicit batch windows as carveouts, but we have a few guardrails we need to propose (see below).

Proposed counterpoints:
- Pilot uptime anchor: Redwood can agree to a 98.0% provisional uptime floor for the first 90 days of the pilot, with an automatic reversion to 99.5% after successful validation. We’ll include objective pass/fail criteria tied to your traffic profile to avoid ambiguity.
- Batch latency carveouts: we can exclude scheduled batch windows (0100–0400 PT) from credit calculations provided you flag those windows in advance and they are limited to repeating nightly maintenance/batch jobs (not ad-hoc production spikes).
- Monthly remedy cap: we typically cap monthly credits but not as low as 2% for pilot customers — that would materially limit remedy viability. Proposed alternative: cap monthly remedy to 5% of invoice for the pilot, with an opt-in to lower caps post-production if you require it.
- Operational reporting: we can provide a weekly pilot health digest (uptime/latency token-level breakdowns, incidents, and KV cache hit-rate) during onboarding.

I’m looping in Ben Carter (Sales) and Laura Bennett (Legal) to align on deal mechanics and a compact SLA amendment doc. Attached is a short counter (Redwood_SLA-Counter_v1.docx) illustrating the language we'd like to use. If that looks reasonable we can finalize legal language and get a DocuSign link out this week.

Next steps: please confirm you’re comfortable with the 5% pilot cap alternative and the batch-window requirements, or propose a compromise. Happy to jump on a 30-min call if that helps.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0192
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Ben/Laura — forwarding Ethan's redlines and my proposed counter. Key points for discussion:

- Pilot uptime anchor at 98.0% for 90 days is grantable. We should add explicit validation criteria: e.g., 7-day rolling p95/availability sample across their top-5 routes. If they fail to reach 99.5% after pilot, we escalate to a playbook rather than immediate credit clawbacks.
- Batch carveouts: OK to exclude nightly windows with the condition that Orchid provides a weekly schedule and we reserve right to reclassify repeated daytime spikes as production traffic.
- Remedy cap: Procurement wants 2%. I pushed back and suggested 5%. Laura, can you take a look at the counter doc and advise if we have room to accept 3% as a compromise during pilot? Also confirm whether we should add a clause limiting credit payouts to a maximum cumulative amount over the pilot term.

Quoting Ethan below for context.

-----Original Message-----
From: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
Sent: Tue, 27 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Orchid AI Procurement <procurement@orchid.ai>
Subject: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the demo last week — the platform looks great. Procurement reviewed the standard SLA you shared and we have two asks before we can move to PO: ...
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
Cc: Orchid AI Procurement <procurement@orchid.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 28 May 2025 15:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Ethan — thanks for reviewing and for the redlines. Laura and Ben reviewed and we propose the following formal counter for the pilot term: 

1) Pilot uptime anchor: Redwood will document a provisional uptime floor of 98.0% for the first 90 days starting on the go-live date. We’ll include objective validation criteria (7-day rolling availability sample across their top-5 routes) to confirm drift back to 99.5%. If Orchid meets validation, baseline reverts automatically; if not, we engage an operational remediation playbook (no automatic credit acceleration).

2) Latency carveouts: Scheduled batch windows (0100–0400 PT) will be excluded from monthly credit calculations provided Orchid supplies a weekly schedule and the windows are limited to recurring batch jobs. Ad-hoc large jobs outside those windows will remain in scope.

3) Monthly remedy cap: For the pilot period only, Redwood proposes a 4% cap on monthly remedy (a midpoint between your 2% ask and our standard exposure). Payouts remain subject to incident validation and remediation offsets. After the pilot, we revert to our standard remedy cap unless mutually amended.

4) Reporting and cadence: Redwood will send a weekly pilot health digest (PDF) and host a weekly 30-min check-in during the 90-day pilot to review anomalies, trending, and any scheduling conflicts.

Attached: updated SLA amendment (Redwood_SLA-Counter_v1.docx) and a marked copy of your redlines (Orchid_SLA_Redlines.pdf). If the 4% cap is acceptable, we’ll route the amended SLA to DocuSign and target signatures by end of week. If you prefer to discuss, I’m free Thu 10–12 PT.

Thanks and looking forward to getting the pilot started.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments (referenced): Orchid_SLA_Redlines.pdf (pdf), Redwood_SLA-Counter_v1.docx (docx)

Also confirm whether we should add a clause limiting credit payouts to a maximum cumulative amount over the pilot term.

Quoting Ethan below for context.

-----Original Message-----
From: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
Sent: Tue, 27 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Orchid AI Procurement <procurement@orchid.ai>
Subject: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the demo last week — the platform looks great. Procurement reviewed the standard SLA you shared and we have two asks before we can move to PO: ...


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
Cc: Orchid AI Procurement <procurement@orchid.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 28 May 2025 15:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Ethan — thanks for reviewing and for the redlines. Laura and Ben reviewed and we propose the following formal counter for the pilot term: 

1) Pilot uptime anchor: Redwood will document a provisional uptime floor of 98.0% for the first 90 days starting on the go-live date. We’ll include objective validation criteria (7-day rolling availability sample across their top-5 routes) to confirm drift back to 99.5%. If Orchid meets validation, baseline reverts automatically; if not, we engage an operational remediation playbook (no automatic credit acceleration).

2) Latency carveouts: Scheduled batch windows (0100–0400 PT) will be excluded from monthly credit calculations provided Orchid supplies a weekly schedule and the windows are limited to recurring batch jobs. Ad-hoc large jobs outside those windows will remain in scope.

3) Monthly remedy cap: For the pilot period only, Redwood proposes a 4% cap on monthly remedy (a midpoint between your 2% ask and our standard exposure). Payouts remain subject to incident validation and remediation offsets. After the pilot, we revert to our standard remedy cap unless mutually amended.

4) Reporting and cadence: Redwood will send a weekly pilot health digest (PDF) and host a weekly 30-min check-in during the 90-day pilot to review anomalies, trending, and any scheduling conflicts.

Attached: updated SLA amendment (Redwood_SLA-Counter_v1.docx) and a marked copy of your redlines (Orchid_SLA_Redlines.pdf). If the 4% cap is acceptable, we’ll route the amended SLA to DocuSign and target signatures by end of week. If you prefer to discuss, I’m free Thu 10–12 PT.

Thanks and looking forward to getting the pilot started.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments (referenced): Orchid_SLA_Redlines.pdf (pdf), Redwood_SLA-Counter_v1.docx (docx)
text:
From: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Orchid AI Procurement <procurement@orchid.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the demo last week — the platform looks great. Procurement reviewed the standard SLA you shared and we have two asks before we can move to PO: 

1) Uptime anchoring. For the first 90 days of the pilot we need a provisional uptime floor at 98.0% (rather than the standard 99.5%) due to the expected burst patterns during onboarding. We understand this is temporary and would expect the baseline to re-anchor at 99.5% after the pilot.

2) Latency carveouts. Our inference workload will use scheduled large-batch jobs overnight. We need explicit carveouts for batched inference windows (daily 0100–0400 PT) so batch-induced tail latency doesn’t trigger credits.

Procurement also asked for: monthly remedy cap (we’d like a hard cap of 2% of monthly invoice) and a weekly operational report for the pilot period. I attached our redlines (Orchid_SLA_Redlines.pdf).

Can Redwood accept these pilot terms, or propose an alternative that achieves the same operational protection for Orchid?

Best,
Ethan
Ethan Ross
Head of Platform Ops, Orchid AI
ethan.ross@orchid.ai


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 10:09:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ethan — thanks for the clear note and the redlines. Short answers up front: we can accommodate a temporary pilot uptime anchor and define explicit batch windows as carveouts, but we have a few guardrails we need to propose (see below).

Proposed counterpoints:
- Pilot uptime anchor: Redwood can agree to a 98.0% provisional uptime floor for the first 90 days of the pilot, with an automatic reversion to 99.5% after successful validation. We’ll include objective pass/fail criteria tied to your traffic profile to avoid ambiguity.
- Batch latency carveouts: we can exclude scheduled batch windows (0100–0400 PT) from credit calculations provided you flag those windows in advance and they are limited to repeating nightly maintenance/batch jobs (not ad-hoc production spikes).
- Monthly remedy cap: we typically cap monthly credits but not as low as 2% for pilot customers — that would materially limit remedy viability. Proposed alternative: cap monthly remedy to 5% of invoice for the pilot, with an opt-in to lower caps post-production if you require it.
- Operational reporting: we can provide a weekly pilot health digest (uptime/latency token-level breakdowns, incidents, and KV cache hit-rate) during onboarding.

I’m looping in Ben Carter (Sales) and Laura Bennett (Legal) to align on deal mechanics and a compact SLA amendment doc. Attached is a short counter (Redwood_SLA-Counter_v1.docx) illustrating the language we'd like to use. If that looks reasonable we can finalize legal language and get a DocuSign link out this week.

Next steps: please confirm you’re comfortable with the 5% pilot cap alternative and the batch-window requirements, or propose a compromise. Happy to jump on a 30-min call if that helps.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0192

Ben/Laura — forwarding Ethan's redlines and my proposed counter. Key points for discussion:

- Pilot uptime anchor at 98.0% for 90 days is grantable. We should add explicit validation criteria: e.g., 7-day rolling p95/availability sample across their top-5 routes. If they fail to reach 99.5% after pilot, we escalate to a playbook rather than immediate credit clawbacks.
- Batch carveouts: OK to exclude nightly windows with the condition that Orchid provides a weekly schedule and we reserve right to reclassify repeated daytime spikes as production traffic.
- Remedy cap: Procurement wants 2%. I pushed back and suggested 5%. Laura, can you take a look at the counter doc and advise if we have room to accept 3% as a compromise during pilot? messages:
From: noreply@redwood.ai
To: hannah@cartwave.ai, ava.chen@redwood.ai
Date: Wed, 30 Apr 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Thanks for signing up — Hosted API evaluation request

Hi Hannah,

Thanks for signing up for a Hosted API evaluation. We've received your request and a member of our team will reach out shortly to confirm a hands-on session (30–45 min) tailored to your PLG use case.

Things you might want to have ready:
- A short list of the API flows you plan to test (generation, embeddings, reranking).
- Sample prompts or datasets (if possible).
- Any compliance constraints (data residency, logging).

If you'd like to skip waiting, you can pick a slot here: https://calendly.com/redwood/45min-hostedapi-eval

— Redwood Inference Team

[This is an automated confirmation message]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Cole <hannah@cartwave.ai>
Cc: diego.fernandez@redwood.ai
Date: Wed, 30 Apr 2025 09:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Hosted API evaluation request — quick scheduling options

Hi Hannah —

Nice to meet you (virtually) and welcome to Redwood. I'm Ava on the PLG intake team; Diego (cc) is our SDR who helps with environment provisioning.

Would any of the following work for a 30–45 minute hosted API walkthrough and sandbox test?
- Wed 30 Apr, 11:00–11:45 PDT
- Thu 1 May, 09:00–09:45 PDT
- Thu 1 May, 15:00–15:45 PDT

If none of those fit, feel free to pick a slot directly on our calendar: https://calendly.com/redwood/45min-hostedapi-eval

Agenda I'll plan for:
1) Quick intro + goals (5 min)
2) Live Hosted API demo with your sample prompts (20–25 min)
3) Sandbox access + next steps (5–10 min)

Prework: if you can paste 2–3 representative prompts and any constraints (token budgets, latency targets), that will help us prioritize what to demo.

Thanks — I'll hold the 11:00 PDT slot for a short window and will confirm once you reply.

Best,
Ava Chen
SDR Intake — Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0198

From: Hannah Cole <hannah@cartwave.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 30 Apr 2025 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Hosted API evaluation request — quick scheduling options

Thanks, Ava —

Appreciate the fast follow. The 11:00 PDT slot should work, but small note: our engineering lead will join from EST and might ask to push to 12:30 PDT / 15:30 EDT if possible.

We care mostly about:
- Low-latency generation for conversational flows (~95th percentile < 300ms)
- Embedding throughput for batch indexing
- Cost per 1k tokens and options to control spend (batching/quantization)

I'll attach a short list of sample prompts in a follow-up (team still polishing).

Hannah Cole
Product Manager — Cartwave
hannah@cartwave.ai

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Cole <hannah@cartwave.ai>
Cc: diego.fernandez@redwood.ai, grace.oconnor@redwood.ai
Date: Wed, 30 Apr 2025 10:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Hosted API evaluation request — confirmed time and sandbox access (updated)

Thanks for the heads up, Hannah — 12:30 PDT / 15:30 EDT works for us. Confirmed: Thu 1 May, 12:30–13:15 PDT (45 min).

I've included Grace from Solutions to join the technical portion. Diego will handle provisioning and will drop sandbox credentials once the slot is locked.

I've attached:
- cartwave-demo-prep-checklist.pdf (short prep + suggested prompts)
- redwood-sandbox-credentials.txt (temporary access token + sandbox URL)

Please review the checklist — important callouts:
- Sandbox is rate-limited; if you need a heavier baseline we can request a short lift (ask Diego).
- If you have strict data residency requirements, flag them early so we can route to the appropriate region.

If anything changes, reply to this thread and we'll pivot. Looking forward to it.

Best,
Ava Chen
SDR Intake — Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0198

Attachments: cartwave-demo-prep-checklist.pdf (PDF), redwood-sandbox-credentials.txt (text)

From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Hannah Cole <hannah@cartwave.ai>
Cc: grace.oconnor@redwood.ai
Date: Wed, 30 Apr 2025 16:00:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Sandbox credentials + quick ops note

Forwarding final ops note for the call.

On Wed, 30 Apr 2025 at 10:15, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> I've attached: cartwave-demo-prep-checklist.pdf, redwood-sandbox-credentials.txt
>
> NOTE for the call: the sandbox token in the creds file is scoped to 2 hours. If Cartwave wants longer test time post-demo, reply here and I will request a 24h dev lift (short SLA).
>
> Also tagging Grace to own the model/quality discussion during the live demo.

Diego Fernandez
SDR Operations — Redwood Inference
diego.fernandez@redwood.ai | +1 646-555-0133

[Attachment stubs preserved in thread]
