source.subject: Runtime LTS GA — go/no-go criteria and remaining risks
source.content:
From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 9:08 AM PT
Subject: Runtime LTS GA — go/no-go criteria and remaining risks

Team,

We need to lock GA readiness criteria for Runtime LTS (targeting end of month). This is coming up as a procurement blocker in at least one regulated deal (Northstar) and is also the internal line we’ll use for Dedicated + Private commitments.

Can each of you reply in-thread with:
1) Your explicit go/no-go criteria (what must be true to GA)
2) Top 2 risks you think could still bite us in the first 30 days post-GA
3) Who signs off for your area (so we can document it)

My proposed GA definition (draft):
- LTS branch is cut (runtime-1.18-lts) with tag scheme + patch cadence published.
- At least 2 successful patches cut in pilot OR 1 patch + 1 out-of-band security drill executed end-to-end.
- Perf gates integrated with release branch CI; regression waiver process documented.
- Artifacts are reproducible + checksums/signatures available for Private distribution.
- Support/CS playbook is live; escalation path + on-call expectations documented.

Attaching the latest checklist we’re tracking against.

Attachments:
- Runtime-LTS-GA-Readiness-Checklist_v3.pdf (PDF)

Thanks,
Ava

—
Ava Chen
Director, Engineering Program Management
Redwood Inference


From: Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 9:36 AM PT
Subject: Re: Runtime LTS GA — go/no-go criteria and remaining risks

Criteria (Release Eng):
- Branching/versioning finalized + documented (ADR-027 already approved; public docs aligned).
  - runtime-1.18-lts branch
  - patch tags like 1.18.1-lts, 1.18.2-lts
- Runbook is executable (not just aspirational): we must have completed at least one patch release using the runbook with all gates.
- Backport policy is enforced in tooling:
  - labels are in place (backport-candidate, lts-approved, security)
  - backport PR template + approval checklist
- Artifact provenance/checksums:
  - build produces checksums and provenance metadata
  - verification steps documented for Private customers

Top risks (first 30 days):
1) Perf gate flakiness blocks patch week. If we’re forced into manual judgment repeatedly, we lose credibility with enterprise.
2) Backport scope creep (Sev3 “nice to have” fixes). This is where LTS becomes “mainline but slower,” which defeats the point.

Sign-off:
- Release Eng: me (Connor)
- Runtime Exec sponsor: Mateo
- SRE readiness: Sean (or Rafael as delegate)

Attachment with pilot summary for 1.18.1-lts (includes gate results + waiver notes).

Attachments:
- LTS-Pilot-Summary_1.18.1-lts.pdf (PDF)

Connor

—
Connor O’Brien
Release Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 10:11 AM PT
Subject: Re: Runtime LTS GA — go/no-go criteria and remaining risks

SRE criteria:
- We have LTS-specific dashboards/alerts (latency + error budget) for:
  - Dedicated traffic on LTS
  - Private “phone home” telemetry where available (at minimum installer health + version reporting for pilot accounts)
- Escalation path is written down and tested once:
  - Support → SRE on-call → Runtime on-call → Release Eng
  - Severity mapping is consistent with backport rubric (security/Sev1/Sev2)
- Rollback guidance is unambiguous:
  - what customers do (pin previous patch)
  - what we do (roll back images, disable canary, etc.)

Remaining risks:
1) We don’t have enough signal during rollout of an LTS patch for Dedicated (we can monitor platform SLOs, but we need patch-level correlation in the dashboard).
2) “Mandatory patch” criteria is fuzzy. If we call too many patches mandatory, customers see churn. If we call too few mandatory, we eat incidents.

Sign-off:
- SRE: me (Sean). Rafael can act as backup approver.

One note: I’m comfortable with GA if we commit to a conservative initial stance: one patch/month, minimal diffs, and explicit perf exceptions only with Runtime+SRE sign-off.

Sean

—
Sean Gallagher
Staff SRE
Redwood Inference


From: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 10:44 AM PT
Subject: Re: Runtime LTS GA — go/no-go criteria and remaining risks

Security/compliance criteria:
- We can answer “how quickly do you patch critical vulns on LTS?” with a real SLA and an actual procedure.
  - We don’t need perfection, but we need the documented triage → fix → validate → ship path.
- Artifact integrity:
  - checksums/signatures published for LTS artifacts
  - internal key handling documented (who can sign, where the keys live)
- SBOM:
  - either generated for the runtime artifact or we have a documented plan + date (acceptable if interim is “on request” with a clear process)

Risks:
1) Over-promising on vulnerability response time. Keep language bounded to critical/high (and don’t imply 24/7 for all severities).
2) Confusion between “security patch” vs “bugfix patch.” We need to ensure release notes and comms treat these differently.

Sign-off:
- Security policy sponsor: me (Aisha)
- Vuln response coordination: Hanae (if she’s not on this thread, I can relay)

Aisha

—
Dr. Aisha Rahman
Head of Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 11:19 AM PT
Subject: Re: Runtime LTS GA — go/no-go criteria and remaining risks

Product criteria:
- We must have a customer-consumable explanation of what LTS is/isn’t:
  - stability baseline, slower-change
  - backport policy summary
  - support window + patch cadence
- Release notes must be “enterprise-grade” (structured, includes known issues + mitigations, perf notes, compatibility matrix).
- Console/Docs: at minimum, customers can find LTS release notes easily and see the current patch version + EOL date.
  (We can defer deeper Console pinning UX improvements, but we can’t ship GA with release notes buried.)

Risks:
1) Customer perception: if the first LTS patch creates a performance regression incident, GA is dead on arrival.
2) Sales/CS messaging drift (e.g., “LTS means no upgrades for a year”). We need language locked and repeated.

Sign-off:
- Product: me (Marissa)

Marissa

—
Marissa Cole
Product Lead, Runtime & Console
Redwood Inference


From: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 12:03 PM PT
Subject: Re: Runtime LTS GA — go/no-go criteria and remaining risks

Support criteria:
- Support playbook published + linked from the internal runbook:
  - intake template (what logs + repro we need)
  - severity mapping to backport eligibility
  - expected response times for “security” vs “Sev1 crash” vs “perf regression”
- A single Slack escalation channel + clear “who’s on point” during patch week.
- We’ve trained Tier 2 (30 min) on reading LTS release notes + identifying known issues vs regressions.

Risks:
1) We get a wave of “can you backport X” requests without a consistent “no” script. That will burn cycles fast.
2) Customers interpret “LTS” as “same behavior forever,” and file tickets for behavior changes that are actually bug fixes.

Sign-off:
- Support workflow owner: me

Dev

—
Dev Patel
Head of Support
Redwood Inference


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 2:27 PM PT
Subject: Re: Runtime LTS GA — go/no-go criteria and remaining risks

Adding Runtime leadership perspective.

Go/no-go criteria (Runtime):
- Stability posture is real: kernel autotune defaults frozen on LTS; experimental batching heuristic disabled/guarded.
- Scheduler regression suite is running on release branches (esp. backpressure/load-shedding scenarios).
- We have proven we can backport a Sev1 safely (KV cache compaction crash class) with minimal diff + perf validation.
- Compatibility matrix owned (Quentin) and present in release notes (at minimum: supported GPU SKUs + driver ranges, model families validated, and known incompatibilities).

Risks:
1) We accidentally ship a change that is “correct” but violates enterprise expectations (e.g., slightly different batching behavior). This needs a doc/release note guardrail.
2) Release week coordination debt: if patch cutting still depends on 8 people being awake at once, we will fail the cadence.

Sign-off:
- Runtime: me

I’m supportive of GA with two conditions:
- GA announcement language is conservative (no implied feature parity; no implied 12-month “no upgrade” promise).
- First 60 days we treat waivers as exceptional and track them publicly in the release retro.

Mateo

—
Mateo Alvarez
VP Engineering, Runtime
Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 21, 2025 at 8:42 AM PT
Subject: Re: Runtime LTS GA — go/no-go criteria and remaining risks

Thanks all — consolidating into a GA decision packet today.

Proposed GA gate checklist (based on replies):
- Release Eng (Connor): runbook-executed patch cut + enforced labels/approvals + checksums/provenance
- Runtime (Mateo): kernel/scheduler stability posture + backport of at least one Sev1-class fix + compatibility matrix ownership
- SRE (Sean): LTS dashboards/alerts + escalation path tested + rollback guidance
- Security (Aisha): security patch SLA + signing/key handling documented + SBOM plan/date
- Product (Marissa): customer-facing LTS definition + release notes taxonomy live + discoverability in docs/console
- Support (Dev): playbook + Tier 2 training + “backport request” intake template

Open items I’m tracking as potential no-go if not closed by Wed EOD:
1) Perf gate flake rate: if we can’t get consistent green runs on the canonical suite without manual reruns, we’ll slip GA.
2) Security patch comms: we need an agreed template that doesn’t over-commit.
3) Patch-level monitoring: confirm we can correlate dedicated traffic to LTS patch version in dashboards.

Next step: 30-min go/no-go meeting Monday 3/24 10:00–10:30 PT.
- Please come prepared to say “GO” or “NO-GO” against the checklist above.
- If you need a pre-read doc review, send changes to me by EOD today.

Also: Rachel/Marissa — please align on what we can safely say to Northstar this week so Sales doesn’t invent language.

Ava

—
Ava Chen
Director, Engineering Program Management
Redwood Inference

title: Runtime LTS GA — go/no-go criteria and remaining risks

source.subject: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Mar 12, 2025 at 9:14 AM PT
Subject: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

Team —

I’m putting a lightweight plan together to roll out Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO enablement to Sales/CS (materials + live trainings). Goal is to avoid “what can I promise?” ambiguity and make quoting consistent.

Proposed deliverables (drafts in Drive folder):
1) Seller cheat sheet (1–2 pages): tier summary, what’s guaranteed, burst vs committed, exclusions, how to position Standard vs Plus vs Premium.
2) Enablement deck (15–20 slides): narrative + examples + objection handling.
3) FAQ (internal): measurement basics, credits/escalations, common edge cases.

Proposed schedule:
- Fri 3/14: final review comments in
- Mon 3/17: lock v1 materials
- Wed 3/19 + Thu 3/20: live enablement sessions (recorded)
- Mon 3/24: “internal GA” of materials (posted + pinned)
- Tue 4/1: external-facing messaging can start being used in active opps (post-launch guardrails)

Asks:
- Lauren: confirm customer-facing tier names + 1–2 sentences of positioning per tier (so Sales uses consistent language).
- Finance (Laura/Rishi): confirm discount guardrails + deal desk triggers for Premium (one clear rule set).
- Neha: confirm leadership sign-off on the timeline + whether we should gate usage until 4/1.

Drive folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1rwi-dedicated-enablement-rollout
Confluence landing page (WIP): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/PROD/Dedicated+Tiers+%2B+Throughput+SLO

Attaching current drafts for convenience.

Attachments:
- Dedicated_Tiers_Cheat_Sheet_v3.pdf
- Dedicated_Pricing_and_Tiers_Enablement_Deck_v6.pptx
- Dedicated_Throughput_SLO_FAQ_v2.docx

Thanks,
Ava

—

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Mar 12, 2025 at 10:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

Looks good.

Tier names are: Standard / Plus / Premium.

Positioning (2-liners):
- Standard: “Predictable dedicated capacity for steady-state workloads. Best-effort burst with clear guardrails.”
- Plus: “Throughput floor + defined burst policy for spiky workloads. Better observability + faster response.”
- Premium: “Highest throughput floor, highest availability posture, and fastest response. Built for customer-facing / revenue-critical apps.”

One request: cheat sheet should be explicit about what *isn’t* covered (customer-side throttling, unsupported models/versions, intentional overload tests without coordination).

I’ll add comments in the deck by EOD Fri.

Lauren

—

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Mar 12, 2025 at 11:21 AM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

+1 on the plan.

For guardrails, please include a simple table on the cheat sheet:
- Standard: discount up to X% (no deal desk)
- Plus: discount up to Y% (deal desk if >Y% or non-standard payment terms)
- Premium: any discount requires deal desk, and “premium shape” commitments need Finance sign-off if they change headroom assumptions

I’m not putting numbers in email — I’ll drop them as comments in the Confluence version so we don’t have them floating around.

Also: please add a reminder that “burst” is billable and not a guarantee of infinite headroom.

Laura

—

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Mar 12, 2025 at 12:07 PM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

Adding one nuance for Premium:
- If customer asks to “lock in” a specific GPU type or region where supply is tight, we need a lead time disclaimer and a separate approval step.

I’ll add language suggestions in the FAQ (section: procurement / lead times / shape availability).

Rishi

—

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 13, 2025 at 8:36 AM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

Timeline works.

Please gate “Premium guarantees in writing” until materials are locked (Mon 3/17) and we’ve aligned on the exact SLO phrasing for order forms.

I’m fine with Sales using the narrative starting 4/1 as you propose, as long as:
1) we use the approved SLO language, and
2) we don’t promise a specific tokens/sec number outside the tier/shape tables.

Neha

—

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 14, 2025 at 3:18 PM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

Thanks all — incorporated initial feedback.

Updates:
- Cheat sheet now has a clear “What we guarantee” vs “What’s excluded” box.
- Added a “Do not say / Instead say” section for AEs (re: unlimited burst, fixed GPU promises, and tokens/sec guarantees outside of shape guidance).
- Added a short slide on “shape availability + lead times” with the approval callout.

Two open items I need help with to lock v1 Monday:
1) SLO wording: can we use the exact phrase from PM-503 / Confluence, or is there still legal review pending?
2) Credits: do we want to mention “service credits may apply” at all in enablement, or keep it to CS-only guidance?

If you can reply with preference by Monday 10am PT, I can freeze the PDFs for training.

Ava

On Thu, Mar 13, 2025 at 8:36 AM Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Timeline works.
> ...

—

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 17, 2025 at 9:41 AM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

1) Use the Confluence language (I just updated the section and linked PM-503). Legal review is in progress but we’re not changing the *meaning* — if legal tweaks exact words, we’ll rev the doc.

2) Credits: keep it high-level for Sales (“Remedies handled via Support/CS per contract; don’t negotiate in email”). Detailed policy stays internal.

Also: please highlight that the measurement is based on Redwood-side metrics (not client-side timers) and that streaming has specific accounting (we’ve fixed prior mismatch, but we should set expectations).

Lauren

—

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 4:42 PM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

Locked v1 materials.

What’s happening next:
- Posted finalized cheat sheet + deck to Confluence (pinned): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SE/Dedicated+Tier+Cheat+Sheet
- Trainings scheduled:
  - Wed 3/19 10:00–10:50am PT (Sales + SE)
  - Thu 3/20 1:00–1:50pm PT (CS + Support)
  Recordings + Q&A will be linked on the landing page.
- Internal GA: Mon 3/24 (materials are “source of truth” for quoting language)
- External usage: Tue 4/1 (OK to use tier narrative in opps; written commitments only with approved SLO language)

Notes I added based on this thread:
- “Don’t negotiate credits” guidance is in the FAQ.
- “Redwood-measured throughput; streaming accounting notes; exclusions” are all on page 1 of the cheat sheet.
- Lead time + approval callouts included for Premium / supply-constrained shapes.

If anyone wants a 10-min dry run before tomorrow’s enablement, I’m free 8:30–9:00am PT.

Ava

Ava Chen
Sales Enablement
Redwood Inference

text:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Mar 12, 2025 at 9:14 AM PT
Subject: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

Team —

I’m putting a lightweight plan together to roll out Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO enablement to Sales/CS (materials + live trainings). Goal is to avoid “what can I promise?” ambiguity and make quoting consistent.

Proposed deliverables (drafts in Drive folder):
1) Seller cheat sheet (1–2 pages): tier summary, what’s guaranteed, burst vs committed, exclusions, how to position Standard vs Plus vs Premium.
2) Enablement deck (15–20 slides): narrative + examples + objection handling.
3) FAQ (internal): measurement basics, credits/escalations, common edge cases.

Proposed schedule:
- Fri 3/14: final review comments in
- Mon 3/17: lock v1 materials
- Wed 3/19 + Thu 3/20: live enablement sessions (recorded)
- Mon 3/24: “internal GA” of materials (posted + pinned)
- Tue 4/1: external-facing messaging can start being used in active opps (post-launch guardrails)

Asks:
- Lauren: confirm customer-facing tier names + 1–2 sentences of positioning per tier (so Sales uses consistent language).
- Finance (Laura/Rishi): confirm discount guardrails + deal desk triggers for Premium (one clear rule set).
- Neha: confirm leadership sign-off on the timeline + whether we should gate usage until 4/1.

Drive folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1rwi-dedicated-enablement-rollout
Confluence landing page (WIP): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/PROD/Dedicated+Tiers+%2B+Throughput+SLO

Attaching current drafts for convenience.

Attachments:
- Dedicated_Tiers_Cheat_Sheet_v3.pdf
- Dedicated_Pricing_and_Tiers_Enablement_Deck_v6.pptx
- Dedicated_Throughput_SLO_FAQ_v2.docx

Thanks,
Ava

—

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Mar 12, 2025 at 10:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

Looks good.

Tier names are: Standard / Plus / Premium.

Positioning (2-liners):
- Standard: “Predictable dedicated capacity for steady-state workloads. Best-effort burst with clear guardrails.”
- Plus: “Throughput floor + defined burst policy for spiky workloads. Better observability + faster response.”
- Premium: “Highest throughput floor, highest availability posture, and fastest response. Built for customer-facing / revenue-critical apps.”

One request: cheat sheet should be explicit about what *isn’t* covered (customer-side throttling, unsupported models/versions, intentional overload tests without coordination).

I’ll add comments in the deck by EOD Fri.

Lauren

—

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Mar 12, 2025 at 11:21 AM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

+1 on the plan.

For guardrails, please include a simple table on the cheat sheet:
- Standard: discount up to X% (no deal desk)
- Plus: discount up to Y% (deal desk if >Y% or non-standard payment terms)
- Premium: any discount requires deal desk, and “premium shape” commitments need Finance sign-off if they change headroom assumptions

I’m not putting numbers in email — I’ll drop them as comments in the Confluence version so we don’t have them floating around.

Also: please add a reminder that “burst” is billable and not a guarantee of infinite headroom.

Laura

—

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>

- If customer asks to “lock in” a specific GPU type or region where supply is tight, we need a lead time disclaimer and a separate approval step.

I’ll add language suggestions in the FAQ (section: procurement / lead times / shape availability).

Rishi

I’m fine with Sales using the narrative starting 4/1 as you propose, as long as:

Neha

Subject: New Dedicated demo signup — BrightBridge Analytics

A new self-serve signup has requested a Dedicated capacity demo for BrightBridge Analytics.

Lead details (attached): selfserve-signup.csv
- Name: Jules Parker
- Title: Head of ML Infrastructure
- Company: BrightBridge Analytics
- Email: jules@brightbridge.ai
- Notes: Interested in reserved capacity; target latency <= 80ms p95 at 1k concurrent requests; potential POC Q2.

---
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Jules Parker <jules@brightbridge.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 14 Mar 2025 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity demo intake — BrightBridge (priority lead)

Hi Jules —

Thanks for signing up and for the detail in the form. I'm Ava from Redwood's SDR team; Ben (AE) is copied. We'd love to get a focused 45–60 minute walkthrough of our Dedicated offering and scope a potential POC.

Quick asks so we make the session useful:
- Can you confirm your target latency (p50/p95) and typical concurrent request profile? You mentioned ~1k concurrent requests in the form; is that sustained or bursty?
- Will you want a short technical deep-dive (kernel/quantization discussion) with one of our engineers?
- Time availability next week (Mon/Tues mornings PT are best for us): we can offer 30/45/60 min slots.

Proposed times (PT):
- Mon Mar 17, 9:00–9:45 or 10:30–11:15
- Tue Mar 18, 8:30–9:15 or 11:00–11:45

Best,
Ava Chen
SDR, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0137

From: Jules Parker <jules@brightbridge.ai>

Cc: BrightBridge Sales <sales@brightbridge.ai>

Thanks Ava —

Mon 10:30 PT works for us. A few details up front:

Thanks,
Jules

BrightBridge Analytics
jules@brightbridge.ai

source.subject: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 9:14 AM PT
Subject: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)

Priya — quick exec update on “Dedicated staged rollouts for reserved pools” with current milestone status + top risks/mitigations. Lauren/Marissa/Connor looping in for visibility.

Where we are (as of Mar 18)
- Control plane plan model + migrations: DONE (behind FF)
  - State machine: draft → running → held → aborted → completed
  - Promote/abort idempotency + audit hooks in place
- Routing primitives (stage-based split/partition selection): DONE for single-region; multi-region rules in final review
- Telemetry tagging for stage_id: DONE; mitigations for metrics cardinality landed (see hotfix)
- Gate evaluation v1 (policy engine): DONE for latency/error/saturation; “missing data” semantics default fail-closed w/ override
- Console: timeline + evidence endpoints DONE; UI work in progress (design ready, implementation ongoing)

Top risks & mitigations

1) Capacity fragmentation / effective throughput drop during early stages
What we’re seeing / why it matters:
- On smaller reservations, a 1% / 10% stage can create a partition that’s too small to absorb burst + streaming concurrency → queue depth spikes, 429s, and noisy gate failures.
- We saw a variant of this in beta-like testing (similar to SUP-28522): canary partition reduced effective capacity and caused unexpected 429s.

Mitigations in flight:
- Minimum stage sizing guardrail: validate plan stages against pool size (replica-based minimum where available; otherwise enforce minimum % and headroom).
- “No starvation” routing rule: if canary partition saturates, allow limited spillover to baseline partition *without* violating rollback semantics (still pins in-flight streams).
- Default stage set for Dedicated pools will be conservative for small pools (e.g., 10/50/100 instead of 1/10/50/100) unless pool size qualifies.
- Explicit pre-flight check in Console: shows projected per-stage capacity and blocks promotion if it would drop below headroom threshold.

2) False gate failures / stuck stages due to telemetry lag or noisy signals
What we’re seeing / why it matters:
- Gate eval depends on stage-tagged metrics; telemetry lag or tag mismatch can make gates “unknown” or fail-closed.
- Bursty traffic creates noisy p99 and error-rate swings over short windows → flapping gates, operator fatigue.

Mitigations in flight:
- Staleness detection: gate evaluation will surface “data stale” vs “failed” to reduce confusion; default action is hold (not auto-abort) when data is missing/stale.
- Minimum sample size + windowing: require N requests and a minimum duration before evaluating pass/fail (avoids 3 requests deciding a promotion).
- Conservative defaults for beta: 15m window for latency/sat (vs 5m) + rate-of-change constraints.
- Manual override path is admin-only with mandatory reason string; always emits audit events.

3) Rollback/abort semantics for streaming requests
Concern:
- Mid-stage abort could break in-flight streams if we hard-reroute. That’s customer-visible.

Mitigation:
- In-flight streams remain pinned to their assigned partition; abort affects new requests only. Console + runbook will call this out explicitly.

Beta readiness
- Feature flagging: scoped by account + pool; avoiding partial-region exposure (Connor coordinating release train)
- Audit logging: create/update/promote/abort/force-promote/hold events all present; export pipeline still needs verification (Connor tracking)
- Runbooks: draft exists; oncall escalation path for gate fail (page vs ticket) being finalized

Proposed beta entrance criteria (next ~2 weeks)
- Multi-region routing behavior signed off (Dedicated: region impairment mid-stage)
- Console UI: stage timeline + gate evidence panel functional end-to-end
- Load test evidence: demonstrate no >1% increase in 429s at 10% stage for pools above min size
- “Missing metrics” game-day: verify hold + manual override + audit trail

Asks / decisions
1) Confirm we’re aligned on default fail-closed for missing gate data (with hold/override), at least for beta.
2) Confirm we will block 1% stage for pools below min size and default to 10/50/100 (product-facing change).

Attachments:
- Dedicated_Staged_Rollouts_Exec_Update_2025-03-18.pdf
- beta-readiness-checklist-staged-rollouts.txt

Thanks,
Ava
—
Ava Chen
Director, Platform Programs
Redwood Inference


On Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 10:03 AM Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)

Thanks Ava.

A couple quick questions so I can relay cleanly:
1) For the “spillover” rule to prevent starvation — are we comfortable that it won’t confuse customers (“I thought 10% was canary”) and that audit/evidence remains deterministic?
2) On telemetry lag → hold: what’s the auto-timeout? I don’t want pools sitting in held state for days without anyone noticing.
3) Any specific beta design partners already confirmed / readiness level?

-Priya


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 11:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)

Answers inline.

1) Spillover rule
- We’re scoping spillover as an SRE safety valve, not a silent behavior change.
- Determinism/auditability:
  - Routing decision remains deterministic given (stage, policy version, partition saturation state).
  - We will emit a stage-level annotation + metric when spillover is active (so gate evidence + audit trails can explain “why baseline absorbed some traffic”).
- Customer messaging:
  - Console will continue to show the configured stage (e.g., 10%) but will surface a warning banner if spillover is triggered (“Canary partition saturated; overflow enabled to protect availability”).
  - For beta, we can keep spillover enabled only for Redwood operators (not customer-configurable).

2) Hold auto-timeout
- Proposal from platform/SRE threads: default hold timeout 60 minutes.
- After timeout:
  - If gate data still missing/stale → stays held but pages the owning oncall (and opens a ticket) rather than auto-promoting.
  - If data recovers → re-evaluate gates and unblock promotion (still requires explicit promote action).
- Connor will add the alert wiring to the beta checklist so we don’t miss it.

3) Beta design partners
- Lauren can confirm names in a separate note (some are sensitive), but status is:
  - 1 large Dedicated customer: eager, has prior incident history with upgrades; wants audit trail + strict gates.
  - 1 mid-market Dedicated customer: primarily cares about avoiding 429s / downtime; willing to run staged upgrades off-peak.
- Both are contingent on Console evidence panel being solid (we have a known historical issue class here: telemetry tag mismatches → empty evidence UI, so we’re baking that into the pre-beta validation).

Re: your earlier relay: we’re aligned to keep missing-data fail-closed + hold + admin override for beta. It’s the only posture that passes enterprise change-management expectations without over-automating.

Ava

- Minimum stage sizing guardrail: validate plan stages against pool size (replica-based minimum where available; otherwise enforce minimum % and headroom).
- “No starvation” routing rule: if canary partition saturates, allow limited spillover to baseline partition *without* violating rollback semantics (still pins in-flight streams).
- Default stage set for Dedicated pools will be conservative for small pools (e.g., 10/50/100 instead of 1/10/50/100) unless pool size qualifies.
- Explicit pre-flight check in Console: shows projected per-stage capacity and blocks promotion if it would drop below headroom threshold.

2) False gate failures / stuck stages due to telemetry lag or noisy signals
What we’re seeing / why it matters:
- Gate eval depends on stage-tagged metrics; telemetry lag or tag mismatch can make gates “unknown” or fail-closed.
- Bursty traffic creates noisy p99 and error-rate swings over short windows → flapping gates, operator fatigue.

Mitigations in flight:
- Staleness detection: gate evaluation will surface “data stale” vs “failed” to reduce confusion; default action is hold (not auto-abort) when data is missing/stale.
- Minimum sample size + windowing: require N requests and a minimum duration before evaluating pass/fail (avoids 3 requests deciding a promotion).
- Conservative defaults for beta: 15m window for latency/sat (vs 5m) + rate-of-change constraints.
- Manual override path is admin-only with mandatory reason string; always emits audit events.

3) Rollback/abort semantics for streaming requests
Concern:
- Mid-stage abort could break in-flight streams if we hard-reroute. That’s customer-visible.

Mitigation:
- In-flight streams remain pinned to their assigned partition; abort affects new requests only. Console + runbook will call this out explicitly.

Beta readiness
- Feature flagging: scoped by account + pool; avoiding partial-region exposure (Connor coordinating release train)
- Audit logging: create/update/promote/abort/force-promote/hold events all present; export pipeline still needs verification (Connor tracking)
- Runbooks: draft exists; oncall escalation path for gate fail (page vs ticket) being finalized

Proposed beta entrance criteria (next ~2 weeks)
- Multi-region routing behavior signed off (Dedicated: region impairment mid-stage)
- Console UI: stage timeline + gate evidence panel functional end-to-end
- Load test evidence: demonstrate no >1% increase in 429s at 10% stage for pools above min size
- “Missing metrics” game-day: verify hold + manual override + audit trail

Asks / decisions
1) Confirm we’re aligned on default fail-closed for missing gate data (with hold/override), at least for beta.
2) Confirm we will block 1% stage for pools below min size and default to 10/50/100 (product-facing change).

Attachments:
- Dedicated_Staged_Rollouts_Exec_Update_2025-03-18.pdf
- beta-readiness-checklist-staged-rollouts.txt

Thanks,
Ava
—
Ava Chen
Director, Platform Programs
Redwood Inference


On Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 10:03 AM Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)

Thanks Ava.

A couple quick questions so I can relay cleanly:
1) For the “spillover” rule to prevent starvation — are we comfortable that it won’t confuse customers (“I thought 10% was canary”) and that audit/evidence remains deterministic?
2) On telemetry lag → hold: what’s the auto-timeout? I don’t want pools sitting in held state for days without anyone noticing.
3) Any specific beta design partners already confirmed / readiness level?

-Priya


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 11:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)

Answers inline.

1) Spillover rule
- We’re scoping spillover as an SRE safety valve, not a silent behavior change.
- Determinism/auditability:
  - Routing decision remains deterministic given (stage, policy version, partition saturation state).
  - We will emit a stage-level annotation + metric when spillover is active (so gate evidence + audit trails can explain “why baseline absorbed some traffic”).
- Customer messaging:
  - Console will continue to show the configured stage (e.g., 10%) but will surface a warning banner if spillover is triggered (“Canary partition saturated; overflow enabled to protect availability”).
  - For beta, we can keep spillover enabled only for Redwood operators (not customer-configurable).

2) Hold auto-timeout
- Proposal from platform/SRE threads: default hold timeout 60 minutes.
- After timeout:
  - If gate data still missing/stale → stays held but pages the owning oncall (and opens a ticket) rather than auto-promoting.
  - If data recovers → re-evaluate gates and unblock promotion (still requires explicit promote action).
- Connor will add the alert wiring to the beta checklist so we don’t miss it.

3) Beta design partners
- Lauren can confirm names in a separate note (some are sensitive), but status is:
  - 1 large Dedicated customer: eager, has prior incident history with upgrades; wants audit trail + strict gates.
  - 1 mid-market Dedicated customer: primarily cares about avoiding 429s / downtime; willing to run staged upgrades off-peak.
- Both are contingent on Console evidence panel being solid (we have a known historical issue class here: telemetry tag mismatches → empty evidence UI, so we’re baking that into the pre-beta validation).

Re: your earlier relay: we’re aligned to keep missing-data fail-closed + hold + admin override for beta. It’s the only posture that passes enterprise change-management expectations without over-automating.

Ava

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 9:14 AM PT
Subject: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)

Priya — quick exec update on “Dedicated staged rollouts for reserved pools” with current milestone status + top risks/mitigations. Lauren/Marissa/Connor looping in for visibility.

Where we are (as of Mar 18)
- Control plane plan model + migrations: DONE (behind FF)
  - State machine: draft → running → held → aborted → completed
  - Promote/abort idempotency + audit hooks in place
- Routing primitives (stage-based split/partition selection): DONE for single-region; multi-region rules in final review
- Telemetry tagging for stage_id: DONE; mitigations for metrics cardinality landed (see hotfix)
- Gate evaluation v1 (policy engine): DONE for latency/error/saturation; “missing data” semantics default fail-closed w/ override
- Console: timeline + evidence endpoints DONE; UI work in progress (design ready, implementation ongoing)

Top risks & mitigations

1) Capacity fragmentation / effective throughput drop during early stages
What we’re seeing / why it matters:
- On smaller reservations, a 1% / 10% stage can create a partition that’s too small to absorb burst + streaming concurrency → queue depth spikes, 429s, and noisy gate failures.
- We saw a variant of this in beta-like testing (similar to SUP-28522): canary partition reduced effective capacity and caused unexpected 429s.

Mitigations in flight:
- Minimum stage sizing guardrail: validate plan stages against pool size (replica-based minimum where available; otherwise enforce minimum % and headroom).
- “No starvation” routing rule: if canary partition saturates, allow limited spillover to baseline partition *without* violating rollback semantics (still pins in-flight streams).
- Default stage set for Dedicated pools will be conservative for small pools (e.g., 10/50/100 instead of 1/10/50/100) unless pool size qualifies.
- Explicit pre-flight check in Console: shows projected per-stage capacity and blocks promotion if it would drop below headroom threshold.

2) False gate failures / stuck stages due to telemetry lag or noisy signals
What we’re seeing / why it matters:
- Gate eval depends on stage-tagged metrics; telemetry lag or tag mismatch can make gates “unknown” or fail-closed.
- Bursty traffic creates noisy p99 and error-rate swings over short windows → flapping gates, operator fatigue.

Mitigations in flight:
- Staleness detection: gate evaluation will surface “data stale” vs “failed” to reduce confusion; default action is hold (not auto-abort) when data is missing/stale.
- Minimum sample size + windowing: require N requests and a minimum duration before evaluating pass/fail (avoids 3 requests deciding a promotion).
- Conservative defaults for beta: 15m window for latency/sat (vs 5m) + rate-of-change constraints.
- Manual override path is admin-only with mandatory reason string; always emits audit events.

3) Rollback/abort semantics for streaming requests
Concern:
- Mid-stage abort could break in-flight streams if we hard-reroute. That’s customer-visible.

Mitigation:
- In-flight streams remain pinned to their assigned partition; abort affects new requests only. Console + runbook will call this out explicitly.

Beta readiness
- Feature flagging: scoped by account + pool; avoiding partial-region exposure (Connor coordinating release train)
- Audit logging: create/update/promote/abort/force-promote/hold events all present; export pipeline still needs verification (Connor tracking)
- Runbooks: draft exists; oncall escalation path for gate fail (page vs ticket) being finalized

Proposed beta entrance criteria (next ~2 weeks)
- Multi-region routing behavior signed off (Dedicated: region impairment mid-stage)
- Console UI: stage timeline + gate evidence panel functional end-to-end
- Load test evidence: demonstrate no >1% increase in 429s at 10% stage for pools above min size
- “Missing metrics” game-day: verify hold + manual override + audit trail

Asks / decisions
1) Confirm we’re aligned on default fail-closed for missing gate data (with hold/override), at least for beta.
2) Confirm we will block 1% stage for pools below min size and default to 10/50/100 (product-facing change).

Attachments:
- Dedicated_Staged_Rollouts_Exec_Update_2025-03-18.pdf
- beta-readiness-checklist-staged-rollouts.txt

Thanks,
Ava
—
Ava Chen
Director, Platform Programs
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)
Attachments: CacheTelemetrySprint_OnePager_2025-03-03.pdf; CacheMetricsCardinalityBudget_v6.xlsx

Team — requesting go/no-go today so we can start implementation tomorrow.

Proposed sprint: Prefix + KV cache telemetry + initial “increase hit-rate” recommendations MVP (internal report / minimal Console stub).

Scope (2–3 weeks):
1) Runtime instrumentation (prefix cache + KV cache):
   - hit/miss counters + partial-hit semantics (per ADR)
   - miss reason taxonomy (stable set)
   - eviction + memory pressure signals
   - wasted prefill metric (privacy-safe, bucketed)
   - sampling + local rate limiting
2) Joinability/tags from gateway/control plane:
   - model_id + model_version_id, route_id, deployment_mode, region
   - strict allowlists; no per-tenant high-cardinality labels in raw metrics
3) Ingestion + cardinality controls:
   - enforce label policy + drop/aggregate forbidden label combos
   - sampling strategy to keep cost stable
4) Dashboards/alerts + runbook:
   - SRE dashboards for triage and regression detection
   - guardrail SLO proposal (internal; no customer SLA)
5) Recommendations MVP:
   - daily batch job that produces per-route/actionable levers (cache bytes/TTL/policy/routing locality/prompt canonicalization)

Key risks (need explicit sign-off):
- Metric cardinality/cost blowup if labels drift (route/model/version alone can be high)
- Runtime overhead: must be near-zero on p95/p99
- Privacy: no raw prompts; only bucketed lengths + hashed/canonicalized features
- Cross-system join correctness (model version IDs, route IDs)

Acceptance gates (proposal):
- Perf canary: p95 latency regression <0.5% at default sampling; no measurable throughput drop
- Cardinality: within budget in attached sheet; ingestion drops/guards tested in staging
- Data QA: dashboards show sane counts across Hosted + Dedicated canary; missing-tag rate <1% on canary

Owners (draft):
- Runtime instrumentation: Omar (lead), Samir (impl), Noah (review), Sofia (approval)
- Platform tags/join keys: Ethan + Amara
- Ingestion/cardinality: Logan (lead) + Nadia + Caleb
- Dashboards/alerts/runbook: Allison (lead) + Sergio + Aisha; approval: Sean
- Perf/overhead: Marcus (owner)
- Rollout/canary: Connor
- Program mgmt: Talia

If approved, I’d like a single-thread “go” from each of you (Runtime, Platform/Telemetry, Reliability/SRE) + any hard constraints we need to bake into the plan.

Thanks,
Ava
CTO, Redwood Inference


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:34 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Go from Runtime.

Hard constraints from our side:
- Instrumentation MUST be behind config flags, default on only where we’ve validated overhead (staging/canary first).
- Streaming retries + continuous batching semantics have to be correct (no double counting). We’ve been burned here.
- Miss reason taxonomy needs to be stable; don’t iterate labels every other day or SRE dashboards will churn.

Omar is set as lead; Noah will review the final metric definitions vs the ADR before we flip anything beyond canary.

Mateo
VP Engineering, Runtime


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 10:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Go from Platform/Telemetry with two conditions:

1) Cardinality policy is non-negotiable:
   - allowlist labels only; no “free-form route name”, no tenant_id, no request_id
   - route_id/model_version_id must be normalized (single source of truth from control plane)
   - prompt-length/token-count must be bucketed (per standards)

2) Ingestion needs an explicit “drop detection” signal.
   We need to know when guards are dropping series so dashboards don’t show false “0 hits”. Please make that part of ENG-4823/4825 acceptance.

Logan/Nadia will own schema enforcement; Caleb will implement the guardrails.

Priya
VP Engineering, Platform


---

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 10:29 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Go from Reliability.

A few guardrails:
- Please treat this as an operational change. Rollout needs canary gates + rollback plan (Connor + Sean to sign off before prod enablement).
- I want “telemetry pipeline health” on the same dashboard page as cache hit-rate. When data is missing, we need a clear indicator.
- Any p99 regression >1% at canary should block rollout unless explicitly approved by Runtime + SRE.

If we hit these gates, I’m comfortable proceeding.

Neha Kapoor
VP Infrastructure & Reliability


---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 11:11 AM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

+1 go from SRE.

Specific SRE asks (so we don’t page ourselves to death):
- Alerts must be “guardrail” and avoid paging on workload mix shifts. Start with ticket-level alerts and promote to page only after we see stable baselines.
- Add an alert on “hit-rate appears to be zero because metrics are missing” (drop detection + missing-tag).
- Runbook must include:
  - how to validate join keys (route_id/model_version_id)
  - how to distinguish real regression vs telemetry failure
  - rollback steps and who owns each step

Allison will drive dashboards + runbook; I’ll approve alert policy before prod.

Sean
Director, Site Reliability Engineering


---

From: Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 12:03 PM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Thanks all.

From Product side: +1 to proceed. The minimal requirements for the “recommendations MVP” slice are:
- Output is per route + model_version + region (at minimum) and includes a plain-English “why” with suggested levers.
- Must include caveats when confidence is low (e.g., insufficient sample, high workload volatility).
- We should explicitly avoid any recommendation that implies logging prompt content.

If we can land a first internal report + 1–2 example recommendations validated by CS, that’s a win for this sprint.

Paula
Sr PM, Telemetry Requirements / Rollouts


---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 2:06 PM PT
Subject: Re: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

Approved — thank you.

Decisions captured:
- Proceed with the sprint starting tomorrow.
- Must ship behind flags; staging -> canary -> prod only with gates.
- Cardinality policy enforced via allowlists + ingestion guards; add explicit drop-detection metrics.
- Perf gates: p95 <0.5% regression at default sampling; p99 >1% at canary blocks unless Runtime+SRE approve.
- Alerts start ticket-level; page only after baseline stabilization.
- Runbook + telemetry pipeline health indicator are required deliverables.

Talia: please set up a daily 15-min leadership checkpoint this week only (Mon–Fri) to keep decision latency low.

Connor/Allison/Logan/Omar: please reply-all by EOD with the concrete rollout timeline (staging date, canary start, target prod date) + explicit rollback owner.

Ava
CTO, Redwood Inference

title: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)

- Data QA: dashboards show sane counts across Hosted + Dedicated canary; missing-tag rate <1% on canary

Owners (draft):
- Runtime instrumentation: Omar (lead), Samir (impl), Noah (review), Sofia (approval)
- Platform tags/join keys: Ethan + Amara
- Ingestion/cardinality: Logan (lead) + Nadia + Caleb
- Dashboards/alerts/runbook: Allison (lead) + Sergio + Aisha; approval: Sean
- Perf/overhead: Marcus (owner)
- Rollout/canary: Connor
- Program mgmt: Talia

If approved, I’d like a single-thread “go” from each of you (Runtime, Platform/Telemetry, Reliability/SRE) + any hard constraints we need to bake into the plan.

Thanks,
Ava
CTO, Redwood Inference

text:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Omar El-Sayed <omar.elsayed@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia.benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Cache hit-rate instrumentation sprint — go/no-go (prefix/KV cache telemetry + recommendations MVP)
Attachments: CacheTelemetrySprint_OnePager_2025-03-03.pdf; CacheMetricsCardinalityBudget_v6.xlsx

Team — requesting go/no-go today so we can start implementation tomorrow.

Proposed sprint: Prefix + KV cache telemetry + initial “increase hit-rate” recommendations MVP (internal report / minimal Console stub).

Scope (2–3 weeks):
1) Runtime instrumentation (prefix cache + KV cache):
   - hit/miss counters + partial-hit semantics (per ADR)
   - miss reason taxonomy (stable set)
   - eviction + memory pressure signals
   - wasted prefill metric (privacy-safe, bucketed)
   - sampling + local rate limiting
2) Joinability/tags from gateway/control plane:
   - model_id + model_version_id, route_id, deployment_mode, region
   - strict allowlists; no per-tenant high-cardinality labels in raw metrics
3) Ingestion + cardinality controls:
   - enforce label policy + drop/aggregate forbidden label combos
   - sampling strategy to keep cost stable
4) Dashboards/alerts + runbook:
   - SRE dashboards for triage and regression detection
   - guardrail SLO proposal (internal; no customer SLA)
5) Recommendations MVP:
   - daily batch job that produces per-route/actionable levers (cache bytes/TTL/policy/routing locality/prompt canonicalization)

Key risks (need explicit sign-off):
- Metric cardinality/cost blowup if labels drift (route/model/version alone can be high)
- Runtime overhead: must be near-zero on p95/p99
- Privacy: no raw prompts; only bucketed lengths + hashed/canonicalized features
- Cross-system join correctness (model version IDs, route IDs)

Acceptance gates (proposal):
- Perf canary: p95 latency regression <0.5% at default sampling; no measurable throughput drop
- Cardinality: within budget in attached sheet; ingestion drops/guards tested in staging
- Data QA: dashboards show sane counts across Hosted + Dedicated canary; missing-tag rate <1% on canary

Owners (draft):
- Runtime instrumentation: Omar (lead), Samir (impl), Noah (review), Sofia (approval)
- Platform tags/join keys: Ethan + Amara
- Ingestion/cardinality: Logan (lead) + Nadia + Caleb
- Dashboards/alerts/runbook: Allison (lead) + Sergio + Aisha; approval: Sean
- Perf/overhead: Marcus (owner)
- Rollout/canary: Connor
- Program mgmt: Talia

If approved, I’d like a single-thread “go” from each of you (Runtime, Platform/Telemetry, Reliability/SRE) + any hard constraints we need to bake into the plan.

Thanks,
Ava
CTO, Redwood Inference


---

source.subject: Public docs: Quantization Profiles (route-level enablement) — review before beta
attachments: Quantization Profiles - Beta Docs Review Checklist (Google Doc link)
Screenshot - Console route settings warning copy.png
source.body:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 27, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Public docs: Quantization Profiles (route-level enablement) — review before beta

Hi all —

We’re in final review on the public docs for the Quantization Profile Library (route-level opt-in) ahead of the beta announcement.

Docs PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/redwood-docs/pull/18570
SDK examples:
- Python: https://github.com/redwood-inference/redwood-sdk-python/pull/18574
- TypeScript: https://github.com/redwood-inference/redwood-sdk-typescript/pull/18579

What I need from this group by EOD Fri (2/28):
1) Wording sign-off for “opt-in” + what changes when enabled (and what does NOT).
2) Rollback messaging: route-level disable + global kill-switch (and how we phrase it publicly).
3) Support guidance alignment: what we ask customers to do first if they see output differences or latency changes.

Draft checklist (Google Doc link, for comments): Quantization Profiles - Beta Docs Review Checklist
Attachment stub: Screenshot - Console route settings warning copy.png

Key sections to review (in PR):
- “Enabling a quantization profile on a route”
- “Compatibility and fallbacks”
- “Evaluating quality before rollout”
- “Troubleshooting / rollback”

Goal is to merge Monday morning (3/3) to make the beta email + Console help center consistent.

Thanks,
Alex
Developer Experience
Redwood Inference

---

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 27, 2025 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Public docs: Quantization Profiles (route-level enablement) — review before beta

Reviewed the Console-related bits.

Two requests so docs match the UX we’re shipping:
1) Please avoid promising “expected savings” in the docs. Console will show an *estimate* but only for models with benchmark coverage. Suggest phrasing: “You may see cost-per-token improvements; actual results depend on prompt distribution, context length, and hardware.”
2) Compatibility warning language: in Console we show “This profile may be incompatible with your deployment (GPU / max context / model variant). If enabled, Redwood will fall back to baseline precision.”

Also +1 to keeping the toggle as per-route only (no tenant default) in the docs for v1.

Dylan
Console & Tooling

---

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 27, 2025 at 11:18 AM PST
Subject: Re: Public docs: Quantization Profiles (route-level enablement) — review before beta

+1 overall. From release/ops POV, please make these explicit:

- Default behavior unchanged unless the profile is enabled on a route.
- Rollback path:
  a) disable quantization_profile_ref on the route (immediate)
  b) if widespread issue, platform kill-switch disables profiles globally

On kill-switch wording: I’d keep it high-level (“Redwood may disable profiles globally in emergencies”) and keep the operational details internal.

Also: mention audit/change history exists (who toggled, when) since we know Falconbank asked for it.

Connor
SRE / Release Engineering

---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 27, 2025 at 12:02 PM PST
Subject: Re: Public docs: Quantization Profiles (route-level enablement) — review before beta

A few leadership-level edits:

1) Please do not say “no quality regression” anywhere. We should say “designed to preserve quality” and explicitly recommend customers run A/B evals on their own prompt sets.

2) “Recommended/latest” alias semantics: we should not imply it auto-upgrades without customer action. If the system supports aliases, we need language that sets expectation that profiles are versioned and customers can pin versions.

3) Add a short section: “When NOT to enable” (regulated workloads without evaluation, prompts sensitive to formatting, ultra-low latency routes until validated).

Otherwise looks on track.

Ava
Executive Sponsor

---

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 27, 2025 at 2:47 PM PST
Subject: Re: Public docs: Quantization Profiles (route-level enablement) — review before beta

Agree with Ava.

One more product nuance: beta is allowlisted + opt-in per route. Please ensure the docs say:
- Beta availability is limited (contact support / your account team)
- We publish profiles per model variant (not “all models”)

And please add a “Known limitations (v1)” callout:
- No automatic per-customer recommendation
- No default-on enablement
- Not all deployments/regions have identical profile coverage yet

Aditya

---

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 28, 2025 at 9:31 AM PST
Subject: Re: Public docs: Quantization Profiles (route-level enablement) — review before beta

Thanks — I pushed a round of updates this morning based on the feedback:

Changes in PR (latest commit):
- Removed “expected savings” language; replaced with “may reduce cost per token” + factors.
- Updated compatibility wording to match Console: “may be incompatible… will fall back to baseline precision.”
- Replaced any “no quality regression” with “designed to preserve quality” + explicit A/B eval recommendation.

source.subject: Re: Quantization Profiles: risk review + kill-switch proposal for beta
source.body:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 21, 2025 at 8:17 AM PT
Subject: Quantization Profiles: risk review + kill-switch proposal for beta

Ava,

Ahead of next week’s beta enablement with the design partners, we did a quick risk review on the Quantization Profile Library rollout path.

Top risks we want to explicitly guard:
1) Quality regressions (offline evals pass but edge-case prompts regress).
2) Incompatibility (profile enabled on a route but traffic lands on unsupported GPU SKU / context length / model variant).
3) Control plane or registry load issues causing elevated 5xx (thundering herd on profile metadata fetch).

Proposal (what we need leadership approval on):
- Add an emergency global kill-switch that forces baseline behavior (ignore any route-level profile refs) across hosted + dedicated + private control planes.
- Make the kill-switch audited + RBAC-restricted (SRE oncall + release engineering), with documented runbook.
- Gate beta rollout with explicit guardrails:
  - compatibility checks in policy engine (deterministic) w/ safe fallback to baseline
  - per-route enablement only (no tenant defaults)
  - canary: 1% → 10% → 50% traffic steps, with rollback criteria
  - dashboards: enabled rate, fallback rate, error rate, p95 latency, cost/token deltas

If approved today, we can keep the release train timing.

Attachments:
- QuantProfiles_RiskReview_v3.pdf
- QuantProfiles_RolloutGuardrails_Checklist_v1.docx

Connor
Connor O’Brien
Release / Rollout Lead, Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 21, 2025 at 8:34 AM PT
Subject: Re: Quantization Profiles: risk review + kill-switch proposal for beta

Thanks.

Before I sign off:
- What are the exact semantics of the kill-switch? (Does it override everything, including “recommended/latest” aliases?)
- Who can flip it, and how fast does it propagate?
- What’s the blast radius on private deployments (VPC/on-prem)? We need to be clear about what we can/can’t enforce.

Also: do we have a firm stance that incompatibility must *never* error a request (only fallback), unless the customer explicitly asks for hard-fail?

Ava


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 21, 2025 at 8:57 AM PT
Subject: Re: Quantization Profiles: risk review + kill-switch proposal for beta

Jumping in on semantics + controls from Console/tooling side.

Kill-switch semantics (what we’re implementing in Console/backend):
- When kill-switch is ON, resolved profile outcome is always “disabled_by_killswitch” and runtime uses baseline precision. Route config remains intact (we don’t mutate customers’ saved settings).
- We still record the route’s configured ref in audit logs, but we annotate resolution outcome so Support/SRE can explain why it didn’t apply.

RBAC/audit:
- Console will not expose the kill-switch to customers.
- Internal control should be restricted to SRE oncall and release engineering only.
- Every state change emits an audit event (actor, reason, timestamp, previous→new) and lands in our audit export.

Propagation:
- Once flipped, we rely on config distribution (same path as feature flags). Connor can confirm expected propagation window, but we should assume a few minutes worst case.

On incompatibility:
- My strong preference is default to fallback-not-fail in v1 (less customer-visible breakage). If we want hard-fail, it should be an explicit route policy, not default behavior.

Dylan
Dylan Brooks
Console & Tooling Sponsor, Redwood Inference


From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 21, 2025 at 9:18 AM PT
Subject: Re: Quantization Profiles: risk review + kill-switch proposal for beta

Answering the outstanding items.

Kill-switch semantics:
- Yes, it overrides everything (including any aliases). The resolver returns “baseline” regardless of configured profile ref.
- This is intentionally conservative. If we’re in a bad state (quality regression / incompatibility bug / registry incident), we want one lever.

Who can flip it:
- Proposed: SRE oncall + Release Eng (Connor’s rotation). No other roles.
- We’ll require a reason string in the change request (for audit trail + incident tying).

Propagation:
- Hosted + Dedicated: propagated via control plane config cache; typical < 60s, worst-case 5 min.
- Private (VPC / on-prem): depends on customer’s update cadence. We can ship the capability, but we cannot force-enable kill-switch state unless they are connected to our control plane. We should message “kill-switch available” rather than “kill-switch enforced.”

Incompatibility stance:
- Agree with Dylan: v1 default is fallback-not-fail.
- We’ll still surface a warning / error on *enable* (Console/API) if incompatible is detected for the route’s declared deployment target.
- At request time: if a request lands on unsupported hardware/context, policy engine will resolve “fallback_to_baseline” and increment fallback_rate.

Why we need this: we’ve already seen early signals that two failure modes look the same to customers (“model got worse” vs “system is down”). Kill-switch helps separate safety from availability.

Connor


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 21, 2025 at 10:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Quantization Profiles: risk review + kill-switch proposal for beta

+1 to proceeding with the kill-switch and the guardrails.

Conditions I want baked into the beta launch gates:
- No default-on anywhere. Explicit route-level opt-in only.
- Deterministic resolution: same inputs (route config + model id + hardware class + request constraints) → same result, with outcome logged.
- Canary criteria written down in the checklist and in the oncall runbook (with specific thresholds for fallback_rate and 5xx).
- Confirm we’re protected against registry load spikes (cache + rate limiting). We can’t repeat last month’s “metadata fetch retry storm.”

Ava — if you’re aligned, I’m comfortable approving this plan today.

Aditya
Aditya Rao
VP Engineering, Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 21, 2025 at 11:36 AM PT
Subject: Re: Quantization Profiles: risk review + kill-switch proposal for beta

Approved to proceed with:
- global kill-switch (override-all, audit + RBAC restricted as described)
- fallback-not-fail as the default runtime behavior in v1
- staged rollout guardrails (canary steps + explicit rollback criteria)

Please ensure the public/customer messaging is consistent: “opt-in per route,” “safe fallback to baseline,” and “clear rollback path.”

Connor: update the rollout checklist with the propagation caveat for Private deployments (we offer the mechanism, but enforcement depends on their control plane connection/update).

Dylan: make sure audit log entries are readable enough for security reviewers (actor + route + previous/new ref + resolution outcome).

Aditya: thanks — aligned on the conditions.

Ava
Ava Chen
Executive Sponsor, Redwood Inference
