3) Control plane or registry load issues causing elevated 5xx (thundering herd on profile metadata fetch).

Proposal (what we need leadership approval on):
- Add an emergency global kill-switch that forces baseline behavior (ignore any route-level profile refs) across hosted + dedicated + private control planes.
- Make the kill-switch audited + RBAC-restricted (SRE oncall + release engineering), with documented runbook.
- Gate beta rollout with explicit guardrails:
  - compatibility checks in policy engine (deterministic) w/ safe fallback to baseline
  - per-route enablement only (no tenant defaults)
  - canary: 1% → 10% → 50% traffic steps, with rollback criteria
  - dashboards: enabled rate, fallback rate, error rate, p95 latency, cost/token deltas

If approved today, we can keep the release train timing.

Attachments:
- QuantProfiles_RiskReview_v3.pdf
- QuantProfiles_RolloutGuardrails_Checklist_v1.docx

Connor
Connor O’Brien
Release / Rollout Lead, Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 21, 2025 at 8:34 AM PT
Subject: Re: Quantization Profiles: risk review + kill-switch proposal for beta

Thanks.

Before I sign off:
- What are the exact semantics of the kill-switch? (Does it override everything, including “recommended/latest” aliases?)
- Who can flip it, and how fast does it propagate?
- What’s the blast radius on private deployments (VPC/on-prem)? We need to be clear about what we can/can’t enforce.

Also: do we have a firm stance that incompatibility must *never* error a request (only fallback), unless the customer explicitly asks for hard-fail?

Ava


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 21, 2025 at 8:57 AM PT
Subject: Re: Quantization Profiles: risk review + kill-switch proposal for beta

Jumping in on semantics + controls from Console/tooling side.

Kill-switch semantics (what we’re implementing in Console/backend):
- When kill-switch is ON, resolved profile outcome is always “disabled_by_killswitch” and runtime uses baseline precision. Route config remains intact (we don’t mutate customers’ saved settings).
- We still record the route’s configured ref in audit logs, but we annotate resolution outcome so Support/SRE can explain why it didn’t apply.

RBAC/audit:
- Console will not expose the kill-switch to customers.
- Internal control should be restricted to SRE oncall and release engineering only.
- Every state change emits an audit event (actor, reason, timestamp, previous→new) and lands in our audit export.

Propagation:
- Once flipped, we rely on config distribution (same path as feature flags). Connor can confirm expected propagation window, but we should assume a few minutes worst case.

On incompatibility:
- My strong preference is default to fallback-not-fail in v1 (less customer-visible breakage). If we want hard-fail, it should be an explicit route policy, not default behavior.

Dylan
Dylan Brooks
Console & Tooling Sponsor, Redwood Inference


From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 21, 2025 at 9:18 AM PT
Subject: Re: Quantization Profiles: risk review + kill-switch proposal for beta

Answering the outstanding items.

Kill-switch semantics:
- Yes, it overrides everything (including any aliases). The resolver returns “baseline” regardless of configured profile ref.
- This is intentionally conservative. If we’re in a bad state (quality regression / incompatibility bug / registry incident), we want one lever.

Who can flip it:
- Proposed: SRE oncall + Release Eng (Connor’s rotation). No other roles.
- We’ll require a reason string in the change request (for audit trail + incident tying).

Propagation:
- Hosted + Dedicated: propagated via control plane config cache; typical < 60s, worst-case 5 min.
- Private (VPC / on-prem): depends on customer’s update cadence. We can ship the capability, but we cannot force-enable kill-switch state unless they are connected to our control plane. We should message “kill-switch available” rather than “kill-switch enforced.”

Incompatibility stance:
- Agree with Dylan: v1 default is fallback-not-fail.
- We’ll still surface a warning / error on *enable* (Console/API) if incompatible is detected for the route’s declared deployment target.
- At request time: if a request lands on unsupported hardware/context, policy engine will resolve “fallback_to_baseline” and increment fallback_rate.

Why we need this: we’ve already seen early signals that two failure modes look the same to customers (“model got worse” vs “system is down”). Kill-switch helps separate safety from availability.

Connor


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 21, 2025 at 10:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Quantization Profiles: risk review + kill-switch proposal for beta

+1 to proceeding with the kill-switch and the guardrails.

Conditions I want baked into the beta launch gates:
- No default-on anywhere. Explicit route-level opt-in only.
- Deterministic resolution: same inputs (route config + model id + hardware class + request constraints) → same result, with outcome logged.
- Canary criteria written down in the checklist and in the oncall runbook (with specific thresholds for fallback_rate and 5xx).
- Confirm we’re protected against registry load spikes (cache + rate limiting). We can’t repeat last month’s “metadata fetch retry storm.”

Ava — if you’re aligned, I’m comfortable approving this plan today.

Aditya
Aditya Rao
VP Engineering, Redwood Inference

Date: Fri, Feb 21, 2025 at 11:36 AM PT

- staged rollout guardrails (canary steps + explicit rollback criteria)

Please ensure the public/customer messaging is consistent: “opt-in per route,” “safe fallback to baseline,” and “clear rollback path.”

Connor: update the rollout checklist with the propagation caveat for Private deployments (we offer the mechanism, but enforcement depends on their control plane connection/update).

Dylan: make sure audit log entries are readable enough for security reviewers (actor + route + previous/new ref + resolution outcome).

Aditya: thanks — aligned on the conditions.

Ava
Ava Chen
Executive Sponsor, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Re: Redwood Inference security review: retention & deletion timelines
source.body:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samir Desai <samir.desai@northpeakbank.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 18, 2025 at 4:12 PM
Subject: Re: Redwood Inference security review: retention & deletion timelines

Samir  answering inline below.

1) Prompts/outputs retention: For Hosted API and Dedicated, we do not persistently store prompts and model outputs by default.
2) Request logs retention: We retain operational request logs (metadata only) for 14 days by default.
3) Debug payload logging: If you explicitly enable it for troubleshooting, retention is 7 days.
4) Audit logs: retained for 12 months.
5) Deletion requests: once validated, we complete deletion within 30 days for data stored in Redwood-managed systems.

Happy to jump on a quick call if helpful.

 Ava

--
Ava Chen
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference


On Tue, Feb 18, 2025 at 12:55 PM Samir Desai <samir.desai@northpeakbank.com> wrote:
> Thanks  can you confirm specific timeframes (days/months) for request logs, audit logs, and any payload logging?
> Also confirm deletion SLAs if we request deletion.


From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 19, 2025 at 9:08 AM
Subject: Re: NorthPeak  retention language

Ava  please use the following numbers verbatim for the questionnaire:
- 14 days: operational request logs (metadata only)
- 7 days: debug payload logging if enabled
- 365 days: audit logs
- 30 days: deletion completion after request validation

Also note: for Private, retention is customer-controlled except Redwood control-plane logs.


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 20, 2025 at 9:42 AM
Subject: Re: Redwood Inference security review: retention & deletion timelines

One nuance: for Private we also retain Redwood control-plane access logs for 90 days (support access). Keep audit logs at 365 days.


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: IT Security Reviews <security-review@northpeakbank.com>
Cc: Samir Desai <samir.desai@northpeakbank.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 20, 2025 at 10:05 AM
Subject: Re: Redwood Inference security review: retention & deletion timelines

Adding one clarification for Private deployments: customer-controlled retention in the customer environment; Redwood retains control-plane audit logs for 365 days and control-plane access logs for 90 days.

source.subject: Re: Globex - Audit log completeness question (Redwood Private)
source.body:
From: Megan Stiles <megan.stiles@globex.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Arjun Patel <arjun.patel@globex.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 18, 2025 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Globex - Audit log completeness question (Redwood Private)

Hi Ava,

Were in the middle of an internal compliance review for our Redwood Private deployment. We noticed the audit export appears to be missing some administrative actions (role changes + key creation) from last week.

Can you confirm whether Redwood has had any known issues with audit log completeness in Private/VPC installs?

Attached: Globex_AuditTrail_Questions.xlsx

Thanks,
Megan
Director, Security Assurance
Globex

---

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Megan Stiles <megan.stiles@globex.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia_ivanova@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 18, 2025 at 10:01 AM
Subject: Re: Globex - Audit log completeness question (Redwood Private)

Hi Megan,

Thanks for flaggingwere looking into this. Could you share 21 specific examples (timestamp in UTC + action type) and what sink youre using for audit export (S3, HTTP, SIEM connector)?

Looping in Support (Sofia) to track as an escalation.

Best,
Ava

---

From: Arjun Patel <arjun.patel@globex.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Megan Stiles <megan.stiles@globex.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 18, 2025 at 11:26 AM
Subject: Re: Globex - Audit log completeness question (Redwood Private)

Example: 2025-02-11T22:14:03Z added user to Admins in Console. Not present in our exported events. Sink is HTTP push into our collector.

We also saw a second gap around 22:05-22:25Z (counts dont match).

---

From: Sofia Ivanova <sofia_ivanova@redwoodinference.com>
To: Megan Stiles <megan.stiles@globex.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>, Hiro Tanaka <hiro_tanaka@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 18, 2025 at 1:03 PM
Subject: Re: Globex - Audit log completeness question (Redwood Private)

Hi Megan,

We have identified a scenario where audit events could be dropped under sustained backpressure in certain Private deployments. Engineering has a mitigation and a hotfix in progress.

If youre able to confirm your control plane version and the timeframe of the gaps, we can advise on the safest mitigation for your environment.

Well share a written summary once our incident review is published.

Regards,
Sofia Ivanova
Customer Support
Redwood Inference

---

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Megan Stiles <megan.stiles@globex.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 18, 2025 at 2:47 PM
Subject: Re: Globex - Audit log completeness question (Redwood Private)

Megan  confirming this is a known issue were actively addressing. Well coordinate with your team on applying the mitigation/hotfix and provide an incident write-up suitable for audit.

Thank you,
Ava
VP, Customer Success
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)
source.body:
From: Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 9:14 AM PST
Subject: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

Hi all — requesting final security/compliance approval on the permissions + audit approach for Incident Bot v2 before we merge the remaining PRs and enable beta for SRE rotation.

Summary of proposed model (details in attached PDF + scope matrix):

1) Service identity + scopes (least privilege)
- Bot runs as service identity: svc-incident-bot
- Two new internal scopes:
  - incident_bot:config_diff:read (customer-safe, redacted output only)
  - incident_bot:telemetry:read_privileged (bounded query templates; tenant-aware guardrails)
- No write scopes. No direct access to raw secret stores.

2) Config diff endpoint access
- Endpoint returns ONLY allowlisted fields by default; everything else is either omitted or redacted.
- Redaction layer runs server-side (not in Slack bot) to prevent leakage if Slack rendering changes.
- For Sev0/Sev1 only: break-glass path allows on-call to request an expanded diff view (still redacted for secrets, but includes more non-secret fields).
  - This requires incident_id + reason, plus elevated on-call role.

3) Privileged telemetry access
- Query service exposes template-based queries (no arbitrary PromQL/SQL).
- Hard limits:
  - time window max 30m (defaults to 15m)
  - max series/rows per query (bounded)
  - per-incident caching + coalescing
  - rate limits + circuit breaker during upstream slowness

4) Audit logging
- Audit event emitted for:
  - every config diff request (including tenant/service identifiers + incident_id)
  - every privileged telemetry request (including template id + bounds)
  - every break-glass elevation attempt (approved/denied)
- Audit events go to SIEM pipeline with retention aligned to current internal standard.

Open items we want sign-off on today:
- Is the break-glass flow acceptable as described (Sev0/Sev1 only, logged, time-limited)?
- Are the audit event fields sufficient for later review/investigation?

Attachments:
- incident-bot-v2-permissions-audit-approval-summary-v3.pdf
- incident-bot-scope-matrix-v2.xlsx

If approved, Ava will update ADR-013 with final language and we’ll proceed with merges for PR-29133 / PR-29140.

Thanks,
Kaitlyn
Authn/Authz & Tenant Isolation
Redwood Inference


---

From: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

Approved from Audit Logging / SIEM side with 2 conditions:

1) Ensure audit events include immutable identifiers
- actor_type=service
- actor_id=svc-incident-bot (and any delegated user for break-glass)
- incident_id (required)
- tenant_id OR “global”
- resource_type (config_diff | telemetry)
- resource_id (e.g., config namespace + env; telemetry template id)
- decision (allowed/denied)

2) Retention + review hooks
- Route these events into the existing audit stream (same retention policy as other privileged reads).
- Add a weekly automated report for break-glass usage (count + incident_id + approver), at least during beta.

Break-glass flow as written (Sev0/Sev1, time-limited, fully logged) is acceptable.

If you want, I can help review the event schema in PR-29133 before merge.

Kevin
Audit Logging / SIEM Integration
Redwood Inference


---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:47 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

+1 to Kevin’s conditions.

Security approval granted for the permissioning approach with the following notes to capture in ADR-013:

- Redaction must be enforced server-side for config diffs (you already called this out) and covered by unit tests (negative tests for known secret patterns and a small allowlist test).
- For telemetry: keep the template list small for beta (avoid “footgun” templates that explode cardinality) and require explicit bounds.
- For break-glass: require an incident severity field (Sev0/Sev1) and make the time-limited role grant explicit (e.g., expires in 60 minutes).

No objections to moving forward.

Marcus Lin
Security Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 12:03 PM PST
Subject: Re: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

Thanks all — capturing this as final approval to proceed.

Decision:
- Approved to ship Incident Bot v2 with the proposed least-privilege scopes for config diff + privileged telemetry.
- Break-glass is approved for Sev0/Sev1 only, time-limited, and fully audited.

Conditions (will be tracked as merge blockers for beta):
1) Audit event schema includes the identifiers Kevin listed, plus decision=allowed/denied.
2) Add a weekly break-glass usage report during beta.
3) ADR-013 updated to explicitly state:
   - server-side redaction + test coverage requirements
   - telemetry template approach + bounded query constraints
   - break-glass grant expiry (60 minutes) and severity requirement

Next steps:
- Kaitlyn/Ethan: incorporate notes into ADR-013 today.
- Kevin: please take a pass on PR-29133 event fields before we merge.
- Rafael: once merged, we’ll greenlight SRE beta cohort behind the feature flag.

Related work:
- INT-3312 (bot missing scope) should be resolved by the scope rollout + audit events.

Ava
Platform Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Re: Private deployment security review  audit log export to customer bucket
source.body:
From: Morgan Lee <morgan.lee@northstarbank.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: IT Security Review <security-review@northstarbank.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 8, 2025 at 5:12 PM
Subject: Private deployment security review  audit log export to customer bucket

Hi Ava,

For our Private deployment we need audit logs exported to our AWS account (S3). Our requirements:
- customer-owned bucket
- encryption at rest (KMS)
- no long-lived access keys
- predictable object paths for retention + ingestion

Can Redwood support this? If yes, please share the auth model and a sample bucket policy.

Thanks,
Morgan

---
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Morgan Lee <morgan.lee@northstarbank.com>
Cc: IT Security Review <security-review@northstarbank.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Feb 9, 2025 at 9:05 AM
Subject: Re: Private deployment security review  audit log export to customer bucket

Hi Morgan,

Yes  we are implementing audit log export from Redwood Private to customer-owned object storage. For AWS we will use AssumeRole (short-lived) with an ExternalId, and we do not require long-lived access keys.

Looping in Diego (Private Deployments Eng) to confirm details + timeline.

Best,
Ava

---
From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Morgan Lee <morgan.lee@northstarbank.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, IT Security Review <security-review@northstarbank.com>
Date: Sun, Feb 9, 2025 at 11:22 AM
Subject: Re: Private deployment security review  audit log export to customer bucket

Hi Morgan,

Confirming: the exporter writes JSONL (optionally gzipped) into an S3 prefix you control. We can support SSE-KMS with a customer key ARN.

Auth: IRSA in the Private EKS cluster > AssumeRole into a customer-provided role ARN. We will require ExternalId (fail closed if missing).

Attaching:
- a short overview PDF
- an example bucket policy to scope `s3:PutObject` to a prefix

Were targeting a design-partner rollout this month; Ava can coordinate enablement once your Private cluster is ready.

Diego Fernandez
Staff Engineer, Private Deployments
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: redwood-private-audit-log-export-overview.pdf]
[Attachment: example-s3-bucket-policy.json]

---

source.body:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Team — requesting leadership approval today so we can lock Beta scope and start partner onboarding this week.

TL;DR proposal (Beta):
- Opt-in Quantization Profile Library with route-level enablement (Hosted + Dedicated). No default-on.
- 2–3 design partners: Acme AI + Northstar Search (confirmed interest), 1 enterprise candidate pending security review (FalconBank).
- Clear launch gates + rollback criteria; publish docs that emphasize “opt-in + customer-run evals.”

Key call-outs / decisions needed:
1) Scope: route-level toggle only (no tenant-wide defaults in Beta). Console + Config API.
2) Profile selection semantics: require explicit versioned profile ref (no “latest” alias) to avoid silent changes.
3) Launch gates: schema/validator, compatibility guardrails, determinism, eval thresholds, dashboards/alerts, and kill-switch.

Attachments:
- Quantization Profile Library — Beta Plan v0.9 (link).pdf
- Quant Profile Beta — Launch Gates Checklist (v1).xlsx

If we can get a “ship Beta under allowlist + gates below” approval by EOD, I’ll update the plan and move us into execution.

Marissa
Product Lead, Optimize


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

+1 on route-level only for Beta.

Edits I need reflected before approval:
- RBAC: Only route admins can change quantization settings. Console must show a confirmation step + “what will change” diff.
- Audit: Every enable/disable + profile ref update generates an audit event with actor, route, old/new ref, and resolved outcome.
- Compatibility guardrails: if incompatible, policy engine must deterministically fall back to baseline (and we must emit a “fallback” metric so we can alert).

On semantics: I strongly agree with no “latest”/auto-updating alias for Beta. If we want a “recommended” alias later, we’ll need explicit customer action + visible changelog.

Priya
Platform Sponsor


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:41 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Adding runtime gates I consider non-negotiable for Beta:

1) Determinism:
- For a given (model_id, model_variant, quant_profile_id) we must apply the same kernels/precision path across hosts, or fail closed to baseline.

2) Safety switches:
- Global kill-switch that forces baseline regardless of route config.
- Fast propagation. Target: <5 min worst-case across Hosted.

3) Observability:
- Per-request log annotation: quant_profile_id and resolution outcome (applied/fallback/disabled).
- Metrics: enabled_rate, fallback_rate, and p95 decode latency deltas.

If these are in the gates checklist (and tracked in the release checklist), I’m comfortable.

Mateo
Serving Runtime Sponsor


---

From: Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 11:26 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Aligned with the approach.

Applied ML edits for the plan:
- We will only publish profiles that pass the quant regression thresholds on Redwood prompt sets (task-family gates + statistical confidence). Any borderline deltas are “block publication” until recalibrated.
- Beta customers must be told (in docs + Console copy): “Enabling a profile can change outputs; run your own evals. Redwood provides guardrails, not guarantees.”
- Require an explicit rollback plan per partner (who flips the switch, what metrics, what timeframe). We can provide a playbook.

Arjun will own the partner-facing “what we need from you” checklist (prompt set, acceptance criteria, who to page).

Maya
Applied ML Sponsor


---

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 1:08 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

I reviewed the gates checklist attachment. Suggested tightening (so we don’t debate on the day-of):

Launch gates (must-pass):
- Config migration complete in staging + canary, rollback tested.
- Kill-switch tested end-to-end (flip -> policy/routing -> runtime baseline) + runbook updated.
- Dashboards live: enabled routes, fallback_rate, 5xx, p95 latency (prefill + decode if available), cost/token delta.
- Alerts:
  - fallback_rate > 1% sustained (15m) => page + auto-freeze rollout
  - p95 latency regression > 10% on enabled routes (30m) => page
  - quality guardrail signal (eval harness) => block further profile publication

Rollout proposal:
- Week 1: internal perf-canary + 0.1% on one design partner route
- Week 2: 1% -> 10% with explicit signoff

If we can align on these gates today, release engineering is good.

Connor
Release / Rollout Lead


---

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 2:57 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Ava — summarizing requested edits and asking for final approval.

Edits to incorporate into v1.0 Beta Plan:
1) Scope locked to route-level enablement only (no tenant defaults), Hosted + Dedicated.
2) Profile refs must be explicit and versioned (no “latest” alias in Beta).
3) Platform requirements:
   - RBAC limited to route admins; confirmation modal + visible diff
   - Audit events for all changes incl. actor + old/new + outcome
   - Compatibility checks + deterministic fallback to baseline
4) Runtime requirements:
   - Deterministic selection per (model, variant, profile)
   - Global kill-switch with <5 min propagation target
   - Request logs/metrics tag quant_profile_id + applied/fallback/disabled
5) Applied ML requirements:
   - Only publish profiles that pass regression thresholds
   - Explicit customer messaging: outputs can change; run your evals
   - Partner-by-partner rollback owner + criteria
6) Release gates / rollout:
   - Must-pass gates + dashboards/alerts + runbook (per Connor’s list)
   - 0.1% -> 1% -> 10% staged ramp with explicit signoff
7) Design partners:
   - Acme AI + Northstar Search as initial two; FalconBank optional if security review completes without expanding scope.

If you approve this direction, Marissa will issue Beta Plan v1.0 today and we’ll proceed with partner onboarding + execution.

Aditya


---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 4:48 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Approved with the edits captured below.

Please make sure the Beta Plan v1.0 is explicit on:
- allowlist-only enablement for Beta (no self-serve without review)
- we do not expand scope to tenant defaults or auto-recommendations during Beta
- “no alias” policy for profiles in Beta (explicit version pin)

Marissa: publish v1.0 and proceed.
Connor/Priya/Mateo/Maya: treat the launch gates as hard gates; no exceptions without bringing it back here.

Ava
Executive Sponsor
Redwood Inference

  - quality guardrail signal (eval harness) => block further profile publication

Rollout proposal:
- Week 1: internal perf-canary + 0.1% on one design partner route
- Week 2: 1% -> 10% with explicit signoff

If we can align on these gates today, release engineering is good.

Connor
Release / Rollout Lead


---

To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>

Edits to incorporate into v1.0 Beta Plan:

text:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Team — requesting leadership approval today so we can lock Beta scope and start partner onboarding this week.

TL;DR proposal (Beta):
- Opt-in Quantization Profile Library with route-level enablement (Hosted + Dedicated). No default-on.
- 2–3 design partners: Acme AI + Northstar Search (confirmed interest), 1 enterprise candidate pending security review (FalconBank).
- Clear launch gates + rollback criteria; publish docs that emphasize “opt-in + customer-run evals.”

Key call-outs / decisions needed:
1) Scope: route-level toggle only (no tenant-wide defaults in Beta). Console + Config API.
2) Profile selection semantics: require explicit versioned profile ref (no “latest” alias) to avoid silent changes.
3) Launch gates: schema/validator, compatibility guardrails, determinism, eval thresholds, dashboards/alerts, and kill-switch.

Attachments:
- Quantization Profile Library — Beta Plan v0.9 (link).pdf
- Quant Profile Beta — Launch Gates Checklist (v1).xlsx

If we can get a “ship Beta under allowlist + gates below” approval by EOD, I’ll update the plan and move us into execution.

Marissa
Product Lead, Optimize

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>

Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

+1 on route-level only for Beta.

Edits I need reflected before approval:
- RBAC: Only route admins can change quantization settings. Console must show a confirmation step + “what will change” diff.
- Audit: Every enable/disable + profile ref update generates an audit event with actor, route, old/new ref, and resolved outcome.
- Compatibility guardrails: if incompatible, policy engine must deterministically fall back to baseline (and we must emit a “fallback” metric so we can alert).

On semantics: I strongly agree with no “latest”/auto-updating alias for Beta. If we want a “recommended” alias later, we’ll need explicit customer action + visible changelog.

Priya
Platform Sponsor


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:41 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Adding runtime gates I consider non-negotiable for Beta:

1) Determinism:
- For a given (model_id, model_variant, quant_profile_id) we must apply the same kernels/precision path across hosts, or fail closed to baseline.

2) Safety switches:
- Global kill-switch that forces baseline regardless of route config.
- Fast propagation. Target: <5 min worst-case across Hosted.

3) Observability:
- Per-request log annotation: quant_profile_id and resolution outcome (applied/fallback/disabled).
- Metrics: enabled_rate, fallback_rate, and p95 decode latency deltas.

If these are in the gates checklist (and tracked in the release checklist), I’m comfortable.

Mateo
Serving Runtime Sponsor


---

From: Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 11:26 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Aligned with the approach.

Applied ML edits for the plan:
- We will only publish profiles that pass the quant regression thresholds on Redwood prompt sets (task-family gates + statistical confidence). Any borderline deltas are “block publication” until recalibrated.
- Beta customers must be told (in docs + Console copy): “Enabling a profile can change outputs; run your own evals. Redwood provides guardrails, not guarantees.”
- Require an explicit rollback plan per partner (who flips the switch, what metrics, what timeframe). We can provide a playbook.

Arjun will own the partner-facing “what we need from you” checklist (prompt set, acceptance criteria, who to page).

Maya
Applied ML Sponsor

- Config migration complete in staging + canary, rollback tested.

If we can align on these gates today, release engineering is good.

Connor

source.subject: BluePeak: confirm 'no egress' / disconnected environment assumptions
source.content:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nina Shah <nina.shah@bluepeakins.com>, Tom Wilkes <tom.wilkes@bluepeakins.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: BluePeak: confirm 'no egress' / disconnected environment assumptions

Hi Nina, Tom,

Following up on Fridays call  we want to be precise about what no egress means so we can scope the Private install correctly.

Our current assumptions (please confirm/correct):
- No outbound internet connectivity from the Kubernetes cluster (no NAT, no outbound via proxy).
- Internal-only DNS + internal NTP are OK.
- No public OCSP/CRL checks; certificate validation handled internally.
- All container images must be mirrored into your internal registry (we can provide digests).
- Upgrades delivered as an offline bundle during planned windows (no update checks).

Attached is a short checklist we use for disconnected installs.

Thanks,
Ava

--
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineering
Redwood Inference


On Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:06 AM PT Nina Shah <nina.shah@bluepeakins.com> wrote:
> Ava  yes, that matches. No outbound via proxy either. Internal DNS/NTP only.
> We also need to understand if any Redwood components attempt telemetry by default.

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nina Shah <nina.shah@bluepeakins.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 11:22 AM PT
Subject: Re: BluePeak: confirm 'no egress' / disconnected environment assumptions

Nina  good question. In Private deployments we can disable telemetry, and we do not require any external license checks. The main outbound dependencies to avoid are image pulls and any external cert checks, both handled via your internal endpoints / mirrors.

Well document the exact flags/values for this in the install guide.

-Ben

On Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 1:48 PM PT Tom Wilkes <tom.wilkes@bluepeakins.com> wrote:
> Confirmed. Also please assume outbound is blocked at the firewall, not just DNS.

title: BluePeak: confirm 'no egress' / disconnected environment assumptions

source.subject: Helioscope pilot: dedicated capacity sizing and SLO headroom
messages:
From: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sana Rao <sana@helioscope.ai>
Date: Fri, 15 Mar 2024 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the demo last week. We want to scope a small Dedicated deployment for a July pilot (NDA done). Quick background: our inference pattern is a mix of short chat completions and a reranking endpoint used by our search service. Typical request payloads are ~40–80 tokens; p95 tends to skew towards longer multi-turn flows.

Traffic profile (weekdays):
- Peak concurrent requests (observed): ~60 QPS on search rerank windows (bursty, 30s peaks).
- Average chat QPS outside peaks: 12–18.
- Average tokens per request: 55.
- 95th percentile tokens per request during peak windows: 140.

We care about p95 tail latency <= 300 ms for search rerank (single-turn) and < 700 ms for chat multi-turn flows. Can you help with a rough tokens/sec estimate and how many GPUs we'd need to reserve for Dedicated to meet SLOs with a reasonable headroom buffer? I attached a small CSV with sampled traffic (helioscope_traffic_samples.csv).

Appreciate a short runbook/plan we can show the engineering stakeholders.

Cheers,
Liam
Director of ML Ops
Helioscope AI

Attachments: helioscope_traffic_samples.csv (CSV, 28 KB)

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 15 Mar 2024 11:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Hi Liam —

Thanks — received the CSV and skimmed the burst patterns. Karthik (our deals/solutions lead) and Ben (ops) are looped to help with concrete headroom math. Quick ask: can you confirm the model family you expect to use in pilot (13B vs 70B equivalent) and whether quantized weights are acceptable? That drives per-GPU throughput.

I'll sketch a short, conservative runbook with three scenarios (chat-focused, search-heavy, mixed). Expect a first-pass estimate in the next 24 hours.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference

---
On Fri, Mar 15, 2024 at 9:12 AM Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai> wrote:
> Thanks for the demo last week. We want to scope a small Dedicated deployment for a July pilot (NDA done)...

Attachments: (referenced) helioscope_traffic_samples.csv

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
Date: Sat, 16 Mar 2024 08:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Team —

I pulled the sample CSV and ran quick throughput math. Assumptions:
- Pilot model: 13B-class quantized (we recommend this for cost/latency tradeoffs unless customer insists on a 70B).
- Measured baseline throughput (our conserved expectation): 600 tokens/sec per A100-80GB equivalent when targeting p95 latency budgets around 250–350 ms for single-turn completions. For multi-turn chat with 140-token requests, expect higher GPU time per request; we model on tokens/sec basis.
- Target SLOs: search rerank p95 <= 300 ms (single-turn), chat p95 <= 700 ms (multi-turn averages).

Calculations (mixed workload):
- Peak burst scenario: 60 concurrent requests in 30s window. Use conservative per-request tokens = 140 (p95). Tokens/sec during peak = 60 * 140 = 8,400 t/s.
- Baseline (non-peak) chat: 18 QPS * 55 tokens = 990 t/s.
- Combined peak + baseline (worst overlap): ~9,390 t/s.

GPU demands:
- At 600 t/s per GPU -> raw GPUs = 9,390 / 600 ≈ 15.65 -> 16 GPUs to cover peak sustained tokens/sec (no headroom).
- Add operational headroom: 25% buffer for short-term surges, cache misses, KV overhead -> 16 * 1.25 = 20 GPUs.
- Warm cache / prefix caching can drop effective GPU usage for chat flows with repeated context; assume 10–20% improvement if caching correctly configured, so effective GPUs might be 16–18 in practice.

SLA/fallback considerations:
- If the customer accepts model quantization and batch fusion, we can push per-GPU to ~750 t/s for short bursts, lowering the required GPUs to ~13 raw -> ~16 with headroom.
- For strict low-latency single-turn rerank, reserve a small pool (2–3 GPUs) pinned to low-latency routing to avoid queueing behind heavy chat batches.
- If they require 70B for quality, per-GPU drops to ~120–200 t/s (depending on optimizations) which would multiply GPU needs by ~3–5x; we should call that out as a non-starter for a small pilot.

Next steps for proposal:
1) Confirm model family and quantization tolerance (ask customer).
2) We produce an appendix with token-distribution curves, conservative headroom, and recommended Dedicated slab purchase: recommend a 20-GPU slab for July pilot with canary-scaled burst (add-on) of +4 GPUs for 90-day window.
3) Provide an ops-runbook: cache warm-up schedule, autoscaling throttle windows, and routing policy for rerank vs chat.

I'll attach a quick workbook (capacity_model_v2.xlsx) with the calculations and a short slide we can send to Helioscope.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Solutions Lead | Redwood Inference

Attachments: capacity_model_v2.xlsx

From: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sana Rao <sana@helioscope.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Mar 2024 09:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Thanks Karthik — this is helpful and in-line with our internal napkin math. A few clarifications:
- We are comfortable with quantized 13B for the pilot.
- We cannot exceed $X/month for Dedicated slab (I put the exact number in the HubSpot note).
- Search rerank must stay within 300 ms p95; if that requires routing to a hot pool that's fine.

Can you call out an options set where we prioritize cost vs latency? Specifically:
A) Min-cost option that still meets SLOs most of the time (best-effort tails)
B) Latency-priority option (strict tail guarantees)

If you can share the workbook and a single-slide comparative table we can show engineering and procurement next week that would be ideal.

Liam
Director of ML Ops, Helioscope AI

---
Attachments: (referenced) helioscope_traffic_samples.csv

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Mar 2024 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Liam / team —

Attaching the workbook and a 1-pager (helioscope_mvp_malpresentation.pdf) that lays out two configuration bundles per your ask: Cost-focused and Latency-focused. Summary (short):

Option A — Cost-focused- Reserved slab: 12 GPUs (13B quantized)- Routing: best-effort single pool, opportunistic prefix caching enabled- Expected p95 search latency: ~280–350 ms (tails may spike during sustained 30s bursts)- Monthly cost estimate: (redacted)

Option B — Latency-priority- Reserved slab: 20 GPUs (13B quantized) with a hotpool of 3 GPUs pinned for rerank routing- Routing: policy routes rerank -> hotpool; chat -> shared slab with aggressive caching- Expected p95 search latency: <= 300 ms with <10% chance of tail violations during extreme bursts- Monthly cost estimate: (redacted)

Operational notes:
- We recommend a 14-day warmup before the pilot to prime caches.
- Add a burst add-on (4 GPUs) available for the first 90 days; billing prorated.

I uploaded the finalized workbook to Drive and updated the HubSpot record with the two options. Link below.

Thanks — happy to schedule procurement sync.

Ben Carter
Solutions Ops | Redwood Inference

Attachments: capacity_model_v2.xlsx, helioscope_mvp_malpresentation.pdf

Related links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2bHelioSizingLink, https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/company/helioscope

title: Helioscope pilot: dedicated capacity sizing and SLO headroom
preview:
From: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sana Rao <sana@helioscope.ai>
Date: Fri, 15 Mar 2024 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the demo last week. We want to scope a small Dedicated deployment for a July pilot (NDA done). Quick background: our inference pattern is a mix of short chat completions and a reranking endpoint used by our search service. Typical request payloads are ~40–80 tokens; p95 tends to skew towards longer multi-turn flows.

Traffic profile (weekdays):
- Peak concurrent requests (observed): ~60 QPS on search rerank windows (bursty, 30s peaks).
- Average chat QPS outside peaks: 12–18.
- Average tokens per request: 55.
- 95th percentile tokens per request during peak windows: 140.

We care about p95 tail latency <= 300 ms for search rerank (single-turn) and < 700 ms for chat multi-turn flows. Can you help with a rough tokens/sec estimate and how many GPUs we'd need to reserve for Dedicated to meet SLOs with a reasonable headroom buffer? I attached a small CSV with sampled traffic (helioscope_traffic_samples.csv).

Appreciate a short runbook/plan we can show the engineering stakeholders.

Cheers,
Liam
Director of ML Ops
Helioscope AI

Attachments: helioscope_traffic_samples.csv (CSV, 28 KB)

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 15 Mar 2024 11:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Hi Liam —

Thanks — received the CSV and skimmed the burst patterns. Karthik (our deals/solutions lead) and Ben (ops) are looped to help with concrete headroom math. Quick ask: can you confirm the model family you expect to use in pilot (13B vs 70B equivalent) and whether quantized weights are acceptable? That drives per-GPU throughput.

I'll sketch a short, conservative runbook with three scenarios (chat-focused, search-heavy, mixed). Expect a first-pass estimate in the next 24 hours.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference

---
On Fri, Mar 15, 2024 at 9:12 AM Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai> wrote:
> Thanks for the demo last week. We want to scope a small Dedicated deployment for a July pilot (NDA done)...

Attachments: (referenced) helioscope_traffic_samples.csv

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
Date: Sat, 16 Mar 2024 08:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Team —

I pulled the sample CSV and ran quick throughput math. Assumptions:
- Pilot model: 13B-class quantized (we recommend this for cost/latency tradeoffs unless customer insists on a 70B).
- Measured baseline throughput (our conserved expectation): 600 tokens/sec per A100-80GB equivalent when targeting p95 latency budgets around 250–350 ms for single-turn completions. For multi-turn chat with 140-token requests, expect higher GPU time per request; we model on tokens/sec basis.
- Target SLOs: search rerank p95 <= 300 ms (single-turn), chat p95 <= 700 ms (multi-turn averages).

Calculations (mixed workload):
- Peak burst scenario: 60 concurrent requests in 30s window. Use conservative per-request tokens = 140 (p95). Tokens/sec during peak = 60 * 140 = 8,400 t/s.
- Baseline (non-peak) chat: 18 QPS * 55 tokens = 990 t/s.
- Combined peak + baseline (worst overlap): ~9,390 t/s.

GPU demands:
- At 600 t/s per GPU -> raw GPUs = 9,390 / 600 ≈ 15.65 -> 16 GPUs to cover peak sustained tokens/sec (no headroom).
- Add operational headroom: 25% buffer for short-term surges, cache misses, KV overhead -> 16 * 1.25 = 20 GPUs.
- Warm cache / prefix caching can drop effective GPU usage for chat flows with repeated context; assume 10–20% improvement if caching correctly configured, so effective GPUs might be 16–18 in practice.

SLA/fallback considerations:
- If the customer accepts model quantization and batch fusion, we can push per-GPU to ~750 t/s for short bursts, lowering the required GPUs to ~13 raw -> ~16 with headroom.
- For strict low-latency single-turn rerank, reserve a small pool (2–3 GPUs) pinned to low-latency routing to avoid queueing behind heavy chat batches.
- If they require 70B for quality, per-GPU drops to ~120–200 t/s (depending on optimizations) which would multiply GPU needs by ~3–5x; we should call that out as a non-starter for a small pilot.

Next steps for proposal:
1) Confirm model family and quantization tolerance (ask customer).
2) We produce an appendix with token-distribution curves, conservative headroom, and recommended Dedicated slab purchase: recommend a 20-GPU slab for July pilot with canary-scaled burst (add-on) of +4 GPUs for 90-day window.
3) Provide an ops-runbook: cache warm-up schedule, autoscaling throttle windows, and routing policy for rerank vs chat.

I'll attach a quick workbook (capacity_model_v2.xlsx) with the calculations and a short slide we can send to Helioscope.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Solutions Lead | Redwood Inference

Attachments: capacity_model_v2.xlsx

From: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sana Rao <sana@helioscope.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Mar 2024 09:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Thanks Karthik — this is helpful and in-line with our internal napkin math. A few clarifications:
- We are comfortable with quantized 13B for the pilot.
- We cannot exceed $X/month for Dedicated slab (I put the exact number in the HubSpot note).
- Search rerank must stay within 300 ms p95; if that requires routing to a hot pool that's fine.

Can you call out an options set where we prioritize cost vs latency? Specifically:
A) Min-cost option that still meets SLOs most of the time (best-effort tails)
B) Latency-priority option (strict tail guarantees)

If you can share the workbook and a single-slide comparative table we can show engineering and procurement next week that would be ideal.

Liam
Director of ML Ops, Helioscope AI

---
Attachments: (referenced) helioscope_traffic_samples.csv

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Mar 2024 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Liam / team —

Attaching the workbook and a 1-pager (helioscope_mvp_malpresentation.pdf) that lays out two configuration bundles per your ask: Cost-focused and Latency-focused. Summary (short):

Option A — Cost-focused- Reserved slab: 12 GPUs (13B quantized)- Routing: best-effort single pool, opportunistic prefix caching enabled- Expected p95 search latency: ~280–350 ms (tails may spike during sustained 30s bursts)- Monthly cost estimate: (redacted)

Option B — Latency-priority- Reserved slab: 20 GPUs (13B quantized) with a hotpool of 3 GPUs pinned for rerank routing- Routing: policy routes rerank -> hotpool; chat -> shared slab with aggressive caching- Expected p95 search latency: <= 300 ms with <10% chance of tail violations during extreme bursts- Monthly cost estimate: (redacted)

Operational notes:
- We recommend a 14-day warmup before the pilot to prime caches.
- Add a burst add-on (4 GPUs) available for the first 90 days; billing prorated.

I uploaded the finalized workbook to Drive and updated the HubSpot record with the two options. Link below.

Thanks — happy to schedule procurement sync.

Ben Carter
Solutions Ops | Redwood Inference

Attachments: capacity_model_v2.xlsx, helioscope_mvp_malpresentation.pdf

Related links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2bHelioSizingLink, https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/company/helioscope
